era.zh: 1280年，蒙古帝国时代
era.en: 1280 CE, Age of the Mongol Empire

summary.zh: 1280年时，蒙古诸汗国与元朝共同主导欧亚大陆政治格局；东亚由元朝统治中国，日本处于镰仓幕府时期，朝鲜为高丽；西亚由伊儿汗国、马穆鲁克苏丹国与安纳托利亚诸突厥政权并立；南亚有德里苏丹国、雅达瓦、卡卡提亚、潘地亚与曷萨拉等王朝；东南亚包括蒲甘、吴哥、素可泰、陀罗钵地后继泰族与爪哇信诃沙里；欧洲则在神圣罗马帝国、法兰西、英格兰、卡斯蒂利亚、阿拉贡、葡萄牙、匈牙利、拜占庭及金帐汗国影响下分裂并存；非洲有马里、马林、哈夫斯、埃塞俄比亚与基尔瓦等政权；美洲则由阿兹特克前诸纳瓦城邦、米斯特克、普雷佩查前身政体、奇穆、印加前库斯科王国等众多区域性政体组成。
summary.en: In 1280 CE, the Mongol imperial world and its successor khanates dominated the political order of Eurasia; East Asia was ruled largely by the Yuan dynasty in China, while Japan was under the Kamakura shogunate and Korea under Goryeo. West and Central Asia were divided among the Ilkhanate, the Mamluk Sultanate, and Turkic powers in Anatolia. South Asia featured the Delhi Sultanate alongside the Yadavas, Kakatiyas, Pandyas, and Hoysalas. Southeast Asia included Pagan, Angkor, Sukhothai, Tai successor states, and Singhasari Java. Europe remained politically fragmented among the Holy Roman Empire, France, England, Castile, Aragon, Portugal, Hungary, Byzantium, and the Golden Horde's sphere. Africa included Mali, Marinid Morocco, the Hafsids, Ethiopia, and Kilwa. In the Americas, the map was filled by regional powers such as Nahua city-states before the Aztec Empire, Mixtec polities, proto-Purépecha formations, Chimor, and the early Kingdom of Cusco before Inca expansion.

description.zh: 统治东欧草原与伏尔加流域的蒙古汗国，并支配罗斯诸公国。
description.en: The Mongol khanate ruling the Pontic steppe and Volga basin while dominating the Rus' principalities.

dynasty.zh: 成吉思汗家族察合台系
dynasty.en: Chagataid branch of the House of Genghis

governmentForm.zh: 蒙古汗国，结合草原可汗统治、成吉思汗后裔宗王权威与河中地区定居行政传统的分层统治体制
governmentForm.en: A Mongol khanate combining steppe khanal rule, Chinggisid dynastic legitimacy, and layered administration drawing on settled traditions in Transoxiana.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由蒙古贵族与军户、突厥化草原部众、河中与绿洲城市居民、伊斯兰学者与商人、农民、工匠及被征服部族构成，游牧与定居社会并存且相互依赖
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Mongol nobles and military households, Turkic steppe tribes, settled urban populations of Transoxiana and the oases, Muslim scholars and merchants, peasants, artisans, and subject tribes, with nomadic and sedentary sectors coexisting in mutual dependence.

rulingClass.zh: 成吉思汗黄金家族的察合台后裔、蒙古那颜、主要军政贵族以及受汗廷依赖的突厥与河中地方精英
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was formed by the Chagataid descendants of the Golden Lineage, Mongol noyans, leading military-administrative nobles, and Turkic and Transoxianan local elites tied to the khan’s court.

succession.zh: 原则上由成吉思汗家族察合台支系成员继承，通常经宗王、贵族与军中首领议定并以忽里勒台或宫廷共识确认，实际常受实力派贵族影响
succession.en: Succession in principle belonged to the Chagataid branch of the House of Chinggis, usually determined through agreement among princes, nobles, and military leaders and confirmed by quriltai or court consensus, though in practice heavily shaped by powerful grandees.

structure.zh: 中央政权以可汗及其营帐朝廷为核心，依靠蒙古军政贵族管理草原部众，同时在河中、七河与绿洲地带保留达鲁花赤、总督、税吏和地方城市官员。治理模式兼具游牧军事统治与伊斯兰—波斯化文书行政。
structure.en: The central state was centered on the khan and his ordo court, relying on Mongol military nobles to manage steppe tribes while retaining darughachi, governors, tax officials, and city administrators in Transoxiana, Semirechye, and the oasis zones. Its governance blended nomadic military rule with Islamicate-Persian bureaucratic practice.

function.zh: 负责可汗近侍、诏令传达、宫廷秩序、使节接待与核心决策执行
function.en: Handled the khan’s household guard, transmission of decrees, court order, reception of envoys, and execution of core decisions.

name.en: Military Affairs and Tumen Command Office

function.zh: 统辖诸军征调、部众编制、军械、牧地调配与边境防务
function.en: Oversaw troop levies, tribal-military organization, weapons, pasture allocation, and frontier defense.

function.zh: 征收牧群贡赋、土地税、城市商税、关税与驿站相关徭役折纳
function.en: Collected pastoral tribute, land tax, urban commercial taxes, customs duties, and commuted obligations linked to relay services.

function.zh: 维护站赤网络、道路安全、军使与商旅通行以及补给转运
function.en: Maintained the yam relay network, road security, military and diplomatic communications, and logistical transport.

function.zh: 处理波斯文与突厥文文书、地方争讼、敕令登记及与伊斯兰法官的协调
function.en: Managed Persian and Turkic documentation, local litigation, registration of decrees, and coordination with Islamic judges.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理高度多元。草原地区主要经部族首领、蒙古那颜和军户体系管辖；河中与绿洲城市则由达鲁花赤、地方总督、税务承包者、城镇长官与伊斯兰法官共同管理。撒马尔罕、布哈拉等定居区比东部草原拥有更成熟的文书行政。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was highly plural. Steppe areas were governed mainly through tribal chiefs, Mongol noyans, and military households; Transoxiana and the oasis cities were jointly managed by darughachi, provincial governors, tax farmers, town officials, and Islamic judges. Settled zones such as Samarkand and Bukhara had more developed documentary administration than the eastern steppe.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系并行不悖：上层政治与军队事务遵循蒙古法令与成吉思汗传统法；城市与穆斯林居民的婚姻、继承、商事及部分刑民争端则多按伊斯兰教法和地方习惯裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was dual in practice: high politics and military matters followed Mongol ordinances and Chinggisid custom, while marriage, inheritance, commerce, and many civil disputes among urban and Muslim populations were often judged under Islamic law and local custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收包括游牧部众的牲畜贡赋与军役义务、农耕地区地税、城市手工业与市场税、道路与关口商税，以及对绿洲农业和长途贸易的定额征收。税负因地区、族群与统治者赐免而差异较大。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation included livestock tribute and military obligations from pastoral groups, land tax in farming districts, urban craft and market dues, customs at roads and gates, and assessed levies on oasis agriculture and long-distance trade. Burdens varied widely by region, community, and exemptions granted by the rulers.

religion.zh: 宗教环境多元。蒙古统治者保留腾格里信仰与祖先崇拜传统，汗国内广大河中与绿洲居民以伊斯兰教逊尼派为主，同时并存佛教、景教和少量其他信仰。1280年前后的察合台汗国尚未全面伊斯兰化，但伊斯兰影响持续增强。
religion.en: The religious environment was plural. Mongol rulers retained Tengrist and ancestral traditions, while most inhabitants of Transoxiana and the oasis zones were Sunni Muslim; Buddhism, East Syriac Christianity, and a few other faiths also persisted. Around 1280 the Chagatai Khanate was not yet fully Islamized, but Islamic influence was steadily growing.

philosophy.zh: 思想基础结合蒙古草原政治伦理、成吉思汗家族正统观念、突厥部族传统以及伊斯兰法学与波斯化宫廷文化。实用治理重于系统哲学，但宗教法学和君主合法性论述影响深远。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual foundations combined Mongol steppe political ethics, Chinggisid legitimacy, Turkic tribal tradition, and Islamic jurisprudence with Persianate court culture. Practical governance mattered more than abstract philosophy, but legal scholarship and ideas of dynastic legitimacy were influential.

writingSystem.zh: 官方与商业文书多用回鹘蒙古文、波斯文和突厥文，阿拉伯字母在伊斯兰学术与城市社会中重要。多语并用是汗国治理与贸易的典型特征。
writingSystem.en: Official and commercial writing used Uyghur-Mongolian script, Persian, and Turkic languages, while the Arabic script was important in Islamic scholarship and urban society. Multilingual literacy was a hallmark of both governance and trade.

culturalAchievements.zh: 察合台汗国的重要文化成就在于维系中亚草原与绿洲世界的交流网络，促进丝绸之路商贸、文书文化和跨区域人员流动。其宫廷与城市社会延续了河中地区的波斯化都市传统，同时将蒙古军事贵族文化嵌入中亚政治格局。
culturalAchievements.en: The khanate’s major cultural achievement was sustaining the connective networks between the Central Asian steppe and oasis world, supporting Silk Road exchange, documentary culture, and transregional mobility. Its court and cities preserved the Persianate urban traditions of Transoxiana while embedding Mongol military aristocratic culture into the political order of Central Asia.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属于蒙古语族与突厥语族，行政和高文化领域广泛使用伊朗语支的波斯语
languageFamily.en: The principal languages belonged to the Mongolic and Turkic families, while Persian of the Iranian branch was widely used in administration and high culture.

gdpDescription.zh: 汗国经济以草原畜牧、河中灌溉农业、城市手工业和丝绸之路中转贸易为基础。与高度都市化的伊利汗国部分地区或南宋旧地相比，其人均产出较为中等，但凭借横贯中亚的交通位置，贸易抽税与马匹、牲畜、纺织品交换具有显著优势。
gdpDescription.en: The khanate’s economy rested on steppe pastoralism, irrigated agriculture in Transoxiana, urban crafts, and transit trade along the Silk Road. Compared with more heavily urbanized parts of the Ilkhanate or former Southern Song lands, per capita output was moderate, but its central position across Inner Asia gave it major advantages in customs revenue and the exchange of horses, livestock, and textiles.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、马匹繁育、灌溉农业、棉纺与毛织、皮革加工、金属手工业、商旅转运、驿站服务、城市市场贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, horse breeding, irrigated agriculture, cotton spinning and wool weaving, leatherworking, metal crafts, caravan transit, relay services, and urban market trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、羊毛、皮革、牲畜、棉布、毛织物、金属器、粮食、干果、玉石、丝绸转口货、香料转运货、奴隶及奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Horses, wool, leather, livestock, cotton cloth, woolens, metalwares, grain, dried fruits, jade, re-exported silks, transit spices, slaves, and luxury goods.

name.zh: 银币与金属称量并行货币

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。游牧贵族以大规模马群、羊群、牛群、奴仆与战利品为财富核心；城市商人和税务承包者以货币、仓储与贸易网络积累财富；普通农户和牧户则多仅维持基本生计并留有少量牲畜或谷物余裕。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Nomadic nobles measured wealth in vast herds, retainers, and war booty; urban merchants and tax farmers accumulated money, storage, and trading networks; ordinary peasants and herders usually maintained only subsistence with modest reserves in animals or grain.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为游牧贡赋、城市税收、商队贸易与地方农业租税相结合的混合型体系。国家干预主要表现为征税、交通保护、驿站供给和对商人的特许，而不是统一而细密的直接管理。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a mixed one combining nomadic tribute, urban taxation, caravan trade, and local agrarian dues. State intervention was expressed chiefly through taxation, protection of communications, relay provisioning, and privileges for merchants rather than through uniform, detailed direct management.

source.en: Land tax and oasis agrarian dues
Customs, transit duties, and market taxes
Tribute from subordinate territories and dependents

source.zh: 罚没、特许费与杂项收入

category.en: Army and support of princes and nobles
Frontier garrisons and emergency military costs

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以贵金属、铸币、牲畜、布匹、战利品和地方仓储实物共同构成，并非完全以现银保存。汗廷可依赖机动的营帐国库与若干城市库藏，但财政稳健性仍受战争、部族内讧和贸易线路安全影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metals, coin, livestock, textiles, booty, and in-kind stores rather than cash alone. The khan could draw on a mobile ordo treasury and several urban treasuries, but fiscal strength remained sensitive to warfare, tribal conflict, and the security of trade routes.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持军政忠诚、保障驿路与贸易、并通过税赋和贡纳支撑汗廷开支。相较纯粹官僚国家，察合台汗国更依赖协商式征收、特权豁免与对地方精英的利益分配。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining military-political loyalty, securing the relay roads and trade, and financing the khan’s court through taxes and tribute. Compared with more bureaucratic states, the Chagatai Khanate relied more on negotiated exactions, privileged exemptions, and distribution of revenue to local elites.

description.zh: 以轻重骑结合为主，依靠复合弓、长枪、刀剑和高度机动性，是汗国的核心作战力量。
description.en: A mix of light and heavier cavalry armed with composite bows, lances, and sabers, forming the khanate’s core combat arm through speed and maneuver.

description.zh: 部署于河中城市、绿洲据点和税关，负责治安、征税保护和城防。
description.en: Stationed in Transoxianan cities, oasis strongpoints, and tax posts, responsible for security, revenue protection, and urban defense.

description.zh: 由附属部族和地方势力按需征发，战斗力不一，但在大规模远征和边境冲突中十分重要。
description.en: Raised as needed from subordinate tribes and local powers; uneven in quality but important in major campaigns and frontier warfare.

name.en: Khanal Guard and Elite Household Troops

description.zh: 负责拱卫可汗、执行关键突击和维持宫廷威望，多由忠诚家臣和精选骑士组成。
description.en: Protected the khan, carried out decisive strikes, and upheld court prestige, composed of loyal retainers and selected elite horsemen.

commanderInChief.zh: 都哇可汗

technology.zh: 军事实力建立在成熟的草原骑射传统、复合弓、优良马群、迅速集结和站赤通信体系之上。攻城能力存在，但较依赖从河中、突厥和波斯工匠中吸收技术，整体攻坚水平逊于元朝或伊利汗国的重型攻城体系。
technology.en: Military strength rested on advanced steppe horse-archery, composite bows, high-quality remounts, rapid mobilization, and the relay communications network. Siege capability existed, but it depended more on borrowed expertise from Transoxianan, Turkic, and Persian craftsmen and was generally less developed than the heavy siege systems of the Yuan or Ilkhanate.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与窝阔台系残余势力及中亚地方竞争者的权力斗争、与金帐汗国和伊利汗国之间的边境与草场摩擦，以及河中城市在税负和军政控制下可能出现的叛乱。
threats.en: Major threats came from struggles with surviving Ögedeid interests and regional rivals in Central Asia, frontier and pasture frictions with the Kipchak Khanate and Ilkhanate, and possible unrest in Transoxianan cities under heavy taxation and military oversight.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约五百万，分布极不均衡。大多数定居人口集中于河中、费尔干纳和主要绿洲，草原与山地则由较稀疏的游牧部众和牧民占据。人口结构兼具蒙古、突厥、伊朗语居民及其他中亚族群成分。
populationDescription.en: The population was about five million and very unevenly distributed. Most settled inhabitants lived in Transoxiana, the Fergana basin, and major oasis zones, while the steppe and uplands were occupied by more thinly spread nomadic groups and pastoralists. The population combined Mongol, Turkic, Iranian, and other Central Asian elements.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致分为成吉思汗家族宗王、蒙古与突厥军政贵族、宗教人士与学者、商人与税务经营者、城市工匠、农民、牧民以及奴仆和战俘。地位高低往往由血统、军功、对汗廷的亲近程度和掌控税源或牧地的能力决定。
socialClasses.en: Social classes were broadly divided into Chinggisid princes, Mongol and Turkic military-administrative nobles, religious figures and scholars, merchants and tax entrepreneurs, urban artisans, peasants, pastoralists, and slaves or captives. Status was shaped by lineage, military service, proximity to the khan’s court, and control of revenue sources or pasture.

allies.zh: 1280年前后无稳定意义上的长期正式盟邦，但都哇在蒙古诸汗国内部经营与反忽必烈势力及部分草原集团的合作空间，并与中亚若干绿洲政权维持附属或交易性关系。
allies.en: Around 1280 the khanate had no long-term formal allies in the modern sense, but Duwa cultivated cooperation with anti-Khubilai elements and certain steppe groups within the wider Mongol world, while maintaining subordinate or transactional ties with several Central Asian oasis polities.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象包括与其争夺蒙古世界主导权的大元朝，以及因边境、属地和贸易路线问题产生冲突的伊利汗国；与金帐汗国也存在潜在竞争和边疆摩擦。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies included the Great Yuan, with which it contested influence in the Mongol world, and the Ilkhanate, with which it clashed over frontiers, dependencies, and trade routes; there was also potential rivalry and friction with the Kipchak Khanate.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护察合台系自主地位、控制中亚交通与草场、利用丝路商贸获利，并在蒙古诸汗国竞争中寻求战略平衡为主。外交常与婚姻、部族结盟、军事威慑和贸易特许交织进行。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving Chagataid autonomy, controlling Central Asian routes and pastures, profiting from Silk Road commerce, and seeking strategic balance amid competition among the Mongol khanates. Diplomacy was intertwined with marriage ties, tribal alliances, military pressure, and commercial privileges.

era.zh: 蒙古帝国时代的中亚草原—绿洲复合技术阶段
era.en: Central Asian steppe-oasis composite technological stage of the Mongol Empire era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键优势不在原创发明而在跨区域整合：成熟的复合弓骑射体系、站赤驿传网络、多语文书行政、跨欧亚商旅护送机制，以及对灌溉农业、铸币和城市手工业传统的吸收与维持。
keyInnovations.en: Its key strengths lay less in original invention than in transregional integration: the mature composite-bow cavalry system, the yam relay network, multilingual documentary administration, caravan protection across Eurasia, and the absorption and maintenance of irrigated agriculture, minting, and urban craft traditions.

strengths.zh: 察合台汗国的最大优势在于其地理位置与机动军事能力。它控制连接中国、草原、伊朗和印度方向的中亚通道，能从商税、转运与草原动员中获益；同时成吉思汗家族的合法性仍赋予政权强大号召力。
strengths.en: The khanate’s greatest strengths were its geography and mobile military power. It controlled Central Asian corridors linking China, the steppe, Iran, and India, allowing it to profit from customs, transit, and steppe mobilization; Chinggisid legitimacy also continued to give the regime strong authority.

weaknesses.zh: 其主要弱点在于内部结构松散、游牧贵族与定居税源地区之间张力长期存在、继承与宗王政治不稳、地方行政不够统一，且过度依赖贸易路线安全与草原精英忠诚。
weaknesses.en: Its main weaknesses lay in a loose internal structure, persistent tension between nomadic nobles and settled revenue-producing regions, unstable succession and princely politics, uneven local administration, and heavy dependence on secure trade routes and the loyalty of steppe elites.

outlook.zh: 1280年的察合台汗国总体稳定，且在都哇统治下具有恢复与扩张潜力。若能整合河中财赋与草原军事资源，并在与元朝、伊利汗国的竞争中保持平衡，汗国可在未来数十年继续成为中亚关键强权；但若宗王纷争加剧或贸易动脉受阻，其稳定将迅速削弱。
outlook.en: In 1280 the Chagatai Khanate was broadly stable and under Duwa possessed real potential for recovery and expansion. If it could integrate the revenues of Transoxiana with steppe military resources and maintain balance against the Yuan and Ilkhanate, it could remain a major Central Asian power for decades; but intensified princely conflict or disruption of the trade arteries would quickly weaken that stability.

description.zh: 中亚蒙古汗国，连接草原、河中与丝绸之路要道。
description.en: A Mongol khanate of Central Asia linking the steppe, Transoxiana, and key Silk Road routes.

governmentForm.zh: 以军事奴隶精英为核心的伊斯兰苏丹制君主国，依靠军政家臣网络、宗教法合法性与行省总督统治埃及与叙利亚
governmentForm.en: An Islamic sultanate monarchy centered on a military slave elite, ruling Egypt and Syria through military households, religious legitimacy, and provincial governors

socialStructure.zh: 社会大体分为马穆鲁克军事统治阶层、乌里玛与法官等宗教学者、城市商人与手工业者、乡村农民、贝都因与部族群体，以及犹太人与基督徒等受保护民。统治层在族源上多为突厥、钦察、切尔克斯及高加索奴隶兵出身，与主体阿拉伯语社会相区隔。
socialStructure.en: Society was broadly divided into the ruling Mamluk military elite, the ulama and judges, urban merchants and artisans, rural peasants, Bedouin and tribal groups, and protected Jewish and Christian communities. The ruling stratum was largely of Turkic, Kipchak, Circassian, and Caucasian military-slave origin, socially distinct from the predominantly Arabic-speaking population.

rulingClass.zh: 马穆鲁克埃米尔集团及其家臣军团，辅以高阶乌里玛、首席法官、大商人和宫廷文官
rulingClass.en: The Mamluk amir class and their military households, supported by senior ulama, chief judges, major merchants, and court secretaries

succession.zh: 名义上为苏丹家族世袭，实际上由马穆鲁克军政精英通过拥立、政变、协商与武力决定，1280年盖拉温已稳固其篡位后统治
succession.en: Nominally hereditary within the sultan's household, but in practice determined by the Mamluk military-political elite through acclamation, coup, negotiation, and force; by 1280 Qalawun had consolidated rule after his seizure of power

structure.zh: 中央政府以苏丹、诸大埃米尔、御前军务与财政机关组成。开罗为帝国中枢，叙利亚由大马士革、阿勒颇、哈马、萨法德等行省治理。政务由御书记局、军务机构、财政部门、宗教司法体系和市场监督体系共同运作。
structure.en: The central government was composed of the sultan, leading amirs, and court military and fiscal institutions. Cairo served as the imperial center, while Syria was administered through provinces such as Damascus, Aleppo, Hama, and Safad. Governance functioned through the chancellery, military offices, fiscal bureaus, the religious-judicial hierarchy, and market supervision.

function.zh: 起草敕令、外交文书、任命文书与官方通信，维系苏丹政令与对外往来
function.en: Drafted decrees, diplomatic correspondence, appointments, and official communications, maintaining the sultan's authority and foreign contacts

function.zh: 管理马穆鲁克名册、军饷、军马、军械、调兵与检阅
function.en: Managed Mamluk rolls, military pay, horses, arsenals, mobilization, and inspections

function.zh: 征收土地税、商业税、港口税与特许收入，核算岁入并监督国库
function.en: Collected land taxes, commercial dues, port taxes, and monopoly revenues, accounting for annual income and supervising the treasury

function.zh: 登记、分配并复核军功采邑，以支持军队供养与地方控制
function.en: Registered, assigned, and reviewed military iqta holdings to sustain the army and local control

function.zh: 协调四大法学派法官、宗教基金、学校与清真寺事务，维护逊尼派合法性
function.en: Coordinated the judges of the four Sunni legal schools, religious endowments, schools, and mosque affairs, sustaining Sunni legitimacy

function.zh: 监管城市物价、度量衡、公共秩序、手工业行会与食品质量
function.en: Supervised urban prices, weights and measures, public order, guild activity, and food quality

localAdmin.zh: 地方由总督、城堡司令、税务官与法官共同治理。埃及核心地区依赖尼罗河税赋与村社体系；叙利亚诸城则由行省埃米尔与驻军管理，沿海与边境地带具有较强军事色彩。
localAdmin.en: The provinces were administered by governors, fortress commanders, tax officials, and judges. Core Egypt relied on Nile-based taxation and village structures, while Syrian cities were managed by provincial amirs and garrisons, with coastal and frontier zones strongly militarized.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以逊尼派沙里亚为核心，同时并行苏丹敕令、军法惯例与行政裁量。四大法学派在开罗和主要城市均设首席法官，商业、婚姻、继承与宗教事务多由伊斯兰法处理，军政与治安问题则常由苏丹与埃米尔直接裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system centered on Sunni sharia, alongside sultanic decrees, military custom, and administrative discretion. Chief judges from the four Sunni schools were established in Cairo and major cities; commerce, marriage, inheritance, and religious matters were mostly handled under Islamic law, while military and security issues were often decided directly by the sultan and amirs.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家财政主要来自农业地税、灌溉区赋税、城市市场税、港口关税、商队通行费、宗教基金相关收入以及部分贡赋。军队供养大量通过伊克塔采邑实现，减少了中央现银支出但也强化了军功贵族的地方影响。
taxationSystem.en: State finance relied mainly on agricultural land tax, revenues from irrigated zones, urban market taxes, port customs, caravan tolls, income linked to religious endowments, and some tribute. Much military maintenance was financed through iqta assignments, reducing central cash expenditure but strengthening the local influence of military elites.

religion.zh: 国教为逊尼派伊斯兰教，强调反十字军与抗蒙古的护教者形象。沙斐仪、哈乃斐、马立克、罕百里四大法学派并存，苏菲教团亦活跃。科普特基督徒、亚美尼亚人、犹太人和其他社群在受保护民制度下存在。
religion.en: Sunni Islam was the state religion, and the regime emphasized its role as defender of the faith against Crusaders and Mongols. The Shafi'i, Hanafi, Maliki, and Hanbali legal schools coexisted, and Sufi orders were active. Coptic Christians, Armenians, Jews, and other communities lived under protected dhimmi status.

philosophy.zh: 政治思想强调苏丹作为伊斯兰共同体保护者的职责，法学与经学占主导地位，历史写作、行政文书传统与苏菲伦理并重。相较哲学思辨，制度更重视法学、圣训学、阿拉伯文辞学与实用治国术。
philosophy.en: Political thought stressed the sultan's duty as protector of the Islamic community, while jurisprudence and religious scholarship predominated. Historical writing, bureaucratic literary culture, and Sufi ethics were all important. Compared with speculative philosophy, the system valued law, hadith scholarship, Arabic belles-lettres, and practical statecraft more highly.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉伯字母书写阿拉伯语，行政与学术文书高度成熟；宫廷军事圈也保留部分突厥语与钦察语口语传统。
writingSystem.en: The Arabic script was used primarily for Arabic, with highly developed administrative and scholarly documentation; Turkic and Kipchak oral usage also persisted within court and military circles.

culturalAchievements.zh: 1280年前后的马穆鲁克世界在宗教学校、清真寺、经学院、医院和陵寝建筑方面成就显著。开罗与大马士革是伊斯兰学术与手工业中心，玻璃、金属镶嵌、木工、纺织与书法艺术繁荣。政权通过纪念性建筑和宗教赞助塑造合法性。
culturalAchievements.en: The Mamluk world around 1280 was distinguished by major achievements in madrasas, mosques, hospitals, and funerary architecture. Cairo and Damascus were centers of Islamic scholarship and skilled crafts, with flourishing glasswork, inlaid metalwork, woodworking, textiles, and calligraphy. The regime used monumental construction and religious patronage to build legitimacy.

languageFamily.zh: 主导语言属亚非语系闪米特语族阿拉伯语；统治军事精英内部另有突厥语支使用
languageFamily.en: The dominant language belonged to the Semitic branch of the Afroasiatic family, namely Arabic; Turkic languages were also used within the ruling military elite

unit.zh: 马穆鲁克银迪拉姆年值
马穆鲁克银迪拉姆每人每年
unit.en: Mamluk silver dirhams per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 马穆鲁克苏丹国在1280年拥有东地中海最强的综合经济之一。埃及尼罗河农业高产，叙利亚城市网络发达，红海与地中海转口贸易获利丰厚。其经济规模虽低于同时期中国，但在伊斯兰西部与东地中海世界处于领先地位。
gdpDescription.en: In 1280 the Mamluk Sultanate possessed one of the strongest composite economies in the eastern Mediterranean. Egypt's Nile agriculture was highly productive, Syrian urban networks were dense, and profits from Red Sea and Mediterranean transit trade were substantial. Its economic scale was below that of contemporary China but among the leading economies of the Islamic west and eastern Mediterranean.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、粮食与糖生产、亚麻与棉纺织、金属工艺、造船修船、玻璃制造、香料转口、骆驼商队贸易、造纸与宗教教育相关服务
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, grain and sugar production, linen and cotton textiles, metalworking, shipbuilding and repair, glassmaking, spice transshipment, camel-caravan trade, papermaking, and services linked to religious education

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、糖、亚麻布、棉布、纸张、玻璃器、金属器、香料、乳香、药材、奴隶、马匹、木材、印度洋奢侈品与非洲黄金
tradeGoods.en: Grain, sugar, linen, cotton cloth, paper, glassware, metal goods, spices, frankincense, medicinal substances, slaves, horses, timber, Indian Ocean luxuries, and African gold

householdWealth.zh: 城市富商、税农与高阶埃米尔拥有显著财富，常以地产、宗教基金、仓储、商队投资和金银器形式保存。普通农户财富较有限，多集中于牲畜、农具、粮食储备和家庭纺织品。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchant magnates, tax farmers, and senior amirs held substantial wealth, often stored in landed property, endowments, warehouses, caravan investment, and precious metal goods. Ordinary peasant households possessed far more limited assets, concentrated in livestock, tools, grain stocks, and domestic textiles.

unit.zh: 马穆鲁克银迪拉姆每名劳动人口每年
unit.en: Mamluk silver dirhams per working person per year

unit.zh: 马穆鲁克银迪拉姆年贸易额
unit.en: Mamluk silver dirhams annual trade volume

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的农业税收与军功采邑体系之上，叠加高度商业化的城市与跨区域贸易网络。政府对港口、关税、部分战略商品和运输节点施加强力监管，但市场交换、私人资本与宗教基金在日常经济中同样重要。
economicSystem.en: The economy combined a state-centered agrarian tax base and military iqta system with highly commercialized urban and interregional trade networks. The government exercised strong control over ports, customs, some strategic goods, and transport nodes, but market exchange, private capital, and religious endowments were also central to everyday economic life.

source.zh: 农业地税与尼罗河灌溉区收入
source.en: Agrarian land tax and Nile irrigation revenues

source.zh: 港口关税与海陆贸易税
source.en: Port customs and maritime-land trade duties

source.zh: 城市市场税、作坊税与消费征收
source.en: Urban market taxes, workshop levies, and consumption exactions

source.zh: 附庸贡赋、罚没与特别征敛

source.en: Minting, crown domains, and miscellaneous receipts

category.zh: 行省驻军、防御工事与边境堡垒
category.en: Provincial garrisons, fortifications, and frontier strongholds

category.zh: 宫廷、官僚与司法宗教机构
水利、城市维护与公共工程
category.en: Irrigation, urban maintenance, and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况总体稳健，受益于近年政权整合、贸易税收和农业基础。然财政很大程度依赖持续的尼罗河丰收、贸易畅通和对军功贵族的有效控制，一旦战事扩大或内斗加剧，现金流会迅速承压。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was broadly sound, benefiting from recent political consolidation, trade revenues, and the agrarian base. Yet finances depended heavily on continued Nile prosperity, open trade routes, and effective control over military elites; if war widened or factional conflict intensified, cash flow would come under rapid strain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守与实用，优先保障军费、边防和首都供给，同时维持水利、港口与市场监管。政权常通过重新分配伊克塔、临时征敛和罚没财产弥补短期缺口。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and pragmatic, prioritizing military costs, frontier defense, and provisioning of the capital, while maintaining irrigation, ports, and market supervision. The regime often covered short-term gaps through iqta redistribution, extraordinary levies, and confiscation of property.

description.zh: 帝国核心突击力量，由苏丹直属马穆鲁克与高级埃米尔家臣组成，装备精良，以重骑兵弓马战和近战冲击见长。
description.en: The empire's core striking force, composed of the sultan's own Mamluks and the retainers of major amirs, heavily equipped and skilled in mounted archery and close-combat shock action.

description.zh: 由叙利亚与埃及各地埃米尔供给的骑兵，战时集结，承担边境巡防、快速反应和会战支援。
description.en: Cavalry supplied by amirs across Syria and Egypt, mobilized in wartime for frontier patrol, rapid response, and battlefield support.

description.zh: 包括弓箭手、长矛兵、堡垒驻军和城市卫队，负责城防、攻城与后方治安。
description.en: Included archers, spearmen, fortress troops, and urban guards, responsible for defense, sieges, and internal security.

description.zh: 承担沙漠侦察、交通线掩护、追击和边地向导任务，机动性强但纪律性较弱。
description.en: Provided desert reconnaissance, line-of-communication protection, pursuit, and frontier guidance, with high mobility but looser discipline.

description.zh: 规模较陆军小，主要用于尼罗河运输、沿岸巡逻、港口防务和必要的东地中海行动。
description.en: Smaller than the army, used mainly for Nile transport, coastal patrol, port defense, and limited eastern Mediterranean operations.

commanderInChief.zh: 苏丹曼苏尔·盖拉温

technology.zh: 军队以复合弓骑射、锁子甲与札甲防护、长枪冲击、攻城器械和成熟的堡垒体系见长。马穆鲁克训练体系严格，重视骑术、射术、编队与实战演练。虽未形成后世火药优势，但在对抗蒙古机动作战与十字军城堡战方面经验丰富。
technology.en: The army excelled in composite-bow mounted warfare, mail and lamellar protection, lance shock tactics, siege engines, and a mature fortress network. The Mamluk training system was strict, emphasizing horsemanship, archery, formation discipline, and practical drill. Although it lacked later gunpowder advantages, it was highly experienced in countering Mongol mobile warfare and Crusader fortress fighting.

threats.zh: 首要外部威胁是伊儿汗国蒙古军再次入侵叙利亚的可能，以及与其结盟的亚美尼亚奇里乞亚王国。残存十字军沿海据点虽已衰落，仍构成局部海岸威胁。内部则有马穆鲁克派系斗争、地方埃米尔离心和贝都因扰动。
threats.en: The primary external threat was renewed Ilkhanid Mongol invasion into Syria, along with their alignment with Cilician Armenia. The remaining Crusader coastal enclaves, though weakened, still posed localized littoral threats. Internally, factional conflict among Mamluks, centrifugal provincial amirs, and Bedouin unrest remained persistent dangers.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约六百万，以埃及尼罗河流域最为密集，叙利亚城镇与农耕带次之。开罗是全帝国最大都会之一，大马士革与阿勒颇为叙利亚重镇。总体人口在经历十字军战争与蒙古冲击后仍维持强劲恢复力。
populationDescription.en: The population was about six million, concentrated most densely in the Egyptian Nile Valley, with Syria's towns and cultivated belts forming the secondary core. Cairo was one of the greatest metropolises of the empire, while Damascus and Aleppo were the principal Syrian cities. Overall population retained strong resilience despite the disruptions of Crusader warfare and Mongol invasions.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为苏丹、王族、埃米尔和高级乌里玛；中层包括法官、文书、商人、行会首领和富裕手工业者；下层为农民、城市工匠、劳工、运输者与家内仆役；另有奴隶、战俘出身群体以及科普特、犹太、亚美尼亚等宗教社群。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the sultan, princely households, amirs, and senior ulama; the middle strata included judges, scribes, merchants, guild leaders, and prosperous artisans; the lower strata comprised peasants, urban craftsmen, laborers, transport workers, and domestic servants; there were also slave and war-captive populations as well as Coptic, Jewish, Armenian, and other religious communities.

allies.zh: 马穆鲁克苏丹国没有稳定意义上的对等盟国，但与金帐汗国存在共同牵制伊儿汗国的战略默契，并与部分红海、麦加-麦地那地区势力和北非伊斯兰政权保持友好联系。
allies.en: The Mamluk Sultanate had no permanent equal ally in the strict sense, but shared a strategic understanding with the Golden Horde against the Ilkhanate, while maintaining friendly ties with powers of the Red Sea, the Mecca-Medina region, and Islamic states in North Africa.

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为伊儿汗国及其附庸或协作者，包括奇里乞亚亚美尼亚；同时敌视残存十字军沿海据点。对拜占庭、安纳托利亚诸政权和拉丁西欧国家则多采取审慎接触与有限现实外交。
enemies.en: Its chief enemy was the Ilkhanate and its dependents or collaborators, including Cilician Armenia; it was also hostile to the remaining Crusader coastal enclaves. Toward Byzantium, Anatolian polities, and the Latin western kingdoms, it generally pursued cautious engagement and limited pragmatic diplomacy.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保卫叙利亚门户、遏制蒙古西进、逐步削弱十字军据点、控制麦加朝觐线路和维持红海—东地中海贸易优势为核心。盖拉温时期外交与军事并用，在战争准备、边境威慑、使节往来与商贸协调之间保持灵活平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on defending the Syrian gateway, containing further Mongol advance, gradually weakening Crusader enclaves, controlling the pilgrimage routes to Mecca, and preserving dominance over Red Sea-eastern Mediterranean trade. Under Qalawun, diplomacy and military pressure were combined in a flexible balance of war preparation, frontier deterrence, embassies, and commercial coordination.

era.zh: 中世纪后期伊斯兰军事与商业文明高峰阶段
era.en: High phase of late medieval Islamic military and commercial civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的马穆鲁克骑兵训练制度、复合弓骑射战术、城堡与攻城体系、医院与经学院组织、港口税务管理、水利维护制度、精细金属镶嵌工艺和跨区域商队金融安排
keyInnovations.en: A mature Mamluk cavalry training system, composite-bow mounted tactics, fortress and siege systems, organized hospitals and madrasas, port customs administration, irrigation maintenance regimes, fine inlaid metalworking, and interregional caravan-finance arrangements

strengths.zh: 马穆鲁克苏丹国的优势在于高质量骑兵军团、稳固的尼罗河农业税基、开罗与叙利亚城市网络、连接印度洋与地中海的贸易枢纽地位，以及以逊尼派护教者身份建立的政治合法性。它是1280年前后最有能力阻止蒙古继续南下西进的伊斯兰强国。
strengths.en: The Mamluk Sultanate's strengths lay in its high-quality cavalry corps, solid Nile agrarian tax base, the urban network of Cairo and Syria, its position as the trade hinge between the Indian Ocean and the Mediterranean, and political legitimacy built on Sunni defense of Islam. Around 1280 it was the Islamic great power best able to block further Mongol advance southward and westward.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括继承制度不稳、宫廷军阀化、财政对农业与贸易通畅的高度依赖、地方埃米尔权力坐大，以及海军和长期远征能力相对有限。统治集团与本地多数人口之间的社会隔阂，也使政权容易受内部派系斗争影响。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included unstable succession, militarized court politics, heavy dependence on uninterrupted agriculture and trade, the growing power of provincial amirs, and relatively limited naval and long-range expeditionary capacity. The social distance between the ruling elite and the local majority also left the state vulnerable to internal factional struggles.

outlook.zh: 1280年的前景总体乐观。若盖拉温继续巩固军政结构并压制伊儿汗国在叙利亚的攻势，苏丹国有望进一步清除十字军据点、强化贸易收入并稳居伊斯兰世界核心强权之一。但其长期稳定仍取决于能否驯服马穆鲁克派系竞争与维持财政纪律。
outlook.en: The outlook in 1280 was broadly favorable. If Qalawun continued consolidating the military-political order and checking Ilkhanid offensives in Syria, the sultanate was well positioned to further eliminate Crusader enclaves, expand trade income, and remain one of the central great powers of the Islamic world. Its long-term stability, however, would still depend on controlling Mamluk factional rivalry and maintaining fiscal discipline.

description.zh: 控制埃及与叙利亚的军人王朝，是阻止蒙古西进的主要伊斯兰强国。
description.en: The military sultanate ruling Egypt and Syria, and the leading Islamic power that checked Mongol expansion.

governmentForm.zh: 蒙古汗国君主制，结合草原军政传统、波斯财政官僚体系与伊拉克—伊朗地方行政结构的复合型帝国统治
governmentForm.en: A Mongol khanate monarchy combining steppe military-political traditions with Persian fiscal bureaucracy and the local administrative structures of Iraq and Iran

socialStructure.zh: 社会由蒙古与突厥军事贵族、波斯文官与税务阶层、城市商人和手工业者、乌里玛与宗教社群领袖、乡村地主与农民、部落牧民以及附属少数族群构成，具有明显的族群与职业分层
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Mongol and Turkic military aristocrats, Persian bureaucrats and fiscal officials, urban merchants and artisans, ulama and religious community leaders, rural landholders and peasants, tribal pastoralists, and subordinate minority groups, with clear ethnic and occupational stratification

rulingClass.zh: 旭烈兀家族成员、蒙古那颜与军功贵族、亲附的突厥诸部首领，以及依附宫廷的波斯高等文官与财政官员
rulingClass.en: Members of the Huleguid house, Mongol noyans and military nobles, allied Turkic tribal chiefs, and senior Persian court and fiscal administrators

succession.zh: 以成吉思汗黄金家族男性后裔的继承资格为核心，实际由宗王、军事贵族与宫廷集团协商拥立，带有草原选举与家族继承并行的特点
succession.en: Succession centered on the eligibility of male descendants of Chinggis Khan, but in practice depended on the support of princes, military nobles, and court factions, blending steppe election with dynastic inheritance

structure.zh: 中央政权由伊儿汗宫廷、诸王与那颜会议、御前宿卫体系和波斯化的财政文书机关共同构成。1280年前后，阿八哈汗倚重蒙古军政贵族，同时依赖波斯裔文官管理税收、赋役、驿站、农地、城市与工匠事务。
structure.en: The central government was formed by the Ilkhan's court, councils of princes and noyans, the imperial guard system, and Persianized fiscal and chancery institutions. Around 1280, Abaqa Khan relied on Mongol military aristocrats while depending heavily on Persian officials to manage taxation, labor obligations, relay stations, lands, cities, and artisan affairs.

name.zh: 御前中书与敕令文书署

function.zh: 起草汗廷诏令、任命文书、外交信函与行政记录，协调蒙古语、波斯语及阿拉伯语文书事务
function.en: Drafted royal decrees, appointments, diplomatic correspondence, and administrative records, coordinating documentation in Mongolian, Persian, and Arabic

function.zh: 负责田赋、商税、人头税、贡纳、官仓与地方征收核算，是帝国财政运转核心
function.en: Oversaw land tax, commercial taxes, poll taxes, tribute, state granaries, and provincial revenue accounting, serving as the core of imperial finance

function.zh: 管理军队编制、军粮军马、牧场分配、征召与边境驻防，联络蒙古千户百户体系
function.en: Managed army organization, military provisions and horses, pasture allocations, mobilization, and frontier garrisons, linking with the Mongol decimal command system

function.zh: 维护驿站网络、信使、道路补给与官方旅行凭证，保障帝国军政与贸易信息流通
function.en: Maintained the relay-station network, couriers, road supplies, and official travel warrants, ensuring military, administrative, and commercial communications

function.zh: 管理授予寺院、教会、清真寺与宗教人士的豁免、保护状与仲裁关系，维持多宗教帝国秩序
function.en: Administered exemptions, protection charters, and arbitration relationships for monasteries, churches, mosques, and religious personnel, preserving order in a multi-religious empire

function.zh: 征调与管理工匠、军械作坊、织造、金工、宫廷器物及部分战利品再分配
function.en: Requisitioned and supervised artisans, armories, textile production, metalworking, court manufactures, and part of the redistribution of war booty

function.zh: 巡察地方长官、审核税额、处理重大申诉，并协调蒙古法与伊斯兰、习惯法之间的适用
function.en: Inspected provincial governors, audited taxes, handled major petitions, and coordinated the application of Mongol law with Islamic and customary law

localAdmin.zh: 地方上以行省、州城和乡里层级治理，通常由蒙古或突厥军事总督掌控武装与征收，波斯裔书记官、税吏和城市长官负责日常行政。伊拉克阿拉伯、阿塞拜疆、法尔斯、呼罗珊等地区保留了相当多前伊斯兰与伊斯兰时期的行政惯例。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through provincial, urban, and rural tiers. Mongol or Turkic military governors usually controlled force and extraction, while Persian secretaries, tax officials, and city administrators handled routine governance. Regions such as Iraq-i Arab, Azerbaijan, Fars, and Khurasan retained many pre-Mongol and Islamic administrative practices.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系具有多元并存特征：统治层尊奉蒙古法令与汗谕，穆斯林居民在民事、婚姻与宗教事务上多受伊斯兰法影响，基督徒、犹太人及地方社群亦保有部分自治法权。重大政治案件与军纪问题由汗廷及那颜裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was pluralistic: the ruling elite upheld Mongol ordinances and khanal decrees, Muslim inhabitants were often governed by Islamic law in civil, family, and religious matters, and Christians, Jews, and local communities retained elements of autonomous jurisdiction. Major political and military disciplinary cases were judged by the khanal court and noyans.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括土地税、城市商业税、市场税、牲畜与牧地征收、人头与户税、附庸贡赋及临时军需征调。驿站供给和军马饲养对民间构成额外负担，但阿八哈时期相较征服初期已趋于制度化。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation included land tax, urban commercial dues, market taxes, levies on livestock and pasture, poll and household taxes, tributary payments from dependents, and extraordinary military requisitions. Support for relay stations and horse fodder imposed additional burdens, though under Abaqa these demands were more institutionalized than in the initial conquest era.

religion.zh: 国家上层仍保持宗教宽容与多信仰并存格局。阿八哈本人倾向佛教并延续蒙古萨满传统，同时宫廷中有聂斯脱里派基督徒、佛教徒、穆斯林和少量其他宗教人士；帝国人口主体则以伊斯兰教为主，尤其在波斯和伊拉克城市地区。
religion.en: The state elite still maintained religious tolerance and a multi-faith order. Abaqa himself leaned toward Buddhism while continuing Mongol shamanic traditions, and the court included Nestorian Christians, Buddhists, Muslims, and smaller groups; the majority of the empire's population, however, was Muslim, especially in the urban regions of Persia and Iraq.

philosophy.zh: 思想文化以波斯—伊斯兰学术传统为主，包括法学、神学、文学、历史书写与行政实务思想；同时蒙古统治带来草原政治观、普世帝国观和跨欧亚知识流动，形成多元而务实的宫廷文化。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was dominated by the Persian-Islamic scholarly tradition, including jurisprudence, theology, literature, historiography, and administrative thought; Mongol rule also introduced steppe political concepts, universal imperial ideology, and trans-Eurasian knowledge exchange, creating a diverse and pragmatic court culture.

writingSystem.zh: 行政与学术文书主要使用波斯文和阿拉伯文书写的阿拉伯字母体系；蒙古统治层亦使用蒙古文，部分外交与宗教环境中可见叙利亚文、亚美尼亚文和格鲁吉亚文。
writingSystem.en: Administration and scholarship primarily used Persian and Arabic written in the Arabic script; the Mongol ruling elite also used the Mongolian script, while Syriac, Armenian, and Georgian appeared in certain diplomatic and religious contexts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 伊儿汗国时期是波斯与美索不达米亚在蒙古统治下重新整合的重要阶段。大不里士、马拉盖和巴格达保持学术与商业活力；马拉盖天文台的学术传统仍具影响力；宫廷赞助史学、抄本制作、金工、织物和跨区域艺术风格融合，推动了伊朗—蒙古视觉文化的发展。
culturalAchievements.en: The Ilkhanid period marked a major reintegration of Persia and Mesopotamia under Mongol rule. Tabriz, Maragha, and Baghdad remained active centers of scholarship and commerce; the scholarly legacy of the Maragha observatory was still influential; court patronage of historiography, manuscript production, metalwork, textiles, and blended artistic styles advanced an Iranian-Mongol visual culture.

languageFamily.zh: 主体行政与文学语言属伊朗语族中的波斯语；统治军事上层广泛使用蒙古语与突厥语；阿拉伯语在宗教、法学和学术领域仍极为重要。
languageFamily.en: The principal administrative and literary language was Persian of the Iranian family; Mongolian and Turkic were widely used among the ruling military elite; Arabic remained highly important in religion, law, and scholarship.

gdpDescription.zh: 1280年前后的伊儿汗国经济在西亚游牧—农耕帝国中处于高位。其核心财富来自伊拉克和伊朗高原农业、手工业城市、长途商路税收以及横贯欧亚的蒙古和平所促进的转运贸易。虽然局部地区仍受战争与赋役压力影响，但整体处于繁荣恢复阶段。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1280 the Ilkhanate ranked among the wealthiest pastoral-agrarian empires of West Asia. Its core wealth came from the agriculture of Iraq and the Iranian plateau, urban manufactures, taxation of long-distance trade routes, and transit commerce encouraged by the wider Mongol peace across Eurasia. Although some regions still suffered from warfare and fiscal pressure, the economy as a whole was in a phase of thriving recovery.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、小麦与大麦种植、园艺与葡萄栽培、畜牧、丝织与毛织、金属加工、陶器、造纸、制糖、地毯前身工艺、军械制造、骡马与驼队运输、香料与奢侈品转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, wheat and barley cultivation, horticulture and viticulture, animal husbandry, silk and wool textiles, metalworking, ceramics, papermaking, sugar production, proto-carpet crafts, armaments manufacture, mule-horse-camel transport, and the entrepot trade in spices and luxuries

tradeGoods.zh: 生丝、织锦、羊毛织物、棉布、金属器、马匹、染料、纸张、糖、干果、珍珠、香料、药材、皮革、奴隶、宝石及来自印度洋与地中海的转口货物
tradeGoods.en: Raw silk, brocades, wool textiles, cotton cloth, metal goods, horses, dyes, paper, sugar, dried fruits, pearls, spices, medicinal substances, leather, slaves, gemstones, and re-exported goods from the Indian Ocean and Mediterranean

name.zh: 第纳尔与第尔汗并行货币

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。城市商人、税包征承办者、大地主和受宠军贵积累了可观财富；普通农户多持有有限农具、牲畜与粮食储备；游牧家庭财富主要体现为马、羊、牛和骆驼等牲群规模。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied enormously. Urban merchants, tax farmers, large landholders, and favored military nobles accumulated substantial riches; ordinary peasant households generally possessed limited tools, animals, and grain reserves; pastoral households measured wealth primarily in horses, sheep, cattle, and camels.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制是国家征收、王公分封收益、城市市场交换、部落牧业与国际商贸并存的混合体系。蒙古统治重视控制路线、驿站和商队保护，波斯文官则强化账册和税收制度，使帝国经济兼具掠夺遗产与常规财政化特征。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a mixed one combining state extraction, princely appanage revenues, urban market exchange, tribal pastoralism, and international commerce. Mongol rule emphasized control of routes, relay stations, and caravan protection, while Persian officials strengthened bookkeeping and taxation, giving the empire both the legacy of conquest and the traits of regularized fiscal administration.

source.en: Commercial taxes, customs, and market dues
Tribute from dependents and subordinate polities

source.zh: 官营作坊、矿产及杂项收入
source.en: State workshops, mines, and miscellaneous income

category.en: Religious patronage, public works, and relief

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库在阿八哈时期保持相对充盈，得益于贸易税和农业恢复，但仍需应对对马穆鲁克战争、对草原诸汗国竞争以及王公赏赐带来的高额支出。财政稳定性高于征服初期，但尚未完全摆脱掠夺性征收遗留问题。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury under Abaqa remained relatively full thanks to trade taxes and agricultural recovery, though it had to absorb heavy costs from wars with the Mamluks, rivalry with other steppe khanates, and princely distributions. Fiscal stability was stronger than in the early conquest period, but the state had not fully escaped the legacy of extractive exactions.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体倾向在维持蒙古军事精英利益的同时，依靠波斯官僚改进税额核算、稳定农业收入、保障商路和增加关税收益。其重点不是减税，而是提高可持续征收能力与现金化程度。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy generally aimed to preserve the interests of the Mongol military elite while using Persian bureaucracy to improve assessments, stabilize agrarian revenues, secure trade routes, and raise customs income. The emphasis was not on lowering taxes but on making extraction more sustainable and more monetized.

description.zh: 帝国军队核心，由重轻骑混编的机动部队组成，擅长弓骑、突击、包抄和远距离作战，是与马穆鲁克和草原敌手对抗的主力。
description.en: The core of the imperial army, composed of mobile mixed light and heavy cavalry skilled in horse archery, shock action, encirclement, and long-distance campaigning; it was the principal force against the Mamluks and steppe rivals.

name.en: Royal guard and princely household troops

description.zh: 直属汗廷和宗王的精锐部队，负责宫廷警卫、统帅护卫和关键战场预备队任务。
description.en: Elite troops directly attached to the Ilkhan and princes, responsible for court security, command escort, and key battlefield reserve duties.

name.zh: 地方征发骑兵与部落附属军
name.en: Provincial cavalry levies and tribal auxiliaries

description.zh: 由附属部落、地方军头和受封贵族按需征集，补充边境巡防、侦察与季节性远征。
description.en: Raised as needed from subordinate tribes, local military leaders, and enfeoffed nobles, supplementing frontier patrols, reconnaissance, and seasonal campaigns.

name.zh: 步兵、工匠兵与攻城部队
name.en: Infantry, engineer corps, and siege troops

description.zh: 主要来自伊朗、伊拉克和高加索定居人口，承担守城、营垒、器械操作、投石机与后勤任务，在叙利亚方向作战时尤为重要。
description.en: Drawn mainly from the settled populations of Iran, Iraq, and the Caucasus, they handled garrison duty, camp works, machinery operation, trebuchets, and logistics, especially important in campaigns toward Syria.

name.zh: 亚美尼亚、格鲁吉亚及其他盟属部队
name.en: Armenian, Georgian, and other allied contingents

description.zh: 由高加索与安纳托利亚盟邦提供，参与边境作战、山地行动和对马穆鲁克战争中的联合作战。
description.en: Supplied by Caucasian and Anatolian allies, these troops served in frontier warfare, mountain operations, and joint campaigns against the Mamluks.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿八哈汗

technology.zh: 军事技术综合蒙古草原机动作战传统与中东攻城技术，装备复合弓、长枪、刀剑、札甲和锁子甲，并运用投石机、攻城塔、壕沟工事及驿站后勤体系。火药武器尚未成为主导，但经欧亚交流已知其军事潜力。
technology.en: Military technology combined Mongol steppe mobility with Middle Eastern siege methods, using composite bows, lances, sabers, lamellar armor, and mail, along with trebuchets, siege towers, fieldworks, and the relay-based logistical system. Gunpowder weapons were not yet dominant, though their potential was becoming known through Eurasian exchange.

threats.zh: 首要外敌是马穆鲁克苏丹国，双方争夺叙利亚与上美索不达米亚；北方与西北方向需防范钦察汗国势力与高加索通道冲突；东方则关注察合台诸势力及中亚商路安全。内部威胁包括宗王纷争、地方税收失控和部族忠诚波动。
threats.en: The primary external enemy was the Mamluk Sultanate, with both sides contesting Syria and Upper Mesopotamia; to the north and northwest the Ilkhanate had to watch the Golden Horde and conflicts over Caucasian corridors; to the east it monitored Chagatai forces and the security of Central Asian trade routes. Internal threats included princely factionalism, loss of control over provincial revenues, and fluctuating tribal loyalties.

populationDescription.zh: 伊儿汗国人口主要分布于伊拉克平原、阿塞拜疆、伊朗西部和呼罗珊绿洲地带。城市人口在征服后已有明显恢复，但全国仍以农村农民与半游牧、游牧群体为主体，族群与宗教成分极其复杂。
populationDescription.en: The Ilkhanate's population was concentrated in the plains of Iraq, Azerbaijan, western Iran, and the oasis zones of Khurasan. Urban life had substantially recovered after the conquests, but the empire remained predominantly rural, with peasants and semi-nomadic or nomadic groups forming the majority, amid great ethnic and religious complexity.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为蒙古皇族、那颜和受封军事贵族；其下是波斯文官、宗教精英、城市豪商与地主；中层包括工匠、店主、抄写员与中小地主；底层主要是农民、佃户、城市贫民、运输劳工和部分奴隶。另有大量部落牧民游离于定居等级之外，但在军事与税收上极为重要。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the Mongol royal house, noyans, and enfeoffed military aristocrats; beneath them were Persian bureaucrats, religious elites, wealthy merchants, and landholders; the middle layers included artisans, shopkeepers, scribes, and lesser proprietors; the lower strata consisted mainly of peasants, tenants, urban poor, transport laborers, and some slaves. Large numbers of tribal pastoralists stood partly outside settled hierarchies but were crucial militarily and fiscally.

allies.zh: 主要盟友包括奇里乞亚亚美尼亚王国、格鲁吉亚诸势力及部分十字军沿海据点；与拜占庭帝国和部分拉丁欧洲政权保持使节往来，并持续寻求与法兰西、英格兰及教廷联合夹击马穆鲁克。
allies.en: Its main allies included the Armenian Kingdom of Cilicia, Georgian forces, and some Crusader coastal enclaves; it also maintained diplomatic contacts with the Byzantine Empire and certain Latin European powers, while persistently seeking a joint anti-Mamluk alignment with France, England, and the papacy.

enemies.zh: 首敌为马穆鲁克苏丹国；此外与钦察汗国长期敌对，并与察合台方向存在潜在冲突。对鲁姆苏丹国则保持宗主—附庸与干预并存的复杂关系。
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was the Mamluk Sultanate; it was also in long-term hostility with the Golden Horde and faced potential friction from the Chagatai direction. Its relationship with the Sultanate of Rum was more ambiguous, combining overlordship with intervention.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以三项目标为核心：压制马穆鲁克、维护高加索与安纳托利亚通道、保障欧亚商路安全。阿八哈积极经营横跨地中海与欧亚草原的使节网络，试图将蒙古世界内部联动与对欧洲联盟外交结合起来。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on three objectives: containing the Mamluks, maintaining the Caucasian and Anatolian corridors, and securing Eurasian trade routes. Abaqa actively cultivated a diplomatic network stretching across the Mediterranean and the steppe, trying to combine intra-Mongol coordination with alliance diplomacy toward Europe.

era.zh: 十三世纪蒙古帝国时代的西亚综合技术体系
era.en: West Asian composite technological system of the 13th-century Mongol imperial era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键优势不在单一发明，而在技术汇聚：蒙古驿传与远程军政调度、波斯财政记账、成熟的灌溉农业、复合弓骑兵战法、改良攻城术、跨大陆天文与医学知识传播，以及多语文书治理能力。
keyInnovations.en: Its key strengths lay less in a single invention than in technological integration: Mongol relay communications and long-range command, Persian fiscal record-keeping, mature irrigation agriculture, composite-bow cavalry warfare, improved siegecraft, transcontinental transmission of astronomical and medical knowledge, and multilingual documentary governance.

strengths.zh: 伊儿汗国在1280年拥有西亚最强大的综合实力之一：控制波斯和伊拉克核心财富区，握有连接地中海、中亚与印度洋的贸易节点，军事机动性强，外交触角广，且在蒙古世界中具有重要战略地位。
strengths.en: In 1280 the Ilkhanate possessed one of the strongest overall power positions in West Asia: it controlled the core wealth zones of Persia and Iraq, held trade nodes linking the Mediterranean, Central Asia, and the Indian Ocean, fielded highly mobile forces, enjoyed broad diplomatic reach, and occupied a major strategic place within the Mongol world.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于统治集团族群分裂、继承不稳、财政对高压征收依赖较大、地方行政整合尚未完成，以及对叙利亚方向战争长期消耗严重。宗教上层与人口主体之间也存在一定认同断裂。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in ethnic divisions within the ruling elite, unstable succession, strong dependence on heavy extraction, incomplete provincial integration, and the severe long-term drain of warfare in the Syrian theater. There was also a degree of disconnect between the religious orientation of the upper court and that of the majority population.

outlook.zh: 短期前景总体积极，国家仍处于繁荣与扩张企图并存阶段，若能稳定财政、整合文官体系并避免宗王内斗，仍可继续主导西亚政治。但若对马穆鲁克战争失利或继承危机爆发，其强盛局面将迅速受损。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was broadly positive, as the state remained both prosperous and expansionist; if it could stabilize finances, integrate its bureaucracy, and avoid princely infighting, it could continue to dominate West Asian politics. But defeat against the Mamluks or a succession crisis could quickly undermine its power.

description.zh: 波斯与伊拉克的蒙古政权，是西亚最强大的国家之一。
description.en: The Mongol state of Persia and Iraq, one of the dominant powers of West Asia.

governmentForm.zh: 由蒙古大汗兼中国皇帝统治的多民族征服王朝与中央集权帝国
governmentForm.en: A multiethnic conquest dynasty and centralized empire ruled by the Mongol Great Khan as Emperor of China

socialStructure.zh: 社会按征服秩序与职业层级分化，朝廷通常将居民区分为蒙古人、色目人、汉人、南人等大类；上层为皇室、蒙古贵族与高级军政官员，中层为地方官、儒士、僧侣、商人和手工业者，下层为广大农民、屯田军户、差役与部分贱役人群。
socialStructure.en: Society was stratified by conquest status and occupational hierarchy, with the court commonly distinguishing Mongols, Semuren or 'various categories' of Inner and West Asians, former Jin subjects in the north, and southern subjects of the former Song. The upper ranks were the imperial clan, Mongol nobles, and senior military-administrative officials; the middle ranks included local officials, literati, clergy, merchants, and artisans; the lower ranks were the broad peasantry, military-agricultural households, corvée laborers, and some servile groups.

rulingClass.zh: 孛儿只斤皇族、蒙古诸王与勋贵、色目高官以及受任用的汉地文武官僚
rulingClass.en: The Borjigin imperial clan, Mongol princes and nobles, senior Semuren officials, and appointed Chinese civil and military administrators

succession.zh: 皇位原则上由孛儿只斤皇族继承，需经宫廷与宗王政治协商确认，兼具蒙古忽里勒台传统与中原皇位继承观念
succession.en: The throne in principle passed within the Borjigin imperial house, but required confirmation through court and princely politics, combining Mongol quriltai traditions with Chinese concepts of imperial succession

structure.zh: 1280年的元朝中央政务以中书省为核心，总领全国行政；枢密院掌军政，御史台掌监察，宣政院逐步负责吐蕃与佛教事务，财政、盐铁、仓储、漕运、驿站等多由专门机构分掌。地方上以行中书省、路、府、州、县构成层级治理体系，但在1280年前后行省制度仍处于定型过程中，边疆与新附地区常由军政合一机构治理。
structure.en: In 1280 the Yuan central government revolved around the Central Secretariat, which supervised overall civil administration. The Bureau of Military Affairs handled military governance, the Censorate oversaw surveillance and remonstrance, and the Xuanzheng Yuan was emerging as the office for Tibetan and Buddhist affairs. Finance, salt and iron, granaries, transport, and the relay-post network were handled by specialized bureaus. Locally, governance operated through a hierarchy of branch secretariats, circuits, prefectures, subprefectures, and counties, though the provincial system was still taking shape around 1280, and frontier or recently conquered regions were often ruled through combined military-civil institutions.

function.zh: 总理全国行政、诏令、官员任命、财政协调与地方政务
function.en: Supervised empire-wide civil administration, edicts, appointments, fiscal coordination, and provincial governance

function.zh: 统筹军队调动、军籍、守备、边防与军令传达
function.en: Directed troop mobilization, military registers, garrisons, frontier defense, and transmission of commands

function.zh: 监察百官、弹劾贪污、巡按地方并纠察司法与财政失当
function.en: Monitored officials, impeached corruption, inspected the provinces, and investigated judicial and fiscal misconduct

function.zh: 管理吐蕃事务、僧侣关系及部分佛教教团与边疆行政
function.en: Managed Tibetan affairs, clerical relations, and portions of Buddhist and frontier administration

name.zh: 户部及诸财赋转运机构

function.zh: 掌户籍、赋税、钱钞、仓储、漕运、盐课和物资征解
function.en: Managed household registration, taxation, paper currency, granaries, grain transport, salt revenues, and material requisitions

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以行中书省与宣慰司等机构统辖区域政务，下设路、府、州、县。北方旧金地区行政较成熟，江南新附地区则在继承宋制基础上逐步改组。边疆、云南、吐蕃和东北等地多采用因俗而治、土官羁縻、军镇控制与驿站网络相结合的方式。
localAdmin.en: Regional administration was exercised through branch secretariats and pacification offices, beneath which stood circuits, prefectures, subprefectures, and counties. The former Jin north had a more mature administrative apparatus, while the newly conquered south was being reorganized on top of inherited Song institutions. In frontiers such as Yunnan, Tibet, and the northeast, the Yuan frequently relied on a combination of indirect rule, native chieftains, garrisons, and the relay-post system.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系融合蒙古习惯法、可汗敕令与中原成文法传统。朝廷重视诏令、断例和行政裁量，刑罚中笞杖、徒流、籍没并用。不同族群在司法实践中有时适用不同标准，体现征服王朝的差别待遇与实用主义。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Mongol customary law, khanal decrees, and Chinese written legal traditions. The court relied heavily on edicts, precedents, and administrative discretion, while punishments included beating, penal servitude, exile, and confiscation. In practice, different groups were at times judged under different standards, reflecting the conquest dynasty's hierarchical and pragmatic rule.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以田赋、户税、商税、盐课、酒醋课、市舶税、矿冶收入及差发徭役为主。江南富庶地区提供大量粮赋与丝织税源，北方则兼具屯田、牧税与军需征发。元廷还依赖钞法推动财政征收，并通过漕运与仓储体系把财赋输送至大都。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested on land taxes, household levies, commercial taxes, salt revenues, alcohol and vinegar taxes, maritime customs, mining income, and labor services. The wealthy south supplied major grain and silk-based revenues, while the north also contributed from military colonies, pasture-related levies, and military requisitions. The Yuan court further relied on paper-currency policy to facilitate fiscal collection and moved large shares of revenue to Dadu through canal and granary transport.

religion.zh: 元朝宗教生态高度多元，宫廷尤其崇奉藏传佛教，同时并存汉传佛教、道教、儒家礼制、伊斯兰教、景教与民间信仰。忽必烈采取总体上包容而由国家仲裁的政策，在必要时限制教团特权以维持秩序与税收。
religion.en: The Yuan religious world was highly plural. The court especially patronized Tibetan Buddhism, while Chinese Buddhism, Daoism, Confucian ritual culture, Islam, Christianity, and local cults also flourished. Kublai generally pursued a policy of tolerance under state arbitration, though clerical privileges could be curbed when they threatened order or revenue.

philosophy.zh: 思想上呈现蒙古政治传统、佛教护法观、儒家治国术与法令实务并行的格局。儒学仍是地方治理和士人教育的重要框架，但在1280年尚未完全恢复科举主导地位；宫廷更重视能臣、财赋、军政与跨文化治理能力。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life combined Mongol political traditions, Buddhist ideas of sacral kingship, Confucian statecraft, and legal-administrative pragmatism. Confucian learning remained central to local governance and elite education, but by 1280 it had not yet fully regained exam-based dominance; the court valued effective administrators, fiscal expertise, military capacity, and cross-cultural governing skills.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为汉字，蒙古文在皇室和草原事务中广泛使用，畏兀儿文传统影响蒙古文文书体制；同时波斯文、阿拉伯文、藏文和八思巴字在特定行政、宗教与外交场合使用。
writingSystem.en: The main writing system was Chinese characters, while Mongolian was widely used in imperial and steppe affairs and drew on the Uighur script tradition for documentary practice. Persian, Arabic, Tibetan, and the newly promoted 'Phags-pa script were also used in specific administrative, religious, and diplomatic contexts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 元朝在大都营建、运河与漕运整合、天文历法吸收回回学术、宫廷绘画与工艺、杂剧和说唱文学发展、跨欧亚知识与工匠流动方面成就显著。1279年灭宋后，全国文化资源进一步集中于元廷统治之下，形成兼具草原帝国与中华帝国特征的复合文明。
culturalAchievements.en: The Yuan achieved major advances in the building of Dadu, the integration of canal and grain transport systems, the adoption of Islamic astronomical knowledge, court painting and crafts, the growth of zaju drama and vernacular performance traditions, and the movement of knowledge and artisans across Eurasia. After the conquest of the Song in 1279, the empire concentrated the cultural resources of all China under one regime, producing a composite civilization marked by both steppe-imperial and Chinese-imperial features.

languageFamily.zh: 统治层主要使用蒙古语，行政与人口主体广泛使用汉语诸方言；帝国内还通行突厥语、藏语、波斯语、女真与契丹余脉语言、朝鲜语等，整体呈阿尔泰语系与汉藏语系并存的多语环境。
languageFamily.en: The ruling stratum mainly used Mongolian, while administration and the bulk of the population widely used varieties of Chinese. Turkic, Tibetan, Persian, remnants of Jurchen and Khitan usage, and Korean were also present, creating a multilingual environment shaped above all by the Mongolic and Sino-Tibetan language spheres.

unit.en: taels of silver annual value per person

gdpDescription.zh: 元朝经济总量在1280年约居世界前列，核心基础是中国成熟的稻麦农业、江南手工业与全国性市场网络，再叠加蒙古帝国带来的欧亚贸易通道。其财富远高于同时代多数单一王国，但财政吸取能力与真实民间生产之间并不完全一致，且战后重建与新政成本很高。
gdpDescription.en: In 1280 the Yuan economy was among the largest in the world. Its foundations were China's mature rice and wheat agriculture, the handicraft industries of the Yangzi region, and an integrated internal market, further amplified by the trans-Eurasian routes opened by Mongol imperial rule. Its aggregate wealth exceeded that of most contemporary single kingdoms, though the state's fiscal extraction did not fully correspond to total private production, and post-conquest reconstruction and new imperial projects were costly.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、丝织与纺织、瓷器、冶铁、盐业、漕运、造船、茶业、纸张与印刷、城市手工业、马政与牧业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, silk weaving and textiles, ceramics, ironworking, salt production, grain transport, shipbuilding, tea, papermaking and printing, urban handicrafts, horse administration, and pastoral production

tradeGoods.zh: 丝绸、瓷器、茶叶、漆器、铜铁器、纸张、书籍、盐、药材、香料、珠宝、马匹、毛皮、棉布与南海舶来品
tradeGoods.en: Silk, porcelain, tea, lacquerware, copper and iron goods, paper, books, salt, medicines, spices, jewels, horses, furs, cotton cloth, and maritime imports from the South Seas

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。江南地主、盐商、海商、工商业者与受朝廷保护的特权群体积累了可观财富；北方战后恢复地区和普通农户则多承受赋役、物价波动和纸钞信用变化的压力。多数家庭以土地、粮食储备、牲畜、布帛和器具为主要财富形态。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied enormously. Landholders, salt merchants, maritime traders, manufacturers, and court-connected privileged groups in the south accumulated substantial fortunes, while ordinary peasant households and war-recovering regions of the north bore the burdens of taxes, requisitions, price instability, and shifts in paper-currency confidence. For most families, wealth remained tied to land, grain stocks, livestock, cloth, and tools.

unit.en: taels of silver annual value per worker

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的大一统农业商业经济，结合蒙古征服帝国的长途贸易网络。朝廷直接干预盐、铁、货币、粮运与部分工匠户，同时容许市场交换和私人商人广泛活动；纸钞流通、税收实物化与货币化并存，是一种高度行政化但仍充满民间活力的混合经济。
economicSystem.en: The Yuan economy was a state-directed integrated agrarian-commercial system joined to the long-distance trade networks of the Mongol world empire. The court intervened directly in salt, metals, currency, grain transport, and some hereditary artisan households, while also allowing broad market activity by private merchants. With paper money, taxes paid in both goods and cash equivalents, and extensive administrative oversight, it was a highly managed yet still vibrant mixed economy.

source.en: Alcohol, vinegar, tea, and miscellaneous taxes

category.en: Grain transport, granaries, and public works
Religious patronage, diplomacy, and special disbursements

treasuryDescription.zh: 元廷国库规模庞大，但并非全部以贵金属实存，更多体现为仓粮、盐引、纸钞信用、府库物资、布帛和可征调资源。忽必烈时期财政基础强于多数前近代国家，但开支也因战争、宫廷、赈济、运河和都城建设而极重，故常出现紧平衡甚至赤字。
treasuryDescription.en: The Yuan treasury was vast, but not stored entirely as precious metal. Much of it consisted of granary stocks, salt certificates, paper-currency credibility, textiles, arsenals, and resources that could be requisitioned. Under Kublai the fiscal base was stronger than that of most premodern states, yet expenditures were also immense because of war, court spending, relief, canal systems, and capital construction, creating frequent pressure and occasional deficits.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调扩大税基、利用纸钞整合财赋、垄断高收益行业并保障都城与军队供给。忽必烈倾向于用南方经济盈余支撑全国统治，同时通过赦免、蠲税与赈济稳定新附地区；然而频繁工程与远征会削弱财政稳健性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized broadening the tax base, using paper currency to integrate revenue flows, monopolizing high-yield sectors, and securing supplies for the capital and the armies. Kublai sought to use the surplus of the south to sustain empire-wide rule while calming newly conquered regions through remissions and relief, but repeated large projects and overseas expeditions undermined fiscal stability.

description.zh: 帝国核心机动兵力，由蒙古诸部和色目骑兵构成，长于远程机动、复合弓骑射、侦察和快速打击，是边防与远征的精锐骨干。
description.en: The core mobile arm of the empire, composed of Mongol contingents and Inner Asian cavalry, skilled in long-range movement, composite-bow warfare, reconnaissance, and rapid shock operations.

description.zh: 由华北旧金与归附汉军组成，承担城防、驻屯、攻城、后勤护运与地方镇压，是元朝占领中国北方和维持行政秩序的重要力量。
description.en: Drawn largely from north China and former Jin military populations, these troops provided garrison duty, siege operations, convoy protection, and internal security, forming a major pillar of Yuan rule in the north.

description.zh: 主要由原南宋降军编成，数量庞大，熟悉江南水网、舟师与守城事务，在南方驻防和水陆协同作战中不可或缺，但忠诚与战斗力受编制与待遇影响较大。
description.en: Composed largely of former Southern Song troops, this massive force knew the waterways, naval service, and fortress warfare of the south. It was indispensable for southern garrisons and combined land-water operations, though its reliability varied with organization and treatment.

description.zh: 继承并整合宋朝造船和江海作战传统，部署于长江、运河、东南沿海与朝鲜方向，负责运输、巡防、海上远征与登陆支援。
description.en: Built by inheriting and reorganizing former Song naval traditions, the fleet operated on the Yangzi, the canal system, the southeast coast, and the Korean approaches, providing transport, patrol, seaborne expeditions, and landing support.

description.zh: 负责皇帝宿卫、都城防务和关键宫廷警备，由蒙古亲军与精选诸军构成，政治可靠性高于一般驻军。
description.en: Charged with protecting the emperor, defending the capital, and securing the palace, these guards were drawn from Mongol household troops and select formations and were more politically reliable than ordinary garrisons.

commanderInChief.zh: 忽必烈皇帝兼大汗
commanderInChief.en: Emperor Kublai Khan as Great Khan

technology.zh: 元军综合草原骑射传统与中原攻城技术，广泛使用复合弓、甲骑、攻城塔、回回炮与火药兵器雏形。其优势在战略机动、工程组织和多族兵种协同，但在海上远征和湿热地区作战方面仍有明显限制。
technology.en: The Yuan military combined steppe mounted-archery traditions with Chinese siegecraft, employing composite bows, armored cavalry, siege towers, counterweight artillery, and early gunpowder weapons. Its strengths lay in strategic mobility, engineering, and multiethnic combined operations, though it remained limited in maritime expeditions and campaigning in hot, humid environments.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括日本拒绝臣服、海上远征风险、高丽方向后勤负担、西南山地与云南局部抵抗、草原宗王政治竞争，以及察合台汗国等蒙古诸汗国关系的不确定性。
threats.en: The main threats included Japan's refusal to submit, the risks of overseas expedition, logistical burdens through Korea, resistance in the southwest and Yunnan highlands, political rivalry among steppe princes, and uncertainty in relations with other Mongol khanates such as the Chagatai Khanate.

populationDescription.zh: 1280年的元朝人口约8500万，包含原金朝北方、南宋江南与西南新并入地区，是一个人口极为庞大的多民族帝国。人口仍在战后恢复中，北方不少地区尚未完全恢复至宋金盛时水平，而江南稠密农业区则构成全国人口与税赋重心。
populationDescription.en: The Yuan population in 1280 was about 85 million, encompassing the former Jin north, the former Southern Song south, and newly incorporated southwestern regions, making it one of the most populous empires of its age. Recovery from decades of warfare was still under way: many northern areas had not fully returned to earlier Song-Jin levels, while the densely cultivated south had become the main center of population and taxation.

socialClasses.zh: 皇室与蒙古贵族居顶层，其次为色目高官与大商人、汉地文武官员与地主士绅、僧道与宗教团体、城市商人与工匠、普通农民与军户、舟户及差役。族群身份、地域来源、职业世袭和与朝廷关系共同决定社会地位。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the imperial house and Mongol nobility, followed by Semuren high officials and major merchants, Chinese civil and military officials and local gentry, Buddhist and Daoist clergy, urban merchants and artisans, ordinary peasants and military households, and transport laborers and servile workers. Social standing depended on ethnicity, regional origin, hereditary occupation, and proximity to the court.

allies.zh: 高丽王朝是最重要的藩属与合作伙伴，在东征日本和东北秩序中扮演关键角色；与伊儿汗国总体维持较友好关系，并通过蒙古帝国网络与中亚、波斯商人及多方政权保持往来。
allies.en: The Goryeo dynasty was the Yuan's most important subordinate ally and partner, especially in the attempted invasions of Japan and in northeastern affairs. Relations with the Ilkhanate were generally favorable, and the Yuan remained connected through the wider Mongol imperial network to Central Asian, Persian, and many other polities.

enemies.zh: 日本是最显著的拒贡对象；局部抵抗势力存在于西南山地、海上与边疆地区。与察合台汗国及其他蒙古宗王之间虽未必持续全面战争，但竞争、使节纠纷与宗主权争议长期存在。
enemies.en: Japan was the most prominent non-submitting opponent. Pockets of resistance remained in the southwestern uplands, maritime zones, and other frontier regions. Relations with the Chagatai Khanate and other Mongol princely powers did not always amount to constant open war, but rivalry, diplomatic disputes, and competing claims to imperial authority persisted.

foreignPolicy.zh: 忽必烈的外交政策以天下宗主权和册封朝贡秩序为核心，同时利用蒙古帝国遗产维持跨欧亚使节与商业联系。对周边国家常先以招谕、册封和贸易诱导，拒绝者则可能施压或远征；对西方诸国则更重视情报、通商与象征性外交。
foreignPolicy.en: Kublai's foreign policy centered on universal overlordship and an investiture-tribute order, while also drawing on the legacy of the Mongol Empire to maintain trans-Eurasian diplomatic and commercial links. Neighboring states were usually approached first through demands for submission, investiture, and trade incentives, with coercion or expedition following refusal; toward more distant western powers, policy placed greater emphasis on intelligence, commerce, and symbolic diplomacy.

era.zh: 13世纪后期蒙古世界帝国时代
era.en: Late 13th-century age of the Mongol world empire

keyInnovations.zh: 纸钞国家化运用、跨区域驿站体系、整合宋代大型造船与水师技术、回回天文历算输入、改良攻城炮与火药器械、都城规划与大规模粮运组织。元朝并非所有技术都原创，但其最大特征是将草原、伊斯兰世界与中国本土技术置于同一帝国体系中加以整合。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included state-scale use of paper currency, a transregional relay-post system, the integration of large Song shipbuilding and naval techniques, the import of Islamic astronomy and mathematical methods, improved siege artillery and gunpowder devices, and sophisticated capital planning and grain-transport organization. Not all of these were originally invented by the Yuan, but its defining achievement was to integrate steppe, Islamic, and Chinese technologies within a single imperial framework.

strengths.zh: 元朝在1280年拥有当时世界最庞大的人口与经济核心之一，统一中国本部，控制草原与农耕交界，兼具蒙古军事传统和中原财政行政能力。大都作为帝国政治中心，江南作为财富腹地，驿站与海陆交通又把其嵌入横跨欧亚的贸易网络，使其综合国力极强。
strengths.en: In 1280 the Yuan possessed one of the world's largest demographic and economic cores, had unified nearly all of China proper, controlled the steppe-agrarian interface, and combined Mongol military traditions with the fiscal and administrative capacities of Chinese statecraft. With Dadu as the political center, the Yangzi south as the wealth base, and relay and maritime routes tying it into Eurasian exchange, its overall power was formidable.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于征服王朝的合法性张力、族群等级造成的社会摩擦、财政对纸钞和高强度征发的依赖、北方恢复不均、地方治理层次复杂，以及海上远征与南方湿热环境中作战的不适应。庞大的宫廷与宗王体系也会持续消耗财政和政治协调能力。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in the legitimacy tensions of a conquest dynasty, social frictions produced by ethnic hierarchy, dependence on paper currency and heavy extraction, uneven northern recovery, complex local administration, and poor adaptation to maritime expedition and humid southern campaigning. The vast court and princely structure also consumed fiscal resources and political attention.

outlook.zh: 短期内元朝处于强盛上升阶段，凭借刚完成的全国统一和巨额财政动员能力，仍将保持东亚最强国家地位。但若远征日本继续受挫、财政赤字扩大、纸钞信用波动并激化社会矛盾，其长期统治稳定性将受到挑战。
outlook.en: In the short term the Yuan was in a powerful ascending phase, and with recent unification and immense mobilizational capacity it was likely to remain the strongest state in East Asia. But if the Japanese expeditions continued to fail, fiscal deficits widened, and paper-currency credibility weakened while social tensions deepened, the long-term stability of its rule would come under strain.

description.zh: 忽必烈统治下的元朝控制中国大部并为蒙古帝国最富庶的核心国家。
description.en: Under Kublai Khan, the Yuan ruled most of China and formed the wealthiest core state of the Mongol imperial world.

dynasty.zh: 安纳托利亚塞尔柱王朝

governmentForm.zh: 受蒙古伊儿汗国宗主权制约的突厥-波斯化君主制苏丹国，中央权威衰弱，地方军政首领与宫廷派系分权明显。
governmentForm.en: A Turkic-Persianate monarchical sultanate under Mongol Ilkhanid suzerainty, with weakened central authority and strong decentralization toward local military lords and court factions.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由宫廷与军政贵族、波斯文书官僚、乌里玛与苏菲团体、城市商人与手工业者、游牧或半游牧突厥部族、以及占人口多数的乡村农民与基督徒、亚美尼亚人、希腊人等地方社群构成。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the court and military aristocracy, Persian chancery bureaucrats, ulama and Sufi circles, urban merchants and artisans, nomadic or semi-nomadic Turkmen tribes, and a majority of rural cultivators and local Christian, Armenian, and Greek communities.

rulingClass.zh: 塞尔柱王族、宫廷埃米尔、突厥军事贵族、波斯化文官集团，以及在蒙古支配下实际掌握地方资源的总督和军阀。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the Seljuk royal house, court amirs, Turkic military aristocrats, Persianized civil administrators, and provincial governors and warlords who controlled resources under Mongol dominance.

succession.zh: 原则上为王朝世袭，但1280年前后继承常受蒙古伊儿汗国批准、宫廷政变与贵族干预影响，实际并不稳定。
succession.en: Succession was nominally dynastic and hereditary, but by around 1280 it was heavily shaped by Ilkhanid approval, court coups, and aristocratic intervention, making it unstable in practice.

structure.zh: 政府沿用塞尔柱-波斯传统，以苏丹宫廷和大维齐尔为核心，下设财政、文书、军务、司法与宗教管理机构；但在1280年，蒙古驻军、贡赋要求和地方贝伊的独立性已严重削弱中央执行力。
structure.en: The government followed Seljuk-Persian traditions, centered on the sultan's court and the grand vizier, with departments for finance, chancery, military affairs, justice, and religious administration; by 1280, however, Mongol garrisons, tribute demands, and the autonomy of local beys had greatly weakened central enforcement.

function.zh: 统筹朝政、协调各部、传达苏丹与宗主蒙古当局的命令。
function.en: Coordinated state affairs, supervised departments, and transmitted the orders of the sultan and the Mongol overlords.

function.zh: 征收地税、商业税、牧税与非常贡赋，管理国库和支出记录。
function.en: Collected land taxes, commercial dues, pastoral levies, and extraordinary tribute, and managed the treasury and expenditure records.

function.zh: 起草敕令、保管档案、处理外交文书与省级任命。
function.en: Drafted decrees, kept archives, handled diplomatic correspondence, and issued provincial appointments.

function.zh: 登记军役、发放军饷、维持要塞与调度苏丹直属部队。
function.en: Registered military obligations, distributed pay, maintained fortresses, and directed troops under direct sultanic authority.

function.zh: 任命卡迪、监督教法裁判、管理宗教基金与部分城市事务。
function.en: Appointed qadis, supervised sharia adjudication, and oversaw religious endowments and some urban matters.

function.zh: 维护驿站、道路与信使网络，保障商旅和军政通讯。
function.en: Maintained relay stations, roads, and messenger networks to support trade and administrative communication.

localAdmin.zh: 地方上由省级或地区性埃米尔、总督、要塞长官和税务官管理，许多边区与高原地带实际上由土库曼贝伊与半自治军事家族控制；城市则依赖卡迪、行会首领和地方显贵维持秩序。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by provincial amirs, governors, fortress commanders, and tax officials, though many frontier and upland areas were effectively controlled by Turkmen beys and semi-autonomous military families; in towns, qadis, guild leaders, and local notables helped maintain order.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合伊斯兰教法、苏丹敕令、部族习惯与地方社群法；穆斯林主要受卡迪法庭管辖，而希腊、亚美尼亚等基督徒社群在婚姻与宗教事务上保留内部习惯法。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Islamic sharia, sultanic decrees, tribal custom, and communal law; Muslims were mainly subject to qadi courts, while Greek, Armenian, and other Christian communities retained internal customary law in marriage and religious matters.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠农业地税、灌溉与牧场税、市场税、关卡税、矿业和盐税、城市商业税，以及向蒙古宗主缴纳的沉重贡赋前提下加征的非常征收；税制在衰退期往往因包税与地方截留而加重百姓负担。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue relied mainly on agrarian land tax, irrigation and pasture dues, market taxes, customs tolls, mining and salt taxes, urban commercial levies, and extraordinary exactions imposed under the heavy burden of tribute to the Mongol overlords; in the period of decline, tax farming and local diversion often increased the burden on the population.

religion.zh: 官方主导宗教为逊尼派伊斯兰教（哈乃斐学派占优势），同时苏菲教团在安纳托利亚广泛活跃；境内仍有大量希腊正教、亚美尼亚使徒教会与叙利亚基督徒社群。
religion.en: The dominant official religion was Sunni Islam, especially the Hanafi school, while Sufi orders were highly active across Anatolia; the realm also contained large Greek Orthodox, Armenian Apostolic, and Syriac Christian communities.

philosophy.zh: 思想文化深受波斯伊斯兰政治伦理、逊尼法学、苏菲神秘主义以及边疆加齐精神影响，兼容部分古典希腊-拜占庭知识传统与地方民间信仰。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by Persian-Islamic political ethics, Sunni jurisprudence, Sufi mysticism, and frontier ghazi ideals, while also absorbing elements of classical Greek-Byzantine learning and local popular traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 行政与高雅文学主要使用波斯文和阿拉伯字母书写；阿拉伯文用于宗教与法学，突厥语在军旅和民间普遍存在但书面行政地位较弱；希腊文和亚美尼亚文继续在各自社群中使用。
writingSystem.en: Administration and elite literature were conducted mainly in Persian written in the Arabic script; Arabic was used for religion and jurisprudence, while Turkic was widespread in military and popular life but weaker in formal administration; Greek and Armenian continued in their respective communities.

culturalAchievements.zh: 鲁姆塞尔柱时期留下了安纳托利亚最重要的一批中世纪伊斯兰建筑，包括科尼亚、锡瓦斯、开塞利等地的清真寺、经学院、商队驿站与医院；石雕门户、几何装饰与波斯化宫廷文学尤具特色。此时虽已衰落，但此前数十年的建筑和苏菲文化影响仍然深远。
culturalAchievements.en: The Rum Seljuk age produced some of medieval Anatolia's most important Islamic architecture, including mosques, madrasas, caravanserais, and hospitals in Konya, Sivas, Kayseri, and elsewhere; carved stone portals, geometric ornament, and Persianate court literature were especially notable. Though in decline by 1280, the architectural and Sufi cultural legacy of earlier decades remained profound.

languageFamily.zh: 国家文化呈多语状态：统治军事精英以突厥语系为根基，行政文化高度波斯化，宗教学术使用闪含语系的阿拉伯语，地方居民还广泛使用希腊语和亚美尼亚语。
languageFamily.en: The state was multilingual: the ruling military elite was rooted in the Turkic language family, administrative culture was highly Persianized, religious scholarship used Semitic Arabic, and local populations widely spoke Greek and Armenian.

gdpDescription.zh: 1280年的鲁姆苏丹国仍拥有安纳托利亚中部农业、畜牧业、手工业和东西商路的经济基础，但蒙古贡赋、内乱、地方割据和贸易路线不稳定已明显压缩财政与市场活力，整体经济处于中等规模而走向衰退。
gdpDescription.en: In 1280 the Sultanate of Rum still possessed the economic foundations of central Anatolian agriculture, pastoralism, crafts, and east-west transit trade, but Mongol tribute, internal disorder, regional fragmentation, and unstable trade routes had clearly reduced fiscal and market vitality; the overall economy was of moderate scale but in decline.

mainIndustries.zh: 主要产业包括小麦和大麦种植、绵羊与马匹畜牧、地毯和毛织品、皮革加工、金属器制造、建筑石材、盐业、铜银等有限矿业，以及服务商队交通的驿站与贸易中介。
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included wheat and barley cultivation, sheep and horse pastoralism, carpet and wool textile production, leatherworking, metalware manufacture, building stone, salt extraction, limited mining of copper and silver, and caravanserai and brokerage services tied to overland trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 对内外贸易商品有谷物、羊毛、地毯、皮革、马匹、奴隶、染料、金属制品、木材、盐和干果；输入品包括丝绸、香料、精制布匹、纸张、奢侈品和部分金属货币。
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included grain, wool, carpets, leather, horses, slaves, dyestuffs, metal goods, timber, salt, and dried fruits; imports included silk, spices, fine textiles, paper, luxury goods, and some metallic coinage.

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在耕地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备和家用织物上；城市商人与行会工匠财富差异较大，而军政贵族通过采邑、税权和土地控制远比平民富裕。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in access to arable land, livestock, grain stores, and household textiles; urban merchants and guild artisans showed wide variation in means, while military and administrative elites were far wealthier through iqta holdings, tax rights, and land control.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为以农业租税和采邑为基础的前近代混合经济，城市市场、商队贸易和行会手工业发达，但在蒙古宗主压力下愈发依赖包税、贡赋转嫁和地方武装对资源的控制。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a premodern mixed economy based on agrarian taxation and iqta assignments, with developed urban markets, caravan trade, and guild crafts, but under Mongol suzerainty it increasingly relied on tax farming, tribute transfer, and local armed control of resources.

source.en: Agrarian land tax and iqta remittances

source.zh: 市场税、关税与商路收费
source.en: Market taxes, customs, and transit dues
Pastoral dues, salt tax, and mining revenue
Dependent lands and crown estate income

category.zh: 对蒙古宗主的贡赋与附带开支
category.en: Tribute and associated payments to the Mongol overlords

category.zh: 道路、驿站、建筑与宗教基金
category.en: Roads, relay stations, construction, and religious endowments

category.zh: 赈济、外交馈赠与非常支出
category.en: Relief, diplomatic gifts, and extraordinary expenses

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模有限，且易因蒙古征调、宫廷斗争和战时紧急支出被迅速耗尽；较大比例财富也分散在采邑持有者、地方埃米尔与宗教基金手中，中央可直接动员的现金不足。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited in size and could be quickly depleted by Mongol requisitions, court struggles, and wartime emergencies; a large share of wealth was also dispersed among iqta holders, provincial amirs, and religious endowments, leaving the center with insufficient liquid cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持贡赋、军费和最低限度行政运作为先，常通过增税、提前征收和包税弥补赤字，但这进一步削弱农村恢复能力与中央合法性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized tribute, military expenditure, and the bare functioning of government, often covering deficits through increased taxation, advance collection, and tax farming, which further weakened rural recovery and central legitimacy.

description.zh: 由宫廷近卫、采邑骑兵和精锐突厥骑射手组成，是传统塞尔柱军力核心，但在1280年人数和忠诚度均不如盛期。
description.en: Composed of court guards, iqta cavalry, and elite Turkic mounted archers, this was the traditional core of Seljuk military power, though by 1280 both numbers and loyalty were below their former peak.

name.zh: 地方埃米尔与土库曼部族骑兵
name.en: Provincial Amir and Turkmen Tribal Cavalry

description.zh: 依赖地方领主和部族首领征集，机动性强，擅长袭扰和边疆作战，但纪律不稳，常优先服从本地主人而非中央。
description.en: Raised through provincial lords and tribal chiefs, these forces were mobile and adept at raiding and frontier warfare, but unstable in discipline and often more loyal to local patrons than to the center.
