culturalAchievements.en: Goryeo excelled in woodblock printing, Buddhist scripture publication, celadon ceramics, painting, metalwork, and temple architecture. The recently recarved Tripitaka Koreana stood among the greatest achievements of East Asian Buddhist printing, while Goryeo celadon was renowned for its glaze and inlay techniques. Court literature and monastic culture were also highly developed.

languageFamily.zh: 韩语，通常归类为朝鲜语系或孤立语言传统
languageFamily.en: Korean, generally treated as part of the Koreanic family or as a language isolate tradition

gdpDescription.zh: 高丽经济以稻作与旱作农业为基础，辅以手工业、沿海渔盐业、陶瓷业与区域贸易。13世纪蒙古战争造成的人口损失、田地荒废和海防压力尚未完全恢复，因此1280年的总量虽可观，但仍低于和平盛期。经济重心集中在西北与中部农业带、开京及若干港口节点。
gdpDescription.en: The Goryeo economy was based on wet-rice and dry-field agriculture, supported by handicrafts, coastal fishing and salt production, ceramics, and regional trade. Population losses, abandoned fields, and maritime disruption from the Mongol wars had not yet been fully repaired, so output in 1280, while substantial, remained below peak peaceful levels. Economic activity was concentrated in the northwestern and central agrarian belt, Gaegyeong, and selected port nodes.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、青瓷与陶器制造、佛经与书籍雕版印刷、纺织、金属器、造船、渔业、盐业和地方手工业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, celadon and pottery production, woodblock printing of Buddhist and literary texts, textiles, metal goods, shipbuilding, fishing, salt production, and provincial handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 出口与交换品包括青瓷、人参、纸张、金属器、毛皮、纺织品和部分海产；输入品包括丝绸、药材、香料、书籍、奢侈工艺品、马匹及经由元朝网络转运的中亚与中国货物
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included celadon, ginseng, paper, metalware, furs, textiles, and some marine products on the outgoing side; imports included silk, medicines, aromatics, books, luxury manufactures, horses, and goods from China and Central Asia transmitted through Yuan networks

householdWealth.zh: 一般农户财富主要体现在土地使用权、粮食储备、农具、役畜与家中布帛；贵族和寺院则积累大片庄园、奴婢、谷仓、金属器与奢侈消费品。战后社会贫富差距显著扩大。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly held in land-use rights, grain stores, tools, draft animals, and cloth, while aristocrats and monasteries accumulated estates, bonded laborers, granaries, metal goods, and luxury items. Social inequality had widened markedly after the wars.

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导征敛与分配、土地与户籍管理、庄园经济、寺院经济和地方市场并存的前近代农业经济。官方对关键资源、贡赋路线和对外贸易具有较强影响，但实际生产主要由农村家庭与依附劳动力承担。
economicSystem.en: A premodern agrarian economy combining state-directed extraction and allocation, land and household registration, estate production, monastic wealth, and local markets. The court exerted strong influence over key resources, tribute channels, and foreign trade, but actual production rested mainly on rural households and dependent labor.

source.en: Tribute in kind and local product deliveries

source.zh: 役使折纳、杂税与手工业收入
source.en: Commuted labor dues, miscellaneous taxes, and handicraft revenues
Customs, market dues, and trade taxation

category.zh: 对元 obligations、使节与外交支出
category.en: Obligations to Yuan, embassies, and diplomacy

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以谷物、布帛、钱币、金属器和实物库存并存，不完全是单一银本位。由于战争创伤未完全消退，财政更重视维持库存、修复田籍和确保对元关系中的支付能力，而非单纯积聚货币盈余。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was held in mixed form—grain, cloth, coin, metal stores, and goods—rather than as a purely silver fund. Because wartime damage had not fully healed, fiscal policy prioritized maintaining reserves, restoring tax registers, and meeting obligations within the Yuan relationship more than accumulating a large monetary surplus.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体偏稳健保守，以恢复税基、控制宫廷和军费波动、维系对元朝贡与外交成本、并避免对农户征敛过急为目标。国家倾向以实物征收和分配应对货币不足。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally cautious and conservative, aiming to rebuild the tax base, control fluctuations in court and military costs, sustain tribute and diplomatic expenses tied to Yuan relations, and avoid overburdening peasant households. The state relied heavily on in-kind collection and redistribution to cope with limited monetization.

description.zh: 负责宫城、都城与王室安全的常备部队，也是中央最可靠的武装力量之一
description.en: Permanent troops responsible for palace, capital, and royal security, forming one of the most dependable armed elements of the state

description.zh: 由地方编户、乡军和地方官统辖力量构成，战时征发，主要用于守城、治安和区域防御
description.en: Composed of local registrants, rural military households, and provincially managed forces mobilized in wartime, mainly for garrison, policing, and regional defense

description.zh: 数量有限，但在与北方体系接触后更重视骑兵运用，承担侦察、机动支援与边境警戒
description.en: Limited in number, but increasingly valued through contact with northern military systems, serving reconnaissance, mobile support, and frontier watch roles

description.zh: 驻守沿海与岛屿，据点、战船和港湾守备并用，主要防范倭寇、海盗和海上骚扰，并配合元朝海上行动需要
description.en: Stationed along coasts and islands, combining fort posts, warships, and harbor defense to guard against pirates, raiders, and maritime harassment, while also supporting Yuan-related naval needs

commanderInChief.zh: 忠烈王兼最高统帅，实际军务由枢密院与诸将分掌
commanderInChief.en: King Chungnyeol as commander-in-chief, with practical military affairs managed by the Military Secretariat and senior generals

technology.zh: 高丽军队装备以弓弩、长枪、刀剑、甲胄、攻守城器具和战船为主，火药武器尚未形成主导。其技术水平在东亚属中等偏上，海防、筑城和弓术经验较强，但机动骑兵和大规模远征能力逊于蒙古军。
technology.en: Goryeo forces were equipped mainly with bows, crossbows, spears, swords, armor, siege-defense equipment, and warships; gunpowder weapons had not yet become decisive. Their technological level was above average for East Asia, with strong experience in coastal defense, fortification, and archery, but cavalry mobility and long-range expeditionary capability lagged behind Mongol forces.

threats.zh: 主要威胁并非全面外敌入侵，而是元朝要求下的军事动员压力、日本方向的海上行动、沿海治安、地方叛乱余波以及战争后长期的军政重整问题。
threats.en: The main threats were not full-scale foreign invasion so much as military mobilization demands imposed by the Yuan relationship, maritime operations connected to Japan, coastal insecurity, lingering unrest, and the long postwar task of military reorganization.

populationDescription.zh: 高丽在长期蒙古战争后人口仍保持相当规模，但部分地区出现流民、逃户与田地荒废现象。中部与西北部平原人口最为密集，沿海与岛屿地区因战事和海防需要而分布不均。
populationDescription.en: After the long Mongol wars, Goryeo still retained a substantial population, though some regions suffered from displaced people, tax flight, and abandoned farmland. The central and northwestern plains were the most densely settled, while coastal and island zones remained unevenly populated due to warfare and defense needs.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致分为王室与门阀贵族、中央与地方官僚、军人、僧侣、乡吏与地方豪族、平民农户、工商业者，以及奴婢和其他受压迫群体。贵族与寺院控制大量资源，平民承担赋役主体，奴婢制在经济与家内劳动中仍然重要。
socialClasses.en: Social classes broadly included the royal family and aristocratic lineages, central and local officials, military personnel, monks, local clerks and provincial elites, commoner peasants, artisans and merchants, and servile groups including slaves. Aristocrats and monasteries controlled major resources, commoners bore the main tax burdens, and servile labor remained important in both estate and household production.

allies.zh: 无完全对等意义上的正式盟友；最重要的对外关系是与元朝的宗藩与姻亲联系，并通过这一体系与蒙古帝国诸汗国保持间接接触
allies.en: There were no fully equal formal allies in the usual sense; the most important external relationship was the tributary-marital connection with the Yuan, through which Goryeo also had indirect contact with other Mongol successor realms

enemies.zh: 当年并无稳定的公开宿敌，但日本是元朝东征计划中的目标，沿海海盗与倭寇构成现实安全问题；国内亦警惕反元或反王权势力残余
enemies.en: There was no single stable declared enemy in 1280, but Japan stood as the target of Yuan eastern campaigns, while pirates and Japanese raiders posed practical maritime dangers; domestically, remnants of anti-Yuan or anti-royal opposition were also watched

foreignPolicy.zh: 高丽的外交核心是以顺应元朝宗主秩序换取王朝延续、王室安全与国内自治空间。其政策强调谨慎朝贡、联姻、使节往来和有限配合元朝军事需求，同时尽量保护本土制度、贵族利益和行政独立。
foreignPolicy.en: The core of Goryeo foreign policy was to preserve dynastic continuity, royal security, and domestic autonomy by accommodating the Yuan imperial order. Policy emphasized cautious tribute, marriage ties, embassies, and limited cooperation with Yuan military demands, while trying to protect native institutions, aristocratic interests, and administrative independence.

era.zh: 中世纪东亚后期
era.en: High medieval East Asia

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术优势包括高水平雕版印刷、佛经刊刻、精致青瓷烧造、海船建造、堤堰与水利维护、金属工艺和成熟的汉字文书行政技术。金属活字虽尚未在1280年前后形成后世规模，但金属加工能力已相当可观。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological strengths included advanced woodblock printing, large-scale scripture production, refined celadon firing, seagoing ship construction, embankment and irrigation maintenance, metalworking, and a mature documentary administration based on Chinese characters. Metal movable type had not yet developed on its later scale by 1280, but metallurgical capacity was already considerable.

strengths.zh: 高丽在1280年的主要优势是王朝合法性延续、成熟官僚制度、深厚佛教文化、精良手工业、相对稳固的人口基础以及在元朝秩序中获得的生存空间。王室通过与元朝联姻和合作，避免了被直接吞并，同时保住了本国行政与社会结构。
strengths.en: Goryeo’s main strengths in 1280 were dynastic continuity, a mature bureaucratic system, deep Buddhist culture, high-quality handicraft production, a relatively solid demographic base, and room for survival within the Yuan order. Through marriage ties and cooperation with the Yuan, the monarchy avoided direct annexation while preserving native administration and social structure.

weaknesses.zh: 其主要弱点在于对元朝高度依赖、财政税基受战争与庄园化侵蚀、地方控制不均、贵族与寺院特权过强，以及海上与边缘地区防务压力持续存在。政治稳定在很大程度上依赖王室与元廷关系，而非完全自主的战略能力。
weaknesses.en: Its chief weaknesses lay in heavy dependence on the Yuan, a fiscal base eroded by war and estate expansion, uneven local control, overly strong aristocratic and monastic privileges, and continuing pressure on coastal and peripheral defense. Political stability depended largely on the royal court’s relationship with the Yuan rather than on fully independent strategic capacity.

outlook.zh: 短期前景总体稳定。只要元朝维持对高丽王室的支持，高丽将继续以附属而自治的方式存在，并逐步修复战后经济与行政。但若元朝要求加重、东征日本再启或宫廷派系斗争加深，高丽的财政和社会承受力将迅速受压。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was broadly stable. As long as the Yuan continued to support the Goryeo royal house, the kingdom would likely persist as a subordinate yet self-governing realm and gradually repair its postwar economy and administration. However, heavier Yuan demands, renewed campaigns against Japan, or intensified court factionalism could quickly strain its finances and society.

description.zh: 朝鲜半岛王国，在蒙古影响下保持王朝延续与内部自治。
description.en: The Korean kingdom maintained dynastic continuity and internal autonomy under Mongol influence.

governmentForm.zh: 以苏丹为核心的波斯化伊斯兰军事君主制，依靠宫廷奴隶军官、突厥贵族与省级伊克塔持有者治理
governmentForm.en: A Persianate Islamic military monarchy centered on the sultan, governed through court slave-officers, Turkic nobles, and provincial iqta holders

socialStructure.zh: 社会分层鲜明，宫廷突厥与阿富汗军事贵族居上，乌里玛、文书官僚与城市商人次之，广大农村由穆斯林与印度教农民、工匠及地方头人构成，并包含大量受征服地区的地方精英
socialStructure.en: Society was sharply stratified, with Turkic and Afghan military nobles at the top, followed by ulama, scribal bureaucrats, and urban merchants, while the vast countryside consisted of Muslim and Hindu peasants, artisans, and local headmen, including many incorporated local elites from conquered regions

rulingClass.zh: 苏丹、宫廷马木鲁克出身军政贵族、突厥埃米尔、波斯文官与伊斯兰法学者
rulingClass.en: The sultan, court Mamluk military-administrative nobles, Turkic amirs, Persian bureaucrats, and Islamic jurists

succession.zh: 无严格长子继承制；王位通常由苏丹指定或由宫廷军政精英支持的宗室成员继承，常伴随权力斗争
succession.en: There was no strict primogeniture; the throne usually passed to a dynasty member designated by the sultan or backed by court military elites, often amid power struggles

structure.zh: 中央政府围绕苏丹宫廷运作，由维齐尔统筹财政与政务，军务、司法、宗教与文书机构分工明确；地方通过伊克塔授封制度由穆克提、总督和地方军官执行征税与治安
structure.en: The central government functioned around the sultan's court, with the vizier coordinating finance and administration, while military, judicial, religious, and chancery offices had distinct roles; the provinces were managed through the iqta assignment system by muqtis, governors, and local officers responsible for taxation and order

function.zh: 主管财政、土地收入、支出审计与中央行政协调
function.en: Managed finance, land revenue, expenditure auditing, and central administrative coordination

function.zh: 负责军队编制、军马检阅、军饷与边防部署
function.en: Oversaw army organization, horse inspection, pay, and frontier deployment

function.zh: 起草诏令、外交文书、档案与官方通信
function.en: Drafted decrees, diplomatic correspondence, archives, and official communications

function.zh: 管理宗教捐产、法官任命、伊斯兰教育与司法事务
function.en: Handled religious endowments, judicial appointments, Islamic education, and legal affairs

function.zh: 负责王室内廷、礼仪、禁卫与巴尔班特别重视的密探网络
function.en: Managed the royal household, ceremonial protocol, guards, and the spy network especially emphasized by Balban

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以伊克塔为核心，省区由穆克提或总督统辖，下设希克、帕尔加纳与村社层级；村庄仍依靠本地头人、税吏与习惯权威运作，中央通过巡察、情报与定期调任限制地方割据
localAdmin.en: Local administration was centered on iqta assignments, with provinces under muqtis or governors and subordinate shiqs, parganas, and village communities; villages continued to function through local headmen, revenue agents, and customary authority, while the center used inspection, intelligence, and periodic transfers to limit provincial separatism

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合伊斯兰教法、苏丹敕令与地方习惯法。城市与穆斯林事务多由卡迪依据哈乃斐法学审理，涉及非穆斯林社群和乡村财产时则常兼顾既有习惯与行政裁断
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Islamic law, sultanic decrees, and local customary law. Urban and Muslim affairs were commonly judged by qadis under Hanafi jurisprudence, while non-Muslim communities and rural property matters were often handled with attention to existing custom and administrative discretion

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以土地税为主，辅以贡赋、市场税、关税、牧税、战利品分成和对部分非穆斯林征收的人头税；伊克塔持有者代征收入以供养军队与地方治理，但巴尔班加强中央监督以防侵吞
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied chiefly on land revenue, supplemented by tribute, market taxes, customs duties, grazing taxes, shares of booty, and poll taxes on some non-Muslim subjects; iqta holders collected revenue to maintain troops and local administration, but Balban strengthened central oversight to curb embezzlement

religion.zh: 国家主导宗教为逊尼派伊斯兰教，但境内人口绝大多数仍为印度教徒，另有耆那教、佛教残余与苏菲教团活动；宫廷提倡伊斯兰正统与王权威严，同时在实际治理中容纳多宗教社会
religion.en: The state religion was Sunni Islam, but the great majority of the population remained Hindu, alongside Jains, residual Buddhist communities, and active Sufi orders; the court promoted Islamic orthodoxy and royal majesty while in practice governing a multi-religious society

philosophy.zh: 思想上受波斯政治伦理、伊斯兰法学与苏菲神秘主义影响，同时与印度本土的王权观、种姓秩序和地方宗教传统长期互动
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by Persian political ethics, Islamic jurisprudence, and Sufi mysticism, while continually interacting with indigenous Indian ideas of kingship, caste order, and regional religious traditions

writingSystem.zh: 官方书写语言主要为波斯语，宗教学术使用阿拉伯语；地方社会广泛使用天城文、沙拉达文及其他印度书写体系记录文书、碑铭与商业活动
writingSystem.en: Persian served as the main language of government writing, while Arabic was used for religion and scholarship; local society widely used Devanagari, Sharada, and other Indian scripts for records, inscriptions, and commercial activity

culturalAchievements.zh: 德里宫廷巩固了波斯化政治文化在北印度的地位，建筑上发展清真寺、城堡、拱券与早期印度—伊斯兰风格，宫廷礼仪、史书写作与苏菲网络亦不断扩展；巴尔班时期尤其强调王权神圣距离、朝仪与宫廷纪律
culturalAchievements.en: The Delhi court consolidated the place of Persianate political culture in North India, while architecture developed mosques, forts, arches, and an early Indo-Islamic style; court ceremonial, historical writing, and Sufi networks also expanded, and Balban's reign especially stressed sacred royal distance, protocol, and court discipline

languageFamily.zh: 统治精英语言属伊朗语族与突厥语族传统，民间主体为印度-雅利安语支诸语言，如早期印地地区方言、旁遮普语与孟加拉地区语言
languageFamily.en: Elite languages belonged to Iranian and Turkic traditions, while the population mainly spoke Indo-Aryan languages, including early Hindi-area dialects, Punjabi, and languages of Bengal

unit.en: million Delhi silver tankas annual value
Delhi silver tankas per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础坚实，依赖高产农业、恒河平原城镇网络、手工业与区域贸易。与宋末元初中国相比人均产出不算突出，但总量在伊斯兰东方诸国中属可观规模，足以支撑大军、宫廷和边防体系
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on strong agrarian production, the town network of the Gangetic plain, handicrafts, and regional trade. Per capita output was not exceptional compared with late Song and early Yuan China, but total output was substantial among the Islamic states of the East and sufficient to support a large army, court, and frontier defense system

mainIndustries.zh: 粮食种植、棉纺织、靛蓝、制糖、金属加工、马匹贸易、建筑业、皮革、陶器与区域商贸
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, cotton textiles, indigo, sugar production, metalworking, horse trade, construction, leatherwork, pottery, and regional commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 出口棉布、糖、靛蓝、香料转口、手工制品与宝石；进口中亚及伊朗马匹、优质金属器、奢侈织物、书籍、香料与部分军需品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included cotton cloth, sugar, indigo, re-exported spices, crafted goods, and gemstones; imports included horses from Central Asia and Iran, fine metalware, luxury textiles, books, aromatics, and some military supplies

householdWealth.zh: 农村家庭财富多体现为耕畜、种子粮、农具与村社关系，城市家庭则拥有住房、库存、作坊工具与流通银币。贫富差距明显，贵族与大伊克塔持有者财富高度集中
householdWealth.en: Household wealth in the countryside was mainly embodied in draft animals, seed grain, tools, and village ties, while urban households held dwellings, inventories, workshop equipment, and circulating silver coin. Wealth inequality was marked, with great concentration among nobles and major iqta holders

unit.en: Delhi silver tankas per person per year
million Delhi silver tankas annual value

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租和国家军事财政为核心的前近代混合经济，国家通过税收、伊克塔和市场监管抽取剩余，商业则由穆斯林、耆那与印度教商人共同推动
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy centered on agrarian surplus and a military-fiscal state, in which the government extracted revenue through taxation, iqta assignments, and market oversight, while commerce was driven jointly by Muslim, Jain, and Hindu merchants

source.en: Customs, market dues, and commercial taxes

category.en: Army, frontier defense, and military pay

treasuryDescription.zh: 巴尔班治下国库较为充实，但仍需应对边防驻军、宫廷赏赐与突发军事动员。储备包括银币、贵金属、实物粮储及可迅速征调的伊克塔收入
treasuryDescription.en: Under Balban the treasury was relatively sound, though it still had to meet frontier garrisons, court patronage, and sudden military mobilization. Reserves included silver coin, bullion, grain stocks, and iqta revenues that could be rapidly called upon

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策趋于谨慎和集权，强调恢复中央权威、整顿伊克塔、严控贵族侵占与保障军费；目标不是大规模经济改革，而是稳定税源与维持王朝安全
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was cautious and centralizing, emphasizing the restoration of central authority, tighter control over iqta assignments, curbing noble encroachment, and safeguarding military finance; the goal was not sweeping economic reform but stable revenues and dynastic security

description.zh: 由突厥、阿富汗及宫廷奴隶军官统领的精锐骑兵，是对抗草原袭扰和镇压叛乱的核心力量
description.en: Elite cavalry led by Turkic, Afghan, and court slave officers, forming the core force for resisting steppe incursions and suppressing rebellions

description.zh: 由各地穆克提和封授军官维持，战时集结，承担地方守备、征讨与增援边疆任务
description.en: Maintained by provincial muqtis and military grantees, assembled in wartime, and used for local defense, campaigns, and reinforcement of the frontier

description.zh: 主要用于攻城、守城、营地警戒与后勤护卫，战斗力不如精锐骑兵但数量重要
description.en: Used mainly for siege work, garrison defense, camp security, and logistical escort; less formidable than elite cavalry but important in numbers

description.zh: 用于震慑、冲击与仪式展示，在印度战场仍具战术和政治象征意义
description.en: Used for shock, intimidation, and ceremonial display, retaining tactical and political significance on Indian battlefields

description.zh: 部署于旁遮普、木尔坦、萨曼纳等前线据点，以监视蒙古动向并拖延入侵
description.en: Stationed at frontier points such as Punjab, Multan, and Samana to monitor Mongol movements and delay invasions

commanderInChief.zh: 苏丹吉亚斯·乌德·丁·巴尔班

technology.zh: 军技术以复合弓骑兵、锁甲与札甲、长枪骑兵、攻城器械、坚固要塞体系和战象运用为主，尚未形成火药武器的实战体系；对草原战争经验的吸收使其重视机动侦察和边防纵深
technology.en: Military technology centered on composite-bow cavalry, mail and lamellar armor, lance cavalry, siege engines, fortified strongholds, and the use of war elephants, without yet a developed gunpowder battlefield system; experience with steppe warfare encouraged mobility, reconnaissance, and defense in depth on the frontier

threats.zh: 最大外部威胁来自蒙古诸汗国势力对旁遮普和西北边境的压力；内部则有孟加拉、道阿卜与拉杰普特地区的离心倾向，以及贵族叛乱和边将坐大风险
threats.en: The greatest external threat came from Mongol pressure on Punjab and the northwestern frontier; internally, centrifugal tendencies in Bengal, the Doab, and Rajput areas, along with noble rebellions and overmighty frontier commanders, posed continuing dangers

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于恒河—亚穆纳河流域、旁遮普与北印度肥沃农业区。多数居民为农村农业人口，都市人口虽占比较低，但德里及若干区域中心已具有显著政治和商业功能
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly in the Ganges-Yamuna basin, Punjab, and the fertile agrarian regions of North India. Most inhabitants were rural agricultural producers, while the urban share was modest though Delhi and several regional centers had major political and commercial importance

socialClasses.zh: 上层为苏丹家族、军事贵族、波斯文官与宗教学者；中间层包括商人、城镇工匠、书吏和地方地主；下层主体为农民、佃户、劳役者与城市贫民。印度教种姓结构在乡村社会继续存在，并与苏丹国军政等级并行交织
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the sultan's family, military nobles, Persian bureaucrats, and religious scholars; middle groups included merchants, urban artisans, scribes, and local landholders; the lower orders were mainly peasants, tenants, laborers, and the urban poor. Hindu caste structures continued in village society and coexisted with the sultanate's military-administrative hierarchy

allies.zh: 德里苏丹国在1280年并无稳固意义上的正式大国盟友，更多依靠与西北边境部族、附庸拉杰普特首领及部分地方穆斯林统治者的临时合作
allies.en: In 1280 the Delhi Sultanate had no major formal allies in a stable sense, relying instead on temporary cooperation with northwestern frontier groups, subordinate Rajput chiefs, and certain regional Muslim rulers

enemies.zh: 主要敌手是蒙古势力及其西北游牧军事压力；在印度次大陆内部，则与孟加拉离心势力、拉杰普特据点以及德干诸王国存在竞争或敌对关系
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were Mongol forces and the military pressure of the northwestern steppe; within the subcontinent it also faced rivals or enemies among separatist elements in Bengal, Rajput strongholds, and Deccan kingdoms

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以守住西北边防、巩固恒河平原控制、压制地方离叛并维持对伊斯兰世界的象征性合法联系为主。巴尔班总体上采取谨慎防御而非远征扩张的方针
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on holding the northwestern frontier, consolidating control of the Gangetic plain, suppressing provincial defiance, and maintaining symbolic legitimacy within the wider Islamic world. Balban generally followed a cautious defensive line rather than ambitious long-distance expansion

era.zh: 中古后期的印度—伊斯兰军事与城市化时代
era.en: Late medieval Indo-Islamic military and urban age

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力不在单一发明，而在波斯化官僚文书、伊克塔军政体制、骑射与重骑兵结合、石造拱券建筑技术、城市要塞化以及跨区域商路整合
keyInnovations.en: Its key strengths lay not in a single invention but in Persianate bureaucratic documentation, the iqta military-administrative system, the combination of horse archery and heavy cavalry, stone arch architecture, urban fortification, and the integration of long-distance trade routes

strengths.zh: 德里苏丹国在1280年拥有北印度最强的军事财政国家结构之一，首都资源集中、宫廷纪律强化、骑兵与边防体系较成熟，且控制了高产农业腹地与关键商路
strengths.en: In 1280 the Delhi Sultanate possessed one of the strongest military-fiscal state structures in North India, with concentrated capital resources, strengthened court discipline, a relatively mature cavalry and frontier system, and control over productive agrarian heartlands and key trade routes

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于统治阶层规模狭窄、对军政贵族忠诚依赖过深、地方整合不均、宗教与社会差异明显，以及西北长期面临蒙古威胁导致财政和兵力持续紧张
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in the narrow size of the ruling elite, deep dependence on the loyalty of military nobles, uneven provincial integration, marked religious and social differences, and the chronic strain imposed by Mongol threats on the northwest

outlook.zh: 短期内，若巴尔班继续维持强硬中央集权与边防警戒，苏丹国仍可保持兴盛并扩大对北印度的实际控制；但中长期稳定取决于继承是否平顺、贵族是否受控以及能否把征服结构转化为更持久的行政秩序
outlook.en: In the short term, if Balban continued to maintain harsh central authority and vigilant frontier defense, the sultanate could remain prosperous and expand effective control across North India; over the longer term, stability depended on smooth succession, discipline over the nobles, and the ability to turn a conquest regime into a more durable administrative order

description.zh: 北印度最强的伊斯兰国家，巩固恒河平原并防御蒙古压力。
description.en: The strongest Islamic state in North India, consolidating the Gangetic plain while resisting Mongol pressure.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为核心、依赖诸侯封臣、地方总督与婆罗门文官共同运作的印度教世袭王国
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Hindu monarchy centered on the king and administered through feudatories, provincial governors, and Brahmin officials

socialStructure.zh: 社会以种姓秩序、土地控制与寺庙网络为基础；王室与武士贵族居上，婆罗门掌礼仪与文书，商人与手工业者活跃于城镇，广大农民与牧民构成基层社会。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around caste hierarchy, landholding, and temple networks; the royal house and warrior nobility stood at the top, Brahmins dominated ritual and administration, merchants and artisans were active in towns, and peasants and pastoralists formed the social base.

rulingClass.zh: 雅达瓦王室、地方封臣、军功贵族、婆罗门行政精英与寺庙地主集团
rulingClass.en: The Yadava royal family, regional feudatories, military nobles, Brahmin administrative elites, and temple landholding interests

succession.zh: 王位通常在王室男性成员间世袭传承，以长子优先为原则，但亦受宫廷拥立、军事力量与贵族支持影响。
succession.en: Succession was generally hereditary within the royal male line, with a preference for the eldest son, though court support, military strength, and noble backing also shaped outcomes.

structure.zh: 中央政府以国王及其宫廷为核心，下设财政、军务、司法、王室文书与宗教施惠等职能；地方由省级长官、要塞司令、封臣与村社共同治理。
structure.en: The central government revolved around the king and court, with functions for finance, military affairs, justice, royal chancery, and religious patronage; the countryside was governed through provincial officers, fort commanders, feudatories, and village communities.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保存铭文与地契、管理对地方与封臣的文书往来
function.en: Drafted royal orders, preserved inscriptions and land grants, and managed correspondence with provinces and feudatories

function.zh: 征收土地税、关税、商税与附加贡赋，监督仓储与王室收入
function.en: Collected land revenue, customs, trade taxes, and extra levies, and supervised granaries and royal income

function.zh: 统筹王家军队、马匹与象队供给，维护要塞驻军与边境防务
function.en: Coordinated royal forces, horse and elephant supply, and maintained garrisons and frontier defense

function.zh: 处理土地纠纷、刑事案件、地方申诉并监督官员行为
function.en: Handled land disputes, criminal cases, provincial petitions, and oversight of officials

name.zh: 寺庙、施舍与公共工程署
name.en: Temples, Endowments, and Public Works Department

function.zh: 管理寺庙赐田、宗教捐赠、水利设施、道路与部分市场建设
function.en: Managed temple grants, religious endowments, irrigation works, roads, and some market construction

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依靠省、县、堡垒辖区与村社层级展开。王国通过任命总督、税吏和堡主控制战略地区，同时容许地方封臣与村社长老在税收、治安和灌溉方面维持相当自治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through provinces, districts, fort jurisdictions, and village communities. The kingdom controlled strategic areas by appointing governors, revenue officers, and fort commanders, while allowing local feudatories and village elders considerable autonomy in taxation, order, and irrigation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合王室敕令、达摩法传统、地方习惯法与村社仲裁。土地、婚姻和继承多受婆罗门法学解释影响，刑事处罚则由国王、地方长官和军事首领共同执行。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal edicts, dharmashastra tradition, local customary law, and village arbitration. Land, marriage, and inheritance were strongly shaped by Brahmin legal interpretation, while criminal punishments were enforced by the king, local officers, and military chiefs.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家财政以土地税为主，并征收灌溉税、商税、关卡税、手工业税及非常时期征发。部分地区通过封臣缴纳贡赋，寺庙赐田则常享有不同程度减免。
taxationSystem.en: State finance rested primarily on land revenue, supplemented by irrigation dues, trade taxes, transit tolls, craft taxes, and extraordinary levies. Some regions paid tribute through feudatories, while temple lands often enjoyed partial exemptions.

religion.zh: 王国以印度教为主，特别尊奉湿婆、毗湿奴及其地方化形态，同时耆那教在商人和部分精英中仍具影响，民间亦广泛存在乡土神祇崇拜。
religion.en: The kingdom was predominantly Hindu, especially devoted to Shiva, Vishnu, and their regional forms, while Jainism retained influence among merchants and some elites, and local deity worship remained widespread.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以婆罗门教法、吠檀多、湿婆派与毗湿奴派神学为主，并与耆那教伦理及地区性奉爱传统交织。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by Brahmanical law, Vedanta, Shaiva and Vaishnava theology, intertwined with Jain ethics and emerging regional bhakti traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 官方和学术书写主要使用梵文与马拉地语，文字以天城体及其地区变体、卡纳达书体和行政用文书体并行。
writingSystem.en: Official and scholarly writing primarily used Sanskrit and Marathi, written in Nagari and regional variants alongside Kannada-influenced and administrative documentary scripts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 塞乌纳时期是早期马拉地文学成长的重要阶段，宫廷和寺庙赞助诗歌、铭文和宗教著述；建筑上以石制寺庙、堡垒工程与后世称作“黑马盘提式”的行政和宗教建筑传统著称。
culturalAchievements.en: The Seuna period marked an important phase in the growth of early Marathi literature, with court and temple patronage for poetry, inscriptions, and religious writing; architecturally it was noted for stone temples, fortification works, and the administrative and religious building tradition later associated with the Hemadpanti style.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属于印欧语系印度-雅利安语支中的马拉地语，宫廷学术语言梵文同属印欧语系；南部边境亦有达罗毗荼语影响。
languageFamily.en: The chief vernacular belonged to the Indo-European Indo-Aryan branch as Marathi, while the courtly scholarly language Sanskrit was also Indo-European; Dravidian linguistic influence was present along the southern frontier.

gdpDescription.zh: 王国经济以农业剩余为基础，辅以内陆贸易、畜牧、手工业和区域转运。提婆吉里控制德干高原西部要道，使其在南北与东西向贸易中具重要地位。
gdpDescription.en: The kingdom’s economy rested on agricultural surplus, supplemented by inland trade, pastoralism, crafts, and regional transit. Devagiri controlled key routes in the western Deccan, giving it an important role in both north-south and east-west commerce.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、棉花与油料作物、畜牧、纺织、金属加工、寺庙建筑、盐与区域转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, cotton and oilseed farming, pastoralism, textiles, metalworking, temple construction, salt distribution, and regional transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、原棉、粮食、酥油、马匹转售、金属器、染料、宝石、糖、胡椒与从沿海输入的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, raw cotton, grain, ghee, re-sold horses, metalware, dyes, gemstones, sugar, pepper, and luxury goods brought inland from the coasts

name.zh: 加迪亚纳与地方银铜币并用
name.en: Gadyana with local silver and copper coinages

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为耕地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备与家庭器具；城镇商人和寺庙机构拥有更高流动财富、贵金属与信贷能力。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in cultivation rights, livestock, grain stores, and domestic goods; urban merchants and temple institutions possessed greater liquid wealth, precious metals, and credit capacity.

economicSystem.zh: 这是一个以农业税基为核心、由王室再分配、封臣义务、寺庙经济和商人网络共同支撑的前近代区域经济体系。
economicSystem.en: This was a premodern regional economy centered on agrarian taxation, supported by royal redistribution, feudatory obligations, temple finance, and merchant networks.

source.en: Customs, trade taxes, and transit tolls
Tribute from feudatories and subordinate chiefs

source.zh: 王室地产、矿冶与杂项收入
source.en: Royal estates, mines, metalwork, and miscellaneous income

category.zh: 寺庙捐助、宗教施舍与文化赞助
category.en: Temple patronage, religious charity, and cultural support

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况稳健，依靠积累的贵金属、谷物储备、战时动员能力与王室控制的要塞库藏支撑。财富并非完全货币化，部分以粮储、牲畜和受控贡赋形式存在。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was sound, supported by accumulated precious metals, grain reserves, wartime mobilization capacity, and stores held in royal forts. Wealth was not fully monetized, with a significant share existing as grain stocks, livestock, and controlled tribute.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体审慎，偏向维持稳定税基、保护内陆商路并通过宗教施舍和封赏巩固统治；在战争或边防压力下则会临时加征。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, aimed at preserving a stable tax base, protecting inland trade routes, and reinforcing legitimacy through religious grants and patronage; temporary extractions were imposed under military or frontier pressure.

description.zh: 骑兵是机动作战核心，包含王家亲兵、封臣骑士与雇佣骑手，依赖从西海岸和印度洋贸易输入的优质战马。
description.en: Cavalry formed the mobile arm of war, including royal retainers, feudatory horsemen, and hired riders, dependent on quality warhorses imported through west-coast and Indian Ocean trade.

description.zh: 步兵由长矛手、盾兵、弓箭手与地方征召兵组成，承担守城、野战支援和围攻任务，是军队人数主体。
description.en: Infantry consisted of spearmen, shield-bearers, archers, and locally levied troops, serving in garrison duty, battlefield support, and sieges, and made up the largest part of the army.

description.zh: 战象用于震慑、冲击和指挥象征，虽数量有限，但在仪式与战场上都具高度威望。
description.en: War elephants were used for shock, intimidation, and command prestige; though limited in number, they carried great symbolic and battlefield importance.

description.zh: 分布于提婆吉里及各山地、边境要塞，负责储备、侦察、道路控制和长期防御，是德干山堡体系的核心。
description.en: Stationed in Devagiri and other hill and frontier forts, they handled stores, reconnaissance, route control, and long-term defense, forming the backbone of the Deccan hill-fort system.

commanderInChief.zh: 罗摩旃陀罗大王

technology.zh: 军备以传统印度中世纪兵器为主，包括弓、矛、剑、盾、锁甲和攻城器械；要塞工程尤其发达，山地设防与储备体系是王国防御优势。
technology.en: Military technology relied on standard Indian medieval arms including bows, spears, swords, shields, mail, and siege equipment; fortification engineering was especially advanced, and hill defenses with reserve storage were a major strategic strength.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方扩张中的德里苏丹国，以及与卡卡提耶、曷萨拉和潘地亚等南印强权的地区竞争；更广阔的蒙古时代贸易与马匹供给波动也间接影响其安全。
threats.en: The chief threat came from the expanding Delhi Sultanate to the north, along with regional rivalry involving the Kakatiyas, Hoysalas, and Pandyas; wider Mongol-era disruptions in trade and horse supply also indirectly affected security.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于马哈拉施特拉高原、河谷农业区和交通节点附近，乡村人口占绝对多数，城市与堡垒聚落则承担行政、贸易和军事功能。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Maharashtra plateau, riverine farming zones, and transport nodes, with an overwhelming rural majority and urban-fortified settlements serving administrative, commercial, and military roles.

name.zh: 科尔哈普尔地区城镇群

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室、贵族武士、婆罗门、寺庙人员、商人、手工业者、村社农民、牧民和从属劳役群体；不同地区的种姓与职业结构并不完全一致。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the royal house, warrior nobles, Brahmins, temple personnel, merchants, artisans, village peasants, pastoralists, and subordinated laboring groups; caste and occupational structures varied by region.

allies.zh: 王国没有长期固定意义上的现代盟友，更多依靠与地方封臣、商人团体及南印诸国之间不断调整的婚姻、停战与权宜合作关系。
allies.en: The kingdom had no permanent modern-style allies, relying instead on shifting marriages, truces, and pragmatic cooperation with feudatories, merchant bodies, and neighboring southern Indian powers.

enemies.zh: 最重要的潜在敌手是德里苏丹国；同时与卡卡提耶王国、曷萨拉王朝及其他德干和南印势力存在边界与影响力竞争。
enemies.en: The most important potential enemy was the Delhi Sultanate; the kingdom also faced border and influence rivalries with the Kakatiya kingdom, the Hoysala dynasty, and other Deccan and South Indian powers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制德干内陆交通、维持边境均势、保障商路和马匹供给为重点；在远距离层面上，其与蒙古诸汗国并无直接外交主轴，更多通过印度洋与陆上商路受到间接影响。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling inland Deccan routes, maintaining border balance, and securing trade corridors and horse supply; at longer range, it had no direct diplomatic axis with the Mongol khanates and was influenced mainly through Indian Ocean and overland commercial connections.

era.zh: 中古晚期印度德干时代，处于蒙古帝国时代的欧亚交流背景之下
era.en: Late medieval Deccan India within the wider Eurasian context of the Mongol Empire era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键优势在于山地要塞工程、蓄水与灌溉建设、石制寺庙与行政建筑技术、区域道路管理，以及马拉地语行政书写和地方财政记录的发展。
keyInnovations.en: Key strengths included hill-fort engineering, water storage and irrigation works, stone temple and administrative construction, regional road management, and the development of Marathi administrative writing and local fiscal record-keeping.

strengths.zh: 塞乌纳王国在1280年仍属稳定强国，拥有坚固都城、较充足财政、重要商路控制力和成熟的地方统治网络。其地处德干核心，能够从农业、贸易和堡垒防御中获得复合优势。
strengths.en: In 1280 the Seuna Kingdom remained a stable major power, with a formidable capital, solid finances, control over important trade routes, and a mature local governing network. Its position in the Deccan core gave it combined advantages in agriculture, commerce, and fortified defense.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于封臣体系分散、骑兵高度依赖外来马匹、地方自治较强而中央穿透力有限；面对德里苏丹国那类更具机动性和财政军事整合能力的北方政权时，长期抗衡存在隐患。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in a decentralized feudatory structure, heavy dependence on imported horses for cavalry, and limited central penetration over autonomous local powers; these posed long-term problems when facing northern states like the Delhi Sultanate with stronger mobility and tighter fiscal-military integration.

outlook.zh: 短期内王国前景仍然稳固，但北方伊斯兰政权持续南进将显著改变力量平衡。若无法强化中央军政与边境机动能力，塞乌纳王国在未来数十年将面临严峻挑战。
outlook.en: In the short term the kingdom’s outlook remained solid, but continued southward expansion by northern Islamic powers would significantly alter the balance of power. Unless it strengthened central military-administrative capacity and frontier mobility, the Seuna Kingdom would face severe challenges in the coming decades.

description.zh: 德干西部的强大印度教王国，控制重要内陆商路。
description.en: A powerful Hindu kingdom in western Deccan controlling major inland trade routes.

dynasty.zh: 镰仓幕府（北条摄政主导）

governmentForm.zh: 以武家幕府为核心、天皇朝廷与幕府并存的封建军政体制；实权集中于北条氏执权
governmentForm.en: A feudal warrior government centered on the shogunate, coexisting with the imperial court; real power was concentrated in the Hōjō regency

socialStructure.zh: 社会分层以天皇与公家、将军与御家人武士、寺社势力、地方名主与百姓、工匠商人为主，另有被边缘化的贱民群体。武士阶层在政治与军事上占据主导，但庄园领主、寺社和朝廷仍保有重要权威。
socialStructure.en: Society was layered around the emperor and court nobility, the shogun and vassal warriors, powerful temples and shrines, local estate holders and peasants, artisans and merchants, with marginalized outcaste groups as well. The warrior class dominated politics and war, but estate lords, religious institutions, and the court still retained major authority.

rulingClass.zh: 北条氏执权家、镰仓幕府高级御家人、守护与地头，以及部分京都公家与大寺社领袖
rulingClass.en: The Hōjō regent house, senior Kamakura vassals, provincial military governors and estate stewards, together with selected Kyoto courtiers and leaders of major temples and shrines

succession.zh: 名义上将军由朝廷任命，幕府职位多由源氏或皇族将军担任；实际政权由北条氏通过执权、连署与得宗家内部继承控制，地方领地则依武家家督继承与分封惯例传承
succession.en: Formally, the shogun was appointed by the imperial court, and the office was held by Minamoto or princely figures; in practice, power was controlled by the Hōjō through the regency, co-signatory offices, and succession within the Tokusō line, while provincial holdings passed through warrior house inheritance and partition customs

structure.zh: 日本在1280年由京都朝廷与镰仓幕府构成双重政权。朝廷保有礼仪、官位与法统，幕府掌握军事、司法和大部分地方统治。幕府中以执权为核心，辅以连署、评定众、引付众等机构，统辖御家人、守护、地头，并通过文书系统与使节网络向各地发布命令。
structure.en: In 1280 Japan was governed through a dual structure of the Kyoto court and the Kamakura bakufu. The court retained ritual authority, titles, and dynastic legitimacy, while the bakufu controlled military affairs, justice, and most provincial governance. Within the bakufu, the regent stood at the center, assisted by the co-signatory, the Council of State, and the High Tribunal, supervising vassals, provincial constables, and estate stewards through documentary administration and messenger networks.

function.zh: 处理幕府政务、财政文书、人事与日常行政
function.en: Handled bakufu administration, fiscal records, appointments, and routine governance

function.zh: 统辖御家人军务、警备、京镰治安与动员
function.en: Supervised vassal military affairs, guard duties, public order, and mobilization

function.zh: 审理诉讼、土地纠纷与文书核验
function.en: Reviewed lawsuits, land disputes, and documentary claims

function.zh: 进行高级政策裁决、司法复核与重要案件审议
function.en: Conducted high-level policy deliberation, judicial review, and major case adjudication

function.zh: 驻京都监察朝廷、西国武士与畿内治安
function.en: Stationed in Kyoto to monitor the court, western warriors, and security in the Kinai

function.zh: 统筹九州防务、边警与对高丽及元朝相关情报
function.en: Coordinated the defense of Kyushu, frontier alerts, and intelligence related to Goryeo and the Yuan

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托守护与地头体系。守护负责一国范围内的军警、追捕和动员，地头负责庄园与公领的征收、管理和治安。传统国司制度名义上仍存，但实际地方控制多掌握在幕府任命或承认的武士手中。偏远地区如东北与九州边缘地带，地方豪族自治色彩更强。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on the shugo and jitō system. Provincial constables handled policing, military mobilization, and pursuit of offenders across provinces, while estate stewards managed collection, local order, and estate oversight on shōen and public lands. The older civil governorship system nominally remained, but real local control was largely in the hands of warriors appointed or recognized by the bakufu. In frontier regions such as the northeast and outer Kyushu, local magnates retained stronger autonomous features.

legalSystem.zh: 法律秩序由朝廷律令传统、庄园惯例与武家法共同构成。1232年的《御成败式目》是幕府司法核心，重视土地权属、继承、御家人义务与诉讼证据。判决通常依文书、先例、证言与武家习法作出，强调维持秩序而非系统法典化。
legalSystem.en: The legal order combined court ritsuryō traditions, estate custom, and warrior law. The 1232 Goseibai Shikimoku was the judicial core of the bakufu, focusing on land tenure, inheritance, vassal obligations, and evidentiary procedure. Judgments were usually based on documents, precedent, testimony, and warrior custom, emphasizing social order rather than a fully systematic code.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收并非由单一中央税制统一征集，而是由庄园年贡、公领租税、劳役、通行征收、寺社收入与临时军役分担组成。蒙古来袭后，九州沿海防务导致额外的人力、物资与修筑石垣负担。幕府对御家人的直接财政汲取有限，更多依靠领地控制与非常动员。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was not collected through a single centralized fiscal system but through estate dues, public-land taxes, labor obligations, transit levies, religious revenues, and extraordinary military burdens. After the Mongol invasions, coastal defense in Kyushu imposed additional requirements in manpower, provisions, and stone-wall construction. The bakufu had limited direct fiscal extraction from its vassals and relied more on control of land rights and emergency mobilization.

religion.zh: 宗教生活以神佛习合为主，天台宗、真言宗、净土宗、净土真宗的早期发展、禅宗与日莲宗并行。八幡信仰、伊势神宫崇拜和地方神社网络深植社会。蒙古袭来后，神国观念与祈祷仪式更受重视。
religion.en: Religious life was dominated by shinbutsu shūgō, the blending of kami worship and Buddhism. Tendai, Shingon, Pure Land, early True Pure Land developments, Zen, and Nichiren traditions all coexisted. Hachiman devotion, reverence for Ise Shrine, and local shrine networks were deeply embedded in society. After the Mongol attacks, ideas of Japan as a divinely protected land and ritual prayers gained greater prominence.

philosophy.zh: 思想上融合佛教业报与无常观、儒家等级伦理以及武士重视忠义、名誉和家职的实践价值。禅宗在部分武士与知识阶层中影响上升，但尚未完全主导全国武家文化。
philosophy.en: Thought blended Buddhist ideas of karma and impermanence, Confucian hierarchical ethics, and a warrior ethos that valued loyalty, honor, and hereditary duty. Zen was gaining influence among some warriors and intellectual circles, though it had not yet fully defined warrior culture nationwide.

writingSystem.zh: 书写系统以汉字和假名并用。正式公文、寺社记录与学术著述多用汉文或和汉混淆文，文学、日记与私人通信广泛使用平假名与片假名。
writingSystem.en: The writing system used Chinese characters together with kana. Formal documents, temple records, and scholarly texts were often written in classical Chinese or mixed Sino-Japanese styles, while literature, diaries, and private correspondence made broad use of hiragana and katakana.

culturalAchievements.zh: 这一时期延续院政末以来的文学与宗教文化，并形成明显的武家时代特征。和歌、日记文学、军记物语和说话集流行，绘卷与佛教美术繁荣，镰仓雕刻写实有力。东大寺重建、禅宗寺院发展、武家礼法与甲胄工艺均体现时代风貌。
culturalAchievements.en: The period carried forward literary and religious traditions from the late insei age while developing clear warrior-era characteristics. Waka, diary literature, war tales, and anecdotal collections circulated widely; painted handscrolls and Buddhist art flourished; and Kamakura sculpture became strikingly realistic and powerful. The rebuilding of Tōdai-ji, the growth of Zen temples, warrior ceremonial forms, and advances in armor craftsmanship all reflected the age.

languageFamily.zh: 日语系（古日语向中世日语过渡阶段）
languageFamily.en: Japonic language family, in a transition from Old Japanese to Early Middle Japanese

gdpDescription.zh: 日本1280年的经济以稻作农业为基础，庄园与公领并行，地区差异明显。畿内、东海、关东和北九州是较成熟的农业与交换中心。货币化正在推进，但米、丝、布匹和劳役仍是重要价值尺度。与南宋灭亡前后的海上贸易联系虽受元朝扩张与战事影响，仍维持一定活力。
gdpDescription.en: Japan's economy in 1280 rested on rice agriculture, with estates and public lands operating side by side and strong regional differences. The Kinai, Tōkai, Kantō, and northern Kyushu were the most developed centers of farming and exchange. Monetization was advancing, but rice, silk, cloth, and labor services remained key measures of value. Maritime trade, though affected by Yuan expansion and warfare around the fall of the Southern Song, still retained some vitality.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米与杂粮农业、桑蚕与纺织、盐业、渔业、木材采运、铁砂冶炼与锻造、陶器、造船、纸张、漆器以及寺社与城下需求带动的手工业
mainIndustries.en: Rice and grain agriculture, sericulture and textiles, salt production, fishing, timber extraction, iron-sand smelting and metalworking, ceramics, shipbuilding, paper, lacquerware, and handicrafts driven by temple and urban demand

tradeGoods.zh: 输出品包括硫磺、刀剑、漆器、木材、珍珠、海产与部分工艺品；输入品包括丝绸、书籍、药材、香料、陶瓷、铜钱与高档织物。国内流通则以米、布、盐、酒、纸、铁器和马匹为主。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included sulfur, swords, lacquerware, timber, pearls, marine products, and selected crafts; imports included silk, books, medicines, aromatics, ceramics, copper coins, and luxury textiles. Domestic circulation centered on rice, cloth, salt, sake, paper, iron tools, and horses.

householdWealth.zh: 多数农户仅有有限余粮、农具、住房与家庭衣物，财富高度依赖收成与领主负担。中上层名主和富裕庄官可拥有水田、佃户、仓储与役畜。武士家庭财富主要体现为知行地收入、武具、马匹和家人郎从。京都、镰仓与博多的商工与寺社机构积累的可支配财富显著高于乡村。
householdWealth.en: Most peasant households possessed only modest surplus grain, tools, dwellings, and clothing, with wealth heavily dependent on harvests and lordly burdens. Better-off village headmen and estate officials might hold paddies, tenants, storehouses, and draft animals. Warrior household wealth was mainly expressed through income rights from lands, arms, horses, and retainers. Merchants, artisans, and religious institutions in Kyoto, Kamakura, and Hakata held much more disposable wealth than rural households.

economicSystem.zh: 以庄园—公领土地经济为基础、逐步货币化的封建分权经济。生产由农户完成，剩余经庄官、地头、守护、寺社与贵族层层分配。幕府并未建立统一经济官僚体系，而是通过裁判土地权利、维持海道与市舶秩序、以及非常时期动员来影响经济。
economicSystem.en: A decentralized feudal economy based on the estate-public land order and undergoing gradual monetization. Production was carried out by peasant households, and surplus was distributed upward through estate officials, jitō, shugo, temples, and nobles. The bakufu did not create a unified economic bureaucracy, but influenced the economy by adjudicating land rights, maintaining order on roads and maritime routes, and mobilizing resources in emergencies.

source.en: Temple, shrine, and state granary contributions

category.en: Kyushu coastal defense and stone-wall construction

category.zh: 御家人动员、军粮与警备

category.en: Roads, courier stations, and document communications

treasuryDescription.zh: 幕府财政储备并非完全集中于单一金库，而是以现钱、米仓、布帛、兵粮、木材与地方可调用资源组成。由于蒙古威胁持续，表面储备尚可，但可自由支配的现金并不宽裕，且对御家人恩赏能力不足已成为隐忧。
treasuryDescription.en: The bakufu treasury was not concentrated in a single cash vault but consisted of coin, rice stores, cloth, military provisions, timber, and callable local resources. Because the Mongol threat persisted, reserve levels looked respectable on paper, but freely disposable cash was limited, and the inability to reward vassals adequately had become a growing concern.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持海防、稳定御家人关系与尽量避免破坏庄园秩序为主。幕府倾向动用临时摊派、劳役和地方资源，而非建立高度中央集权税制。这种政策短期有利于稳定，长期则会因恩赏不足和防务成本上升而承压。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining coastal defense, preserving ties with vassals, and avoiding disruption of the estate order. The bakufu preferred extraordinary assessments, labor demands, and local resource mobilization rather than a highly centralized tax system. This approach supported short-term stability but would face strain over time from rising defense costs and insufficient rewards for service.

description.zh: 核心作战力量，以骑马武士和其随从为主，重视弓马、个人武勇与家门动员
description.en: The core fighting force, composed of mounted samurai and their retainers, emphasizing archery, horsemanship, personal valor, and household mobilization

description.zh: 由武士家人、郎党及临时征发的步战人员构成，负责近战、守营、运输与沿海巡防
description.en: Consisted of household retainers, warrior bands, and temporarily levied foot fighters, used for close combat, camp defense, transport, and coastal patrols

description.zh: 部署于博多湾及北九州沿海，承担瞭望、筑垒、巡逻、登陆阻击与信号传递
description.en: Deployed around Hakata Bay and the northern Kyushu coast for observation, fortification, patrol, anti-landing defense, and signaling

name.zh: 寺社与地方豪族协从兵

description.zh: 由大寺社、庄园势力和地方豪族提供的辅助兵力，战时参与运输、守备与局部作战
description.en: Auxiliary forces supplied by major temples, estate powers, and local magnates, participating in transport, garrison duty, and local operations during war

commanderInChief.zh: 征夷大将军惟康亲王（实权由北条时宗执掌）
commanderInChief.en: Shogun Prince Koreyasu, with effective command exercised by Hōjō Tokimune

technology.zh: 军备以和弓、太刀、薙刀、长枪前形态、甲胄、骑马战和城栅防御为主。面对蒙古军后，更重视集体防御、夜袭、沿海石垣、信号系统与应对火药兵器。日本已接触元军所用震天雷等爆炸兵器，但尚未形成本土火器体系。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on the Japanese bow, tachi swords, naginata, early spear forms, lamellar armor, mounted combat, and fortified positions. After facing the Mongols, greater emphasis was placed on coordinated defense, night attacks, coastal stone walls, signaling systems, and countermeasures against gunpowder weapons. Japan had encountered explosive devices such as the Mongols' thunder-crash bombs, but had not yet developed a native firearms system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自元朝—高丽联合可能再次发动的海上入侵，尤其是博多湾与北九州沿海。次要威胁包括地方武士不满、恩赏分配不足引发的政治离心，以及东北和岛嶼地区的零星边缘冲突。
threats.en: The principal threat was the possibility of another maritime invasion by the Yuan-Goryeo alliance, especially against Hakata Bay and the northern Kyushu coast. Secondary threats included dissatisfaction among warriors, political centrifugal pressures caused by insufficient rewards, and sporadic peripheral conflicts in the northeast and island regions.

populationDescription.zh: 1280年的日本人口约七百万，主体分布于本州中西部、北九州、四国沿海平原与稻作区。人口密度在畿内、濑户内海周边、东海道与部分关东地区较高，东北、山地与边远岛屿较稀疏。总体人口在长期农业扩展后已达到较高水平，但受饥馑、疫病与战时征发影响仍具波动性。
populationDescription.en: Japan's population in 1280 was about seven million, concentrated in western and central Honshu, northern Kyushu, the coastal plains of Shikoku, and major rice-growing districts. Population density was highest in the Kinai, around the Inland Sea, along the Tōkaidō, and in parts of the Kantō, while the northeast, mountain regions, and remote islands were more thinly settled. After long agricultural expansion, the population had reached a relatively high level, though it remained vulnerable to famine, disease, and wartime demands.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为天皇、公家与摄关旧贵族；实权中枢为将军、北条氏与御家人武士。宗教层面有大型寺社、僧侣与神职。乡村中有名主、百姓、自耕与佃耕农户，城市则有工匠、商人、运输者与港口居民。另有被排斥的贱民群体从事屠宰、皮革、刑役等行业。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the emperor, court nobility, and old aristocratic houses, while effective power lay with the shogun, the Hōjō, and the vassal warriors. The religious sphere included major temples, monks, and shrine clergy. In the countryside were village headmen, commoners, owner-cultivators, and tenant farmers, while towns contained artisans, merchants, transport workers, and port communities. There were also excluded outcaste groups engaged in slaughtering, leather work, and penal duties.

allies.zh: 日本在1280年几乎没有正式意义上的稳定对外盟友。与高丽关系因其从属于元朝而敌对紧张。对外联系主要通过僧侣、商人和海商网络维持，与南宋残余世界的传统联系正在被元朝秩序取代。
allies.en: Japan in 1280 had few formal external allies in a strict sense. Relations with Goryeo were tense and hostile because of its subordination to the Yuan. External connections were maintained mainly through monks, merchants, and maritime trading networks, while older ties to the Southern Song world were being displaced by the Yuan order.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量为元朝及其属国高丽；日本拒绝接受元朝宗藩秩序与朝贡要求，因此双方处于持续敌对状态。
enemies.en: The principal hostile powers were the Yuan dynasty and its subordinate state Goryeo; Japan rejected incorporation into the Yuan tributary order, leaving the two sides in an ongoing state of hostility.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以拒元自守、强化海防、严密监视九州海路和维持有限贸易接触为主。幕府对外交往较少采取主动远征模式，而是依靠岛屿屏障、沿岸防御和对使节的强硬态度维护独立。其外交逻辑是防御性、区域性且以国内秩序优先。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on resisting the Yuan, strengthening coastal defense, closely monitoring sea routes to Kyushu, and preserving limited trade contacts. The bakufu did not pursue active overseas expeditions; instead it defended independence through insular geography, shoreline fortification, and a hard line toward foreign envoys. Its diplomatic logic was defensive, regional, and subordinate to domestic order.

era.zh: 中世东亚封建时代
era.en: Medieval East Asian feudal era

keyInnovations.zh: 这一时期的关键技术并非工业性突破，而是农业、军事和制度性的积累：铁制农具普及、水田开发、灌溉改善、双季作在局部尝试、海船与沿海航运经验增长、武士甲胄与刀剑锻造提升、石垣海防工程、文书行政与土地裁判技术成熟。对蒙古火药兵器的接触也扩展了日本对新式战争工具的认知。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations in this period were less industrial breakthroughs than cumulative advances in agriculture, war, and institutions: wider use of iron tools, paddy expansion, improved irrigation, limited experimentation with multiple cropping, growth in seafaring and coastal navigation, better samurai armor and sword-forging, coastal stone defense works, and more mature documentary administration and land adjudication. Contact with Mongol gunpowder weapons also broadened Japanese awareness of new instruments of war.

strengths.zh: 日本的主要优势在于稳固的武士动员体系、海岛地理屏障、地方社会对防务的参与能力，以及幕府在土地裁判与军事协调上的实际权威。经历1274年入侵后，九州防御明显加强，政治上北条时宗的领导也较为坚决。农业基础稳固，京都与镰仓的双中心结构使政治合法性和军事实力可以同时维持。
strengths.en: Japan's main strengths lay in its durable warrior mobilization system, insular geographic defenses, the capacity of local society to contribute to defense, and the bakufu's practical authority in land adjudication and military coordination. After the 1274 invasion, Kyushu's defenses were markedly strengthened, and Hōjō Tokimune's leadership was politically resolute. The agricultural base was solid, and the dual centers of Kyoto and Kamakura allowed legitimacy and military power to coexist.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于财政分散、中央征税能力有限、对御家人恩赏不足，以及政权合法性结构复杂。朝廷、幕府、寺社和地方势力权力交错，平时可相互制衡，危机时则易形成协调成本。若长期维持大规模海防而无新领土与战利品补偿，武士阶层不满将不断累积。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were fiscal fragmentation, limited central tax capacity, inadequate rewards for vassals, and a complex structure of legitimacy. The court, bakufu, religious institutions, and local powers overlapped in authority; this could provide balance in normal times but imposed high coordination costs in crisis. If large-scale coastal defense had to be maintained for long without new lands or spoils to distribute, discontent within the warrior class would continue to build.

outlook.zh: 1280年的日本总体稳定且防御意志强，但其前景取决于能否再次击退元朝并妥善处理战后恩赏问题。短期内，凭借海防工事、动员经验和地理优势，日本有较大机会维持独立。中长期看，若防务开支持续而幕府财政和土地分配机制不改革，镰仓政权将面临慢性削弱。
outlook.en: Japan in 1280 was broadly stable and possessed strong defensive resolve, but its future depended on whether it could repel another Yuan attack and then handle the question of postwar rewards. In the short term, coastal fortifications, mobilization experience, and geographic advantages gave it a good chance of preserving independence. In the medium and long term, if defense spending remained high without reform of bakufu finances and land distribution, the Kamakura regime would face gradual weakening.

description.zh: 以武家政权为核心的日本国家，刚经历蒙古入侵并强化海防。
description.en: A warrior-led Japanese polity that had just faced Mongol invasions and strengthened coastal defenses.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为核心、依靠地方纳亚迦武士贵族与庙产网络治理的南印度区域王国
governmentForm.en: A South Indian regional monarchy centered on the sovereign and governed through local nayaka warrior elites and temple-based institutional networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会以农业村社为基础，上层由王室、军事贵族、婆罗门与寺庙管理集团构成，中层包括商人、手工业者和文书，下层为广大农民、牧民、工匠与依附劳作者；种姓秩序重要，但在边疆开拓和军役体系中保持一定流动性。
socialStructure.en: Society rested on agrarian village communities. The upper strata consisted of the royal house, military nobles, Brahmins, and temple managerial groups; the middle strata included merchants, artisans, and scribes; the lower strata comprised peasants, pastoralists, craftsmen, and dependent laborers. Caste hierarchy was important, but frontier expansion and military service allowed some mobility.

rulingClass.zh: 卡卡提亚王室、纳亚迦军事封臣、婆罗门官僚与掌控寺庙土地的地方精英
rulingClass.en: The Kakatiya royal house, nayaka military feudatories, Brahmin officials, and local elites controlling temple lands

succession.zh: 王位原则上由王朝世袭继承，但需获得宫廷、军事贵族与地方精英承认；鲁德拉玛·德维的统治体现了在非常情形下女性继承亦可被制度化接受。
succession.en: The throne was in principle dynastic and hereditary, but effective succession required recognition by the court, military nobles, and provincial elites; Rudrama Devi’s reign shows that female succession could be institutionalized under exceptional conditions.

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕君主、王室顾问、军事将领、税务与土地管理官员运作；地方上通过纳亚迦、地区总督、城堡守将与村社机构进行治理，国家与寺庙体系高度交织。
structure.en: The central state operated through the sovereign, royal advisers, military commanders, tax and land-management officers; locally it governed through nayakas, regional governors, fort commandants, and village institutions, with the state deeply intertwined with temple establishments.

function.zh: 起草敕令、保管铭文与土地赐封文书、协调中央行政命令
function.en: Drafted royal orders, kept inscriptions and land-grant records, and coordinated central administrative commands

function.zh: 负责土地丈量、税额评估、灌溉田赋征收与国库入账
function.en: Handled land measurement, tax assessment, irrigation-field revenue collection, and treasury accounting

function.zh: 统筹常备军、封臣军役、城堡维护、战象与马匹调度
function.en: Managed the standing army, feudatory levies, fortress maintenance, and the deployment of elephants and horses

function.zh: 修筑与维护水塘、堤坝、渠道、道路和城防工程
function.en: Constructed and maintained tanks, embankments, canals, roads, and defensive works

function.zh: 监督庙产、确认捐赠、协调宗教机构与国家财政义务
function.en: Supervised temple estates, confirmed donations, and coordinated religious institutions with state fiscal obligations

function.zh: 管理市场税、道路关卡、港口联系与远程贸易征课
function.en: Managed market taxes, road toll stations, port connections, and duties on long-distance trade

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由纳杜、西马等区域层级与村社共同构成。王国依靠地方首领、军事封臣、税吏和村长征收赋税、维持治安并组织劳役。许多边疆与新垦区通过授予军职和土地收益来巩固控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was structured through regional tiers such as nadu and sima together with village institutions. The kingdom relied on local chiefs, military feudatories, revenue officers, and village headmen to collect taxes, maintain order, and organize labor. Many frontier and newly settled areas were secured by grants of office and land income.

legalSystem.zh: 法律实践结合王命、习惯法、村社调解、种姓规范与《法论》传统。土地、灌溉、边界、税收与继承争端常由地方议会、庙宇权威或王室官员裁断；刑罚以罚金、没收、拘押和体罚为主。
legalSystem.en: Legal practice combined royal order, customary law, village mediation, caste norms, and dharmashastra tradition. Disputes over land, irrigation, boundaries, taxation, and inheritance were often adjudicated by local assemblies, temple authorities, or royal officers; punishments commonly included fines, confiscation, detention, and corporal penalties.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以地税为核心，依土地质量、灌溉条件和作物类型征收；另有牧税、市场税、关税、手工业税、矿产与盐税，以及对封臣和附属地区的贡赋。劳役和军役义务也是国家汲取资源的重要形式。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal system centered on land revenue, assessed according to land quality, irrigation conditions, and crop type; it was supplemented by pastoral taxes, market taxes, customs dues, artisan levies, and revenues from minerals and salt, as well as tribute from feudatories and subordinate regions. Labor service and military obligations were also important means of extracting resources.

religion.zh: 以印度教为主，尤其崇奉湿婆，同时容纳毗湿奴派、沙克提信仰与地方神灵崇拜；耆那教在部分城镇与商人群体中仍有影响。
religion.en: Predominantly Hindu, especially Shaiva in orientation, while also accommodating Vaishnava traditions, Shakta worship, and local cults; Jainism still retained influence in some towns and merchant communities.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以印度教宗教哲学、王权护法观念与寺庙赞助的学术传统为核心，兼有《法论》、往世书叙事和地方英雄伦理；政治上强调君主维护秩序、水利与丰饶的责任。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Hindu religious philosophy, kingship as protector of dharma, and temple-sponsored scholarly traditions, alongside dharmashastra, puranic narrative, and local heroic ethics; politically it emphasized the ruler’s duty to maintain order, irrigation, and prosperity.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用泰卢固文与卡纳达文书写行政和文学文本，梵文用于铭文、宗教和高等学术场合。
writingSystem.en: Telugu and Kannada scripts were chiefly used for administrative and literary texts, while Sanskrit served inscriptions, religion, and higher scholarly contexts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 卡卡提亚时期以寺庙建筑、石刻铭文、精致柱廊、舞蹈传统和水利工程闻名。瓦朗加尔及哈奴马孔达一带留下成熟的德干石构艺术风格，王朝也推动泰卢固语文学与地方政治文化的凝聚。
culturalAchievements.en: The Kakatiya age was noted for temple architecture, stone inscriptions, refined pillared halls, dance traditions, and hydraulic works. The Warangal-Hanumakonda region developed a mature Deccan stone architectural style, and the dynasty also fostered Telugu literature and a more integrated regional political culture.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属达罗毗荼语系，以泰卢固语为主，行政与宗教上并用梵语和部分卡纳达语。
languageFamily.en: The main language belonged to the Dravidian family, primarily Telugu, with Sanskrit and some Kannada also used in administration and religion.

gdpDescription.zh: 王国经济在1280年前后处于兴盛阶段，以稻作、杂粮、棉花和灌溉农业为基础，辅以手工业、区域贸易和马匹输入。财富高度依赖水塘灌溉体系与对内陆交通节点的掌控。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1280 the kingdom was in a thriving phase, with an economy based on rice, millets, cotton, and irrigation agriculture, supplemented by handicrafts, regional trade, and horse imports. Wealth depended heavily on the tank-irrigation system and control of inland transport nodes.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、水利建设、棉纺织、金属加工、石工建筑、盐与林产、战象与马匹供养、地区商业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, irrigation construction, cotton textiles, metalworking, stone construction, salt and forest products, maintenance of war elephants and horses, and regional commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、稻米、豆类、芝麻、靛蓝、森林产品、铁器、宝石、食盐，以及从海外和西海岸转运来的马匹、金属与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, rice, pulses, sesame, indigo, forest products, iron goods, gemstones, salt, and horses, metals, and luxury items brought in through overseas and west-coast trade

name.zh: 伽底耶那与地方金币银币

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为耕牛、农具、粮食储备和对水塘灌溉土地的使用权；城市商人和寺庙机构则积累货币、珠宝、仓储与土地收益。整体上社会并不均富，但繁荣地区的农业家庭抗风险能力较强。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly held in plough cattle, tools, grain stocks, and rights in irrigated land; urban merchants and temple institutions accumulated coin, jewelry, storage capacity, and land income. Society was not evenly prosperous, but agrarian households in flourishing regions had relatively stronger resilience.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业剩余汲取为核心、并由寺庙经济、军役封授和区域市场联结起来的混合型前近代经济。国家并不完全直接经营生产，而是通过土地税、赐地、关税和政治保护整合资源。
economicSystem.en: A mixed premodern economy centered on extraction of agrarian surplus and tied together by temple economy, military land assignments, and regional markets. The state did not directly manage all production, but integrated resources through land tax, endowments, customs, and political protection.

source.zh: 市场税、关税与道路关卡
source.en: Market taxes, customs, and road tolls

source.zh: 附属首领贡赋与军役折算
source.en: Tribute from subordinate chiefs and commuted military obligations
Mining, salt taxes, and forest revenues

source.zh: 罚金、登记费与其他杂项
source.en: Fines, registration fees, and miscellaneous income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备较为充实，部分以金币、银币、珠宝、铜器和可动用粮储形式存在。财富亦分散于王室库藏、要塞仓储和寺庙体系，不完全以单一现金库形态集中。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was comparatively well stocked, with reserves held partly as gold and silver coin, jewelry, copper vessels, and deployable grain stocks. Wealth was also distributed across royal stores, fortress granaries, and temple institutions rather than concentrated in a single cash treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持灌溉农业基础、保障军费、以赐地和税惠换取地方忠诚，并在丰年积累储备以应对旱灾和战争。对贸易采取征税而非压制的务实态度。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining the irrigation-based agrarian foundation, securing military finance, and buying local loyalty through grants and tax concessions, while building reserves in good years against drought and war. Trade was treated pragmatically as a taxable activity rather than something to suppress.

description.zh: 军队主体，由持矛、刀盾、弓箭与少量攻城器械人员组成，来源包括常备兵、地方封臣军和村社征发。
description.en: The main body of the army, composed of spear, sword-and-shield, archery, and limited siege personnel, drawn from standing troops, feudatory contingents, and village levies.

description.zh: 骑兵是高价值兵种，部分依赖外来马匹供应，用于快速机动、追击和对抗德干诸国精锐部队。
description.en: Cavalry was a high-value arm, partly dependent on imported horses, used for mobility, pursuit, and fighting the elite forces of other Deccan powers.

description.zh: 战象主要用于冲击、威慑和指挥平台，在攻城与野战中具有象征性和实际用途。
description.en: War elephants were employed for shock, intimidation, and command platforms, serving both symbolic and practical functions in siege and field warfare.

description.zh: 负责瓦朗加尔等多重城防体系、边堡、军械库和道路要点，兼任工兵、后勤和守备任务。
description.en: Maintained the multilayered defenses of Warangal and other forts, frontier strongholds, arsenals, and key routes, while also serving engineering, logistical, and garrison functions.

commanderInChief.zh: 鲁德拉玛·德维女王与其大将集团
commanderInChief.en: Queen Rudrama Devi and her senior generals

technology.zh: 军事技术属于13世纪南印度水准，重视石砌要塞、壕沟、防门系统、弓箭、长矛、刀剑、战象和骑兵机动；尚未形成火药武器体系。
technology.en: Military technology was typical of 13th-century South India, emphasizing stone fortifications, moats, gate defenses, bows, spears, swords, war elephants, and cavalry mobility; gunpowder weapon systems had not yet emerged.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自德干地区竞争者，尤其是西方的塞乌纳王国、南方的曷萨拉与潘地亚势力，以及北方德里苏丹国未来向南扩张的潜在风险；内部则需防范地方纳亚迦离心。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival Deccan powers, especially the Seuna kingdom to the west, the Hoysalas and Pandyas to the south, and the longer-term danger of southward expansion by the Delhi Sultanate; internally, the crown also had to guard against centrifugal tendencies among nayakas.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约四百万，主要分布在特伦甘纳和安得拉内陆灌溉农业区，人口密度在水塘密集、土地开发成熟的地区较高。整体人口规模足以支撑区域强国地位，但仍以农村人口为绝对主体。
populationDescription.en: The population was about four million, concentrated chiefly in the inland irrigated agrarian zones of Telangana and Andhra. Density was higher in regions with dense tank networks and mature cultivation. The overall demographic scale was enough to sustain regional great-power status, though the population remained overwhelmingly rural.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与军事贵族居于顶层，其次为婆罗门、寺庙管理者和富商；再下为城镇工匠、文书和普通商贩；人口主体为村社农民、佃耕者、牧民与劳作者，另有森林边缘社群与服务性职业群体。
socialClasses.en: The royal house and military nobility stood at the top, followed by Brahmins, temple managers, and wealthy merchants; below them were urban artisans, scribes, and ordinary traders; the great majority were village peasants, tenants, pastoralists, and laborers, with forest-margin communities and service groups also forming part of society.

allies.zh: 王国在1280年并无稳定意义上的远程正式盟国，更多依靠与部分地方首领、港口商人集团及对德干均势有利的临时合作关系。
allies.en: In 1280 the kingdom had no stable long-distance formal allies in the later sense; it relied more on temporary cooperation with local chiefs, port merchant groups, and arrangements favorable to the Deccan balance of power.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是塞乌纳王国以及德干和南印度的竞争性邻邦；德里苏丹国虽尚未直接征服瓦朗加尔，但已构成北方战略阴影。
enemies.en: Its principal opponents were the Seuna kingdom and other competitive neighbors in the Deccan and South India; the Delhi Sultanate had not yet conquered Warangal but already cast a northern strategic shadow.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持德干力量平衡、控制贸易通道、争取边疆首领归附并避免多线战争为主。对远方欧亚国家几乎无直接外交往来，其国际联系主要通过印度洋商路间接实现。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at preserving the balance of power in the Deccan, controlling trade routes, securing the submission of frontier chiefs, and avoiding multi-front war. It had little direct diplomacy with distant Eurasian states; its wider international connections were mainly indirect through Indian Ocean commerce.

era.zh: 13世纪后期德干中世纪技术时代
era.en: Late 13th-century medieval Deccan technological era

keyInnovations.zh: 大规模水塘灌溉与堤坝工程、石砌同心防御城、防门体系、区域道路与仓储组织、寺庙石构技术、棉纺和金属加工。其核心优势不在突破性发明，而在成熟工程体系与区域资源整合能力。
keyInnovations.en: Large-scale tank irrigation and embankment engineering, stone concentric fortification, defended gate systems, regional road and storage organization, temple stone-building techniques, and cotton and metal production. Its key strength lay less in radical invention than in mature engineering systems and regional resource integration.

strengths.zh: 瓦朗加尔王国拥有强大的灌溉农业基础、稳固的石砌都城、防御纵深、活跃的泰卢固地区整合能力和充足财政。鲁德拉玛·德维能够维持中央权威，显示王朝组织力与政治韧性。
strengths.en: The kingdom of Warangal possessed a strong irrigation-based agrarian foundation, a solid stone-built capital, defensive depth, active integration of the Telugu region, and substantial fiscal capacity. Rudrama Devi’s ability to maintain central authority showed the dynasty’s organizational strength and political resilience.

weaknesses.zh: 政权对地方纳亚迦和封臣武力依赖较深，中央直接控制并不均衡；骑兵高度依赖外来马匹；财政和农业受降雨、塘坝维护和地方合作程度影响明显。一旦面对持续北方入侵或内部叛离，体系容易承压。
weaknesses.en: The regime depended heavily on local nayakas and feudatory military power, so direct central control was uneven; cavalry relied strongly on imported horses; and fiscal as well as agrarian performance was sensitive to rainfall, tank maintenance, and local cooperation. Under sustained northern invasion or internal defection, the system could come under severe strain.

outlook.zh: 在1280年，王国前景总体良好，仍处于兴盛和扩张余波之中。但长期看，德干竞争加剧与德里苏丹国南下将构成决定性挑战，国家能否继续繁荣取决于中央对地方军事贵族的驾驭以及对防务和财政的持续整合。
outlook.en: In 1280 the kingdom’s outlook was broadly favorable, and it remained in a phase of prosperity following expansion. In the longer run, however, intensified Deccan rivalry and the southward push of the Delhi Sultanate would pose decisive challenges; whether it could continue to thrive depended on the crown’s ability to control military nobles and sustain integration of defense and finance.

description.zh: 德干东部的重要泰卢固王国，以坚固都城与灌溉经济著称。
description.en: A major Telugu kingdom of eastern Deccan known for its fortified capital and irrigation-based economy.

governmentForm.zh: 南印度世袭君主制王国，依托宫廷、地方封臣与寺庙机构共同治理
governmentForm.en: A South Indian hereditary monarchical kingdom governed through the royal court, provincial feudatories, and temple institutions

socialStructure.zh: 社会以农业村社为基础，按种姓、职业团体、寺庙共同体与军事封臣网络分层；婆罗门、地方武士贵族、商人行会与寺庙管理者共同构成上层秩序
socialStructure.en: Society rested on agrarian village communities and was stratified by caste, occupational groups, temple communities, and military-feudatory networks; Brahmins, warrior landed elites, merchant guilds, and temple managers formed the upper order

rulingClass.zh: 王族、宫廷重臣、地方纳亚卡与马哈曼达勒什瓦拉级封臣、婆罗门土地受赐者
rulingClass.en: The royal family, senior court ministers, local nayakas and mahāmandaleśvaras, and Brahmin land grantees

succession.zh: 王位原则上由王族男性世袭继承，但常受兄弟支系、分治安排、贵族支持与军事控制影响
succession.en: The throne was in principle inherited patrilineally within the royal house, though succession was often shaped by collateral branches, partition arrangements, noble backing, and military control

structure.zh: 中央政权以国王为核心，下设王室秘书机构、财政征收、军事指挥、司法与地方监督体系；地方由纳杜、维沙耶与村社层级管理，许多地区由附庸首领在效忠条件下半自治统治
structure.en: The central state was centered on the king and supported by royal secretariat, revenue collection, military command, judicial, and provincial supervisory organs; locally the realm was organized through nāḍu, viṣaya, and village tiers, with many regions ruled semi-autonomously by loyal feudatory chiefs

function.zh: 起草敕令、保存铭文与土地赐予记录、处理宫廷文书与外交往来
function.en: Drafted royal orders, maintained inscriptional and land-grant records, and handled court documentation and diplomacy

function.zh: 评估耕地、征收地租与附加税、监督赋役、核对地方上缴
function.en: Assessed cultivated land, collected land revenue and cesses, supervised obligations in labor and produce, and audited provincial remittances

function.zh: 统筹王室军队、军饷、战马与象队供给，并协调封臣兵役
function.en: Coordinated royal forces, military pay, horse and elephant supply, and the service obligations of feudatories

function.zh: 登记寺庙赐田、监督宗教基金、调解寺庙与村社之间的经济权利
function.en: Registered temple endowments, supervised religious funds, and mediated economic rights between temples and village communities

function.zh: 处理财产争议、税务案件、地方越权与治安问题，执行王命与习惯法裁断
function.en: Handled property disputes, tax cases, local abuses of authority, and order-related issues, enforcing royal commands alongside customary law

name.zh: 地方监察与附庸事务署
name.en: Provincial Inspection and Feudatory Affairs Department

function.zh: 巡察地方官与附庸首领，确认忠诚、征调兵力并处理边境事务
function.en: Inspected local officials and feudatory chiefs, confirmed loyalty, mobilized troops, and managed frontier affairs

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由省级与地区级单位、纳杜共同体及村社会议共同承担。村庄是财政与社会治理核心，地方酋长、地主、寺庙管理者与书记构成实际管理层。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through provincial and district units, nāḍu corporate structures, and village assemblies. The village was the core fiscal and social unit, with chiefs, landholders, temple managers, and scribes forming the effective local governing layer.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合王令、达摩沙斯特罗传统、地方习惯法与寺庙契约实践。财产、灌溉、地界、赐地与商业债务纠纷多由地方会议、婆罗门顾问和王家官员共同裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decree, dharmaśāstra tradition, local custom, and temple contractual practice. Disputes over property, irrigation, boundaries, grants, and commercial debts were often judged by local assemblies, Brahmin advisers, and royal officials together.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以地税为主，按土地产出、灌溉条件和作物类型征收稻米、谷物、油籽、甘蔗等税赋，并辅以职业税、市场税、过路税、牧税和港市贸易税；部分税以实物缴纳，部分以金币或混合方式缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested mainly on land revenue, assessed by output, irrigation condition, and crop type, on rice, grains, oilseeds, sugarcane, and related produce, supplemented by occupational taxes, market dues, transit tolls, pastoral taxes, and port trade duties; some dues were paid in kind, others in gold coin or mixed form.

religion.zh: 以印度教为主，特别崇奉毗湿奴派与湿婆派，同时耆那教在部分精英与城市社群中仍具重要地位
religion.en: Predominantly Hindu, especially Vaishnava and Shaiva in orientation, while Jainism remained important among some elites and urban communities

philosophy.zh: 宗教思想受南印度奉爱运动、吠檀多传统与寺庙神学影响；曷萨拉宫廷时期也是摩陀婆阇阿阇黎等双重论思想逐渐成形的重要阶段
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by South Indian bhakti traditions, Vedantic thought, and temple theology; the Hoysala age was also an important period in the emergence of Dvaita thought associated with Madhvacharya

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用古卡纳达文与梵文书写体系，铭文常以卡纳达语和梵语并用
writingSystem.en: Used primarily Old Kannada and Sanskrit writing systems, with inscriptions often bilingual in Kannada and Sanskrit

culturalAchievements.zh: 以贝鲁尔、哈勒比德、索马纳塔普拉等地精雕细刻的皂石神庙建筑闻名，特色包括星形平面、繁复檐饰、神像浮雕、舞姿刻画与精细碑铭；宫廷赞助诗歌、宗教辩论、铜像与仪式艺术，延续卡纳达文学传统
culturalAchievements.en: Renowned for intricately carved soapstone temples at places such as Belur, Halebidu, and Somnathapura, marked by stellate plans, elaborate cornices, sculptural programs, dance imagery, and fine inscriptions; the court patronized poetry, religious disputation, bronzes, and ceremonial arts, continuing the Kannada literary tradition

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属达罗毗荼语系，以卡纳达语为王国核心行政与文化语言之一，梵语则用于高层宗教与铭文传统
languageFamily.en: Its principal language belonged to the Dravidian family, with Kannada serving as a core administrative and cultural language of the kingdom, while Sanskrit remained important in elite religious and inscriptional traditions

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高产农业为基础，辅以寺庙经济、区域手工业、内陆市场与西海岸贸易网络。以南印度1280年前后的条件衡量，曷萨拉属富庶而区域化程度较高的王国，但尚非横跨海洋的大型商业帝国。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on productive agriculture, complemented by temple-based redistribution, regional crafts, inland markets, and west-coast trade networks. By South Indian standards around 1280, the Hoysala realm was prosperous and fairly regionally integrated, though not a vast ocean-spanning commercial empire.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻与旱地农业、椰枣与槟榔种植、甘蔗、纺织、金属加工、石雕建筑、寺庙工艺、牲畜饲养、森林产物采集与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice and dry-field agriculture, coconut and areca cultivation, sugarcane, textiles, metalworking, stone carving and temple construction, temple crafts, livestock raising, forest products, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、胡椒、豆类、槟榔、椰制品、糖、棉布、染织品、檀香、黑木、铁器、铜器、宝石、战马输入与奢侈品转运
tradeGoods.en: Rice, pepper, pulses, areca nut, coconut products, sugar, cotton cloth, dyed textiles, sandalwood, ebony, iron goods, copper goods, gemstones, imported warhorses, and transshipped luxury goods

householdWealth.zh: 乡村家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、牛只、灌溉份额、粮食储备与首饰；城市与寺庙相关家庭则更多持有货币、库存、借贷权与工坊资产。财富分布明显不均，寺庙、婆罗门受赐地与武士地主占有较多剩余。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth in the countryside was mainly expressed in cultivation rights, cattle, irrigation shares, grain stores, and jewelry; urban and temple-linked households more often held coin, inventories, credit claims, and workshop assets. Wealth was markedly unequal, with temples, Brahmin grantees, and warrior-landholders controlling much of the surplus.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业剩余为核心、王权征收与地方封臣分享并存的前近代再分配经济；寺庙、商人行会、土地赐予制度和港市贸易共同支撑财富流动
economicSystem.en: A premodern redistributive economy centered on agrarian surplus, with royal extraction coexisting with shares held by local feudatories; temples, merchant guilds, land-grant institutions, and port trade together sustained the circulation of wealth

source.zh: 市场税、过路税与贸易关税
source.en: Market taxes, transit tolls, and customs
Tribute from feudatories and commuted service dues

source.zh: 王室领地、矿冶与林产收入
source.en: Royal domain, mining, and forest revenue
Fines, judicial fees, and miscellaneous receipts

category.en: Army, warhorses, elephants, and frontier defense
Court, bureaucracy, and rewards to feudatories

category.zh: 寺庙、宗教赞助与赐地维护
category.en: Temples, religious patronage, and maintenance of endowments

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库维持在较稳健水平，包含金币、银锭、珠宝、粮储与可征调的寺庙和附庸资源。虽有一定缓冲能力，但一旦与潘地亚或德里苏丹国发生长期战争，财政压力会迅速上升。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury stood at a fairly sound level, consisting of gold coin, silver bullion, jewels, grain reserves, and resources callable from temples and feudatories. It provided some buffer, but sustained war with the Pandyas or the Delhi Sultanate would quickly raise fiscal strain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，力求在稳定税收基础上维持军政与宗教支出平衡。王权通常避免剧烈增税，更依赖重新分配赐地、强化征收和向附庸索取支援来应对危机。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly conservative, aiming to balance military, administrative, and religious expenditure on a stable revenue base. The monarchy generally avoided sharp tax increases, relying instead on reallocation of grants, tighter collection, and support extracted from feudatories in times of stress.

description.zh: 由持矛、剑盾与弓箭兵组成，是数量最多的兵种，来源包括王室常备军、地方征发与附庸部队
description.en: Composed of spearmen, sword-and-shield troops, and archers; it was the most numerous branch, drawn from royal standing forces, local levies, and feudatory contingents

description.zh: 依赖从阿拉伯海贸易输入的优质战马，是机动作战与贵族武力核心，但维护成本很高
description.en: Dependent on quality warhorses imported through Arabian Sea trade, it formed the core of mobile operations and aristocratic warfare, though at high maintenance cost

description.zh: 用于冲击、威慑与仪式展示，在攻坚和战场心理效果上尤为重要
description.en: Used for shock action, intimidation, and royal display, especially important in siege warfare and battlefield psychological effect

description.zh: 驻守都城、要塞、边境据点和战略道路，负责守备、运输护卫与局部镇压
description.en: Stationed in the capital, forts, frontier posts, and strategic road corridors, responsible for defense, convoy protection, and localized suppression

commanderInChief.zh: 维拉·罗摩那他国王及其达那亚卡将领集团
commanderInChief.en: King Vira Ramanatha and his corps of dandanayaka commanders

technology.zh: 军备以传统南亚冷兵器体系为主，包括弓、矛、剑、甲胄、战象装具与攻城器械。军事组织相当成熟，但尚未像同时期中亚和中国那样形成高度机动的复合弓骑兵体系或火药作战体系。
technology.en: Military equipment was based on the traditional South Asian pre-gunpowder system, including bows, spears, swords, armor, elephant gear, and siege engines. Organization was fairly developed, but it did not match the highly mobile composite-bow cavalry systems of Central Asia or the gunpowder experimentation seen in parts of China.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南方后期潘地亚王朝的扩张、北方德里苏丹国势力逐渐南压、以及与塞乌那和瓦朗加尔等地区强权之间反复变化的联盟与战争。王国内部因分治与附庸独立倾向也存在潜在军事风险。
threats.en: Major threats came from the expansion of the Later Pandya kingdom in the south, the long-term southward pressure of the Delhi Sultanate from the north, and shifting alliances and wars with regional powers such as the Seuna and Warangal states. Internal risks also arose from partition politics and the autonomous tendencies of feudatories.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于卡纳塔克高原南部、河谷灌溉地带与若干商业城镇，整体人口密度中等，依赖季风农业与地方水利。都城及主要寺庙城镇吸引大量工匠、祭司、商人与役工。
