religion.en: Theravada Buddhism was spreading and coexisted with local ancestor cults, tutelary spirits, naga worship, and ritual traditions; the monastic order had not yet become as fully institutionalized as in later centuries

philosophy.zh: 政治理念强调王者护持佛法、积德建功与以仁威并用统一诸勐，同时保留台族重血缘、盟誓与地方自主的观念
philosophy.en: Political thought emphasized the ruler as protector of the faith, accruing merit through righteous rule and conquest, while preserving Tai ideas of kinship, oath-bonding, and local autonomy

writingSystem.zh: 1280年前后的兰纳文书文化仍有限，行政与外交可借用孟文、高棉文或口头传统；独立成熟的泰阮文字体系尚在形成之前
writingSystem.en: Around 1280 written culture in Lan Na remained limited; administration and diplomacy could draw on Mon, Khmer, or oral traditions, while the later fully developed Tai Tham script had not yet emerged

culturalAchievements.zh: 建城与筑壕技术、灌溉谷地开发、佛寺与圣地营造、台族口述史诗与谱系记忆、通过迁民与联盟形成多族群整合，是这一时期最显著的文化成就
culturalAchievements.en: The most notable cultural achievements of the period were urban foundation and moat construction, irrigated valley development, the establishment of monasteries and sacred sites, Tai oral epics and genealogical memory, and multiethnic integration through resettlement and alliance

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属于侗台语系西南台语支，并与孟语、克木语、掸语及其他山地语言长期接触
languageFamily.en: The main language belonged to the Southwestern Tai branch of the Kra-Dai family and existed in sustained contact with Mon, Khmuic, Shan, and other upland languages

gdpDescription.zh: 兰纳经济在区域层面相当兴盛，但仍以农业实物产出为主。若折算为银，其总量在中等规模东南亚内陆王国中颇具竞争力，财富集中于稻谷盈余、人口控制、林产与区域贸易节点，而非高度货币化市场。
gdpDescription.en: Lan Na's economy was thriving on a regional scale but remained based chiefly on agricultural output in kind. When expressed in silver value, its total product was competitive for a medium-sized inland Southeast Asian kingdom, with wealth concentrated in rice surpluses, control of manpower, forest goods, and regional trade nodes rather than in a highly monetized market.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻农业、灌溉工程、林产采集、象与马等役畜经营、织布、木工、冶铁、陶器与区域转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice agriculture, irrigation works, forest extraction, management of elephants and horses, weaving, carpentry, ironworking, pottery, and regional transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、食盐、漆、蜂蜡、木材、象牙、犀角、药材、棉布、铁器、铜器、牲畜以及来自外部的珠饰与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, lacquer, beeswax, timber, ivory, rhinoceros horn, medicinal products, cotton cloth, iron tools, copperware, livestock, and imported beads and luxury goods

name.en: Bullion silver and barter in parallel use

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭的财富主要体现在稻田、灌溉份额、水牛、房屋、织机、储粮与亲族劳动力。贵族和城主则控制更多人口、田地、林地、战利品和贡赋网络。
householdWealth.en: For ordinary households, wealth lay mainly in paddy land, irrigation shares, buffaloes, houses, looms, stored grain, and kin labor. Nobles and town lords controlled larger populations, fields, woodland, war booty, and tribute networks.

economicSystem.zh: 以王权支配人口和谷地资源的农业贡赋经济，辅以城邦间交换、长途商旅与战利品再分配。市场存在，但主要经济关系仍通过徭役、附庸义务和宗族网络组织。
economicSystem.en: An agrarian tribute economy in which royal power rested on the control of manpower and valley resources, supplemented by inter-mueang exchange, long-distance merchants, and redistribution of war gains. Markets existed, but most economic relations were still organized through labor service, vassal obligations, and kin networks.

source.en: Agricultural taxes in kind and land dues
Commuted labor obligations and service deliveries

category.en: Army, elephant corps, and frontier defense

category.zh: 地方安抚、附庸赏赐与婚盟
category.en: Provincial pacification, vassal gifts, and marriage alliances

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非纯粹以银储存，而是由银锭、铜器、珠宝、粮仓储备、布匹、武器、役象以及可随时征调的人力共同构成。按银值折算，其储备足以支持数次中等规模远征或连续歉年救济。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not held purely in silver; it consisted of bullion, copper goods, ornaments, granary stocks, textiles, weapons, elephants, and manpower that could be mobilized when needed. In silver-equivalent terms, reserves were sufficient to support several medium-scale campaigns or relief over successive poor harvests.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向扩张型与整合型：通过征服新谷地、迁民屯垦、强化贡赋关系和修建基础设施来扩大税基，同时维持适度结余以应对战争与歉收。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was expansionary and integrative: the court sought to widen its tax base through conquest of new valleys, resettlement, stronger tribute relations, and investment in infrastructure, while preserving a modest surplus against war and bad harvests.

description.zh: 由国王直属的核心步兵组成，驻守都城与主要城寨，装备长矛、刀剑、盾牌与弓弩
description.en: The king's directly controlled core infantry, stationed in the capital and main forts, equipped with spears, swords, shields, and bows or crossbows

description.zh: 战时由各地首领率领前来效命，是兰纳军力主体，人数波动大，训练程度不一
description.en: The main body of Lan Na's field force, brought by local chiefs in wartime; numbers fluctuated and quality varied considerably

description.zh: 主要用于冲击、指挥平台、威慑和运输，在山谷战争中具有重要政治与战术象征
description.en: Used chiefly for shock action, command platforms, intimidation, and transport, with strong political and tactical significance in valley warfare

description.zh: 数量有限，多承担传令、侦察、突袭和追击任务，马匹来源不稳定
description.en: Limited in number, they mainly handled dispatch carrying, reconnaissance, raiding, and pursuit, with horse supply not always reliable

description.zh: 由城镇居民和平时不脱产农民构成，负责守门、修壕、运粮与地方防御
description.en: Composed of townsmen and part-time cultivators, responsible for gates, moat repair, grain transport, and local defense

commanderInChief.zh: 孟莱国王亲自统帅
commanderInChief.en: King Mangrai in person as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军备以冷兵器时代技术为主，善于城寨构筑、竹木工事、弓弩、长矛、刀剑与战象运用；尚未形成火药武器体系。后勤依托谷地仓储、河谷道路与民夫运输。
technology.en: Military technology was that of a late pre-gunpowder age, with strengths in fortification, bamboo-and-timber fieldworks, bows and crossbows, spears, swords, and war elephants; no gunpowder weapon system yet existed. Logistics relied on valley granaries, river routes, and porter labor.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与素可泰在泰北谷地的竞争、与蒲甘势力及掸地诸首领的边境摩擦，以及扩张过快导致附属勐离心。来自元朝的威胁更多是间接的区域压力，而非直接入侵兰纳腹地。
threats.en: The main threats came from rivalry with Sukhothai over northern Tai valleys, frontier friction with Pagan's sphere and Shan chiefs, and the centrifugal tendencies of rapidly acquired subordinate mueang. Pressure from the Yuan was more an indirect regional factor than an immediate invasion threat to Lan Na's heartland.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于清莱周边及诸河谷盆地，稻作区人口密度较高，山地则分布较稀疏的部族聚落。国家正在通过战争与迁徙吸纳新的居民与劳动力。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated above all in the valleys around Chiang Rai and other river basins, with relatively dense settlement in paddy zones and sparser tribal communities in the uplands. The kingdom was actively absorbing new inhabitants and labor through warfare and resettlement.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与勐侯、僧侣与宗教人士、自由农民、工匠与商人、附庸山地部族、战俘与依附人口构成主要阶层。身份并非绝对僵化，但政治与军事服役义务差异明显。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata were the royal family and mueang lords, monks and religious specialists, free cultivators, artisans and merchants, tributary upland tribes, and war captives or dependent populations. Status was not absolutely rigid, but political and military obligations varied greatly.

allies.zh: 兰纳的盟友关系多属流动性质，通常依靠婚盟、共同敌人和临时结盟维系；与部分台族城邦及若干地方首领保持友好或臣属关系
allies.en: Lan Na's alliances were fluid, usually maintained through marriage, common enemies, and temporary coalitions; it enjoyed friendly or subordinate relations with some Tai towns and various local chiefs

enemies.zh: 最主要的竞争对手是素可泰，其次是在西部与西北方向有影响力的蒲甘残余势力、掸地诸侯以及不稳定的边境首领
enemies.en: Its chief rival was Sukhothai, followed by remnants of Pagan influence to the west and northwest, Shan lords, and unstable frontier chiefs

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以扩张北泰谷地控制、争取贸易通道、吸纳附庸首领和避免被更大强权直接吞并为核心。兰纳倾向于在强邻之间保持弹性，通过贡礼、婚盟与军事行动交替使用来扩大影响。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on extending control over northern Tai valleys, securing trade routes, absorbing tributary chiefs, and avoiding direct subordination to larger powers. Lan Na sought flexibility among stronger neighbors, alternating tribute, marriage alliances, and military action to expand its influence.

era.zh: 十三世纪东南亚内陆铁器与灌溉扩张时代
era.en: 13th-century mainland Southeast Asian iron-age and irrigation expansion era

keyInnovations.zh: 谷地水利系统扩展、城寨壕沟建设、铁制农具与兵器普及、战象军事组织、跨山地交通与多族群人口迁置技术，是兰纳崛起的关键条件
keyInnovations.en: The extension of valley irrigation systems, moated fortification building, wider use of iron agricultural tools and weapons, military organization around elephants, and practical methods of upland communication and multiethnic resettlement were the key conditions behind Lan Na's rise

strengths.zh: 兰纳在1280年最大的优势是充满活力的开国统治者、持续扩张的谷地农业、灵活的曼陀罗政治结构以及整合多族群边地的能力。其地理位置使其能连接掸地、上缅甸与中南半岛北部贸易网络。
strengths.en: Lan Na's greatest strengths in 1280 were its energetic founding ruler, expanding valley agriculture, flexible mandala political structure, and ability to integrate a multiethnic frontier. Its location allowed it to connect trade networks linking Shan regions, Upper Burma, and northern mainland Southeast Asia.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于中央制度尚不成熟、对地方首领依赖较深、文字行政和税制标准化不足、军事动员受季节与地形限制，且新征服地区可能迅速反叛。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in still immature central institutions, deep dependence on local chiefs, limited written administration and tax standardization, military mobilization constrained by season and terrain, and the possibility of rapid revolt in newly conquered areas.

outlook.zh: 若孟莱能够继续击败竞争性台族政权、稳住西方边境并推动宗教与行政整合，兰纳有望在未来数十年成长为北方强国；但若继承发生争斗或附庸离散，其扩张成果也可能很快瓦解。
outlook.en: If Mangrai could continue defeating rival Tai powers, stabilize the western frontier, and deepen religious and administrative integration, Lan Na could grow into a major northern power over the coming decades; yet succession conflict or vassal fragmentation could quickly undo these gains.

description.zh: 泰北新兴政权，整合山间谷地并与缅甸、素可泰竞争。
description.en: A rising northern Tai polity integrating upland valleys while competing with Burma and Sukhothai.

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心、带有父王式统治色彩的上座部佛教君主制，依靠宗主—附庸关系统辖周边城邦
governmentForm.en: A Theravada Buddhist monarchy centered on royal authority, marked by a paternal style of rule and sustained through suzerain-vassal ties over surrounding mueang city-states

socialStructure.zh: 社会以国王与王族为顶层，其下为地方统治者、僧团、自由农民、工匠、商人及战俘与依附人口；各地以“勐”或城邦为基本组织单位，地方首领向素可泰纳贡并保持一定自治。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the king and royal kin, followed by local rulers, the Buddhist sangha, free peasantry, artisans, merchants, and war captives or dependent populations; the basic unit was the mueang city-state, whose chiefs paid tribute to Sukhothai while retaining limited autonomy.

rulingClass.zh: 帕銮王族、王室姻亲、受封地方领主以及与宫廷关系密切的高级僧侣
rulingClass.en: The Phra Ruang royal house, royal in-laws, enfeoffed local lords, and senior monks closely connected to the court

succession.zh: 王位继承以王族内部推举与长幼、威望、军事能力相结合，并非绝对固定的长子继承制
succession.en: Succession combined dynastic selection within the royal house with considerations of seniority, prestige, and military capacity, rather than following a strictly fixed primogeniture system

structure.zh: 素可泰政体尚未形成高度文官化中央官僚，而是由国王直接统摄王都、宗室成员分掌要地、地方城主作为附庸治理各勐；行政、财政、司法与军事职能彼此重叠，宫廷与宗教机构在治理中作用显著。
structure.en: The Sukhothai polity had not yet developed a heavily bureaucratized central civil administration; instead, the king directly controlled the capital, royal kinsmen managed major centers, and local city lords governed subordinate mueang as vassals. Administrative, fiscal, judicial, and military functions overlapped, while court and religious institutions played major roles in governance.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室财产、礼仪、使节接待与御用仓储
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, ceremonies, reception of envoys, and royal storehouses

function.zh: 征收田赋、劳役、森林与矿产收入，并登记附庸城邦贡品
function.en: Collected land tax, labor obligations, revenue from forests and minerals, and recorded tribute from subordinate city-states

function.zh: 协调各附庸城邦、任命或确认地方首领、处理边地纠纷与忠诚关系
function.en: Coordinated subordinate city-states, appointed or confirmed local chiefs, and handled frontier disputes and loyalty relations

function.zh: 负责征发兵员、整备武器、组织象队与边境防务
function.en: Oversaw troop levies, weapon preparation, organization of elephant corps, and frontier defense

function.zh: 审理重大案件、接受民众申诉，并依习惯法、王命与佛教伦理裁断争端
function.en: Heard major cases, received popular petitions, and judged disputes according to customary law, royal command, and Buddhist moral principles

function.zh: 维护堤坝、水池、渠道与稻田开发，保障农业生产
function.en: Maintained embankments, reservoirs, canals, and paddy expansion to support agricultural production

function.zh: 资助寺院、管理施舍土地、主持佛教法会并刻立王室铭文
function.en: Supported monasteries, managed donated lands, organized Buddhist ceremonies, and commissioned royal inscriptions

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政采取分层城邦体系：核心区域由王室直接控制，近附庸由亲王或忠诚领主镇守，远方城邦则通过纳贡、婚姻、交换人质和军事威慑维持臣属关系。地方首领负责治安、征税、征发劳役与兵员。
localAdmin.en: Local administration followed a tiered city-state system: the core zone was directly ruled by the crown, nearer dependencies were governed by princes or loyal lords, and more distant mueang were kept subordinate through tribute, marriage ties, hostage exchange, and military pressure. Local rulers handled order, taxation, labor levies, and troop mobilization.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要建立在泰人习惯法、王命裁决、地方惯例与佛教伦理之上，尚未形成后世那样系统成文法。国王被视为最高裁判者，重大纠纷可直接上达王廷。
legalSystem.en: Law rested mainly on Tai customary practice, royal judgments, local precedent, and Buddhist ethics, and had not yet become a fully systematized written code of the later type. The king was regarded as the supreme judge, and important disputes could be appealed directly to the royal court.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入来自稻田赋税、森林产出、矿产、手工业贡纳、市场关税以及附庸城邦的贡品；税收往往以实物、劳役和地方性贡输形式征收，而非完全货币化。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from paddy taxes, forest produce, minerals, craft tribute, market dues, and tribute from subordinate mueang; taxation was often collected in kind, labor service, and localized tribute rather than in fully monetized form.

religion.zh: 以上座部佛教为主，并融合地方万物有灵信仰、祖先崇拜与婆罗门礼仪；1280年前后素可泰正积极接受来自斯里兰卡与孟人地区的佛教影响。
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism predominated, blended with local animist beliefs, ancestor veneration, and Brahmanical court ritual; around 1280 Sukhothai was actively absorbing Buddhist influences from Sri Lanka and Mon cultural zones.

philosophy.zh: 政治与伦理观念强调佛教功德、仁王理想、布施与公正统治，同时保留泰人“父王爱民”的统治观。
philosophy.en: Political and ethical thought emphasized Buddhist merit, the ideal of the righteous king, generosity, and just rule, while preserving the Tai notion of a paternal ruler caring for his people.

writingSystem.zh: 素可泰时期与兰甘亨相关的泰文字传统正在形成，以古高棉文字与孟文书写传统为基础发展出早期泰文书写体系。
writingSystem.en: An early Thai writing tradition associated with the Sukhothai age and Ram Khamhaeng was taking shape, developing from Old Khmer and Mon scribal models into an early Thai script system.

culturalAchievements.zh: 素可泰文化成就包括早期泰文铭文传统、佛寺建筑、佛像铸造、以水利与城市规划结合的都城建设，以及将泰语王权表达与佛教宇宙观结合起来的政治文化。此期也为后世泰国艺术风格奠定基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Sukhothai’s cultural achievements included the early Thai inscriptional tradition, temple construction, bronze Buddha casting, urban planning integrated with water management, and a political culture that fused Thai-language kingship with Buddhist cosmology. The period also laid foundations for later Thai artistic styles.

languageFamily.zh: 台语族（侗台语系），同时宫廷与宗教场域受高棉语、孟语与巴利语影响
languageFamily.en: Tai languages of the Kra-Dai family, with Khmer, Mon, and Pali exerting influence in courtly and religious contexts

gdpDescription.zh: 素可泰经济以稻作农业为核心，配合林产、象役、陶器、金属加工和区域贸易。以13世纪东南亚标准看，其经济处于上升阶段，核心平原和贸易节点表现活跃，但整体仍以农业与实物征收为主，货币流通有限。
gdpDescription.en: The Sukhothai economy was centered on wet-rice agriculture, supplemented by forest products, elephant labor, ceramics, metalworking, and regional trade. By 13th-century Southeast Asian standards it was in a rising phase, with active core plains and trade nodes, though the broader economy remained primarily agrarian and based on in-kind extraction with limited monetization.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻种植、灌溉农业、森林采集、象役与畜养、陶瓷烧造、青铜与铁器加工、木材贸易、地方纺织与盐产
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, irrigated agriculture, forest extraction, elephant keeping and labor, ceramic production, bronze and iron working, timber trade, local textiles, and salt production

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、盐、木材、漆、蜂蜡、象、象牙、犀角、鹿皮、陶器、金属器、棉布，以及从外部输入的珠宝、精细织物与宗教用品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, timber, lacquer, beeswax, elephants, ivory, rhinoceros horn, deer hides, ceramics, metal goods, cotton cloth, and imported luxuries such as jewels, fine textiles, and religious items

name.en: Silver baht equivalent with in-kind tribute

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户的财富主要体现在稻田使用权、水牛、工具、粮食储备与家族劳力；地方首领和寺院掌握较多土地、劳役与奢侈品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly embodied in access to paddy land, buffaloes, tools, stored grain, and family labor; local lords and monasteries controlled larger shares of land, service obligations, and luxury goods.

unit.en: Sukhothai silver baht equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王权监督下的农业贡纳经济为主体，结合地方市场交换、附庸进贡和区域性长途贸易；寺院、地方领主与王室共同参与资源再分配。
economicSystem.en: An agrarian tribute economy under royal supervision, combined with local market exchange, vassal tribute, and regional long-distance trade; monasteries, local lords, and the crown all participated in resource redistribution.

source.en: Land tax and commuted labor obligations
Forest, mineral, and royal estate income

category.en: Court, royal household, and patronage to local lords

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以银、金饰、粮仓储备、象匹、武器和贡品存货共同构成，纯粹铸币或金属现金储备有限，但王室掌握可迅速动员的实物资源。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of silver, gold ornaments, granary reserves, elephants, weapons, and stored tribute goods; purely monetized bullion reserves were limited, but the crown controlled substantial in-kind resources that could be mobilized quickly.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体偏向宽缓征收与王室恩惠分配，以维持地方忠诚和鼓励垦殖；在扩张时期则通过附庸贡纳和边地整合增加收入，而非建立沉重统一税制。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy generally favored relatively light extraction and royal redistribution to preserve local loyalty and encourage cultivation; in times of expansion, the kingdom increased revenue through tributary inflows and frontier integration rather than through a heavy uniform tax regime.

description.zh: 驻守都城与核心地区，负责王宫护卫、快速出征和镇压叛乱，以长矛、刀剑、弓弩为主。
description.en: Stationed in the capital and core region, this force protected the palace, served in rapid campaigns, and suppressed rebellions, using spears, swords, and bows or crossbows.

description.zh: 由附庸城邦和地方首领在战时提供，数量大于训练水平，适合守城、运输与区域作战。
description.en: Raised by subordinate mueang and local chiefs in wartime, these forces were larger than their training quality and were suited to garrison duty, transport, and regional warfare.

description.zh: 作为王权象征与战场冲击力量，用于指挥、突破和威慑，常由精锐卫士伴随。
description.en: Serving as both a symbol of kingship and a battlefield shock arm, war elephants were used for command, breakthrough operations, and intimidation, often escorted by elite guards.

description.zh: 规模不大，主要承担侦察、传令、追击和联络，受地形与马匹供给限制。
description.en: Small in scale, these forces mainly carried out reconnaissance, messaging, pursuit, and communications, limited by terrain and horse supply.

description.zh: 负责沿河运输、物资补给、渡口控制和在低地水网中的机动作战。
description.en: Responsible for river transport, supply movement, ferry control, and maneuver operations in lowland water networks.

commanderInChief.zh: 兰甘亨国王

technology.zh: 军备以铁制刀矛、弓箭、盾牌、皮甲与战象运用为主，攻城器械较简，尚未见火药兵器成体系使用；军事优势更多来自机动征召、地形熟悉和城邦网络。
technology.en: Armament centered on iron swords and spears, bows, shields, leather armor, and the use of war elephants; siege equipment was relatively simple, and gunpowder weapons were not yet employed systematically. Military advantages rested more on flexible levies, knowledge of terrain, and the city-state network.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与高棉吴哥势力的竞争、缅甸蒲甘衰落后留下的权力真空、北方兰纳等泰人政权的潜在角逐，以及附庸城邦忠诚不稳所导致的内部离心。
threats.en: Major threats came from rivalry with the Khmer power at Angkor, the power vacuum left by Pagan’s decline, potential competition from northern Tai polities such as Lan Na, and internal centrifugal tendencies caused by uncertain loyalty among subordinate mueang.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在素可泰核心平原、灌溉稻作区和附庸城邦周边。族群以泰人群体为主，并包含孟人、高棉人、山地部族和被纳入统治的边地居民。人口增长受益于农业扩展与区域政治整合。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Sukhothai core plains, irrigated rice zones, and the areas surrounding subordinate mueang. The population was predominantly Tai, but also included Mon, Khmer, upland groups, and frontier peoples incorporated into the kingdom. Growth was supported by agricultural expansion and regional political consolidation.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层可概括为王族与贵族、地方城主、僧侣、自由农民、工匠与商人、依附劳役人口及战俘。与后世更严密的等级制相比，此时社会仍保有较强地方流动性，但政治权力和资源明显集中于王室、领主与寺院。
socialClasses.en: Social classes may be summarized as royalty and nobles, local city lords, monks, free peasants, artisans and merchants, dependent laborers, and war captives. Compared with later more rigid hierarchies, society still retained significant local fluidity, but political power and resources were clearly concentrated in the crown, the lords, and the monasteries.

allies.zh: 素可泰通过宗主关系、婚姻联结和共同对抗吴哥影响，与若干泰人及上湄南河城邦保持合作；同时可能寻求与元朝建立朝贡性接触以提升合法性和贸易机会。
allies.en: Through suzerain ties, marriage connections, and common opposition to Angkorian influence, Sukhothai maintained cooperation with several Tai and upper Chao Phraya city-states; it also likely sought tributary-style contact with the Yuan to enhance legitimacy and trade opportunity.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是吴哥帝国在中南半岛西部与湄南河流域的残余影响力，此外还包括不稳定或反复脱离的附庸城邦，以及在北方可能与其争夺势力范围的兰纳政权。
enemies.en: Its principal rival was the residual influence of the Angkorian Empire in western mainland Southeast Asia and the Chao Phraya basin, along with unstable or rebellious subordinate mueang and the northern Lan Na polity as a possible competitor for regional influence.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交方针以区域扩张、吸纳附庸、保持贸易通道和借助佛教文化提升王权合法性为核心；其政策务实灵活，既使用武力，也依赖朝贡、赐予、联姻与宗教赞助。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on regional expansion, incorporation of tributaries, preservation of trade routes, and the enhancement of royal legitimacy through Buddhist culture; it was pragmatic and flexible, using force when needed but also relying on tribute, gifts, marriage alliances, and religious patronage.

era.zh: 13世纪晚期东南亚中世纪王国时代
era.en: Late 13th-century medieval mainland Southeast Asian kingdom era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度进展包括灌溉与蓄水工程、早期泰文字形成、青铜佛像铸造、陶瓷生产提升、城市与寺院规划，以及更成熟的区域交通与贡纳整合机制。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological and institutional advances included irrigation and reservoir works, the emergence of early Thai script, bronze Buddha casting, improved ceramic production, urban and temple planning, and more mature systems of regional transport and tributary integration.

strengths.zh: 素可泰在1280年前后最显著的优势是政治上升势头强、王权个人魅力突出、农业基础稳固、附庸网络扩展迅速，并能将佛教文化、城市建设与军事扩张结合起来。其统治方式较为灵活，适合整合新征服地区。
strengths.en: Sukhothai’s greatest strengths around 1280 were its strong political momentum, the personal authority of the king, a solid agrarian base, a rapidly expanding tributary network, and the ability to combine Buddhist culture, urban development, and military expansion. Its style of rule was flexible and well suited to integrating newly subordinated territories.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于中央官僚制度尚不发达，对地方忠诚高度依赖个人关系；疆域扩张越快，附庸离心风险越大。经济虽繁荣上升，但货币化程度和远程贸易控制力仍有限，难以长期支撑高度集权国家机器。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in an underdeveloped central bureaucracy and a heavy dependence on personal relationships for local loyalty; the faster the realm expanded, the greater the centrifugal risk among tributaries. Though prosperous and rising, the economy remained only partly monetized and had limited control over long-distance trade, making it difficult to sustain a highly centralized state apparatus over the long term.

outlook.zh: 短期前景乐观：在兰甘亨统治下，素可泰很可能继续扩大在上湄南河与邻近泰人城邦中的影响力，并强化文化声望。长期来看，其命运将取决于王位继承是否稳定、能否把个人统治转化为持久制度，以及如何应对吴哥、兰纳和后来更强大泰人政权的竞争。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was favorable: under Ram Khamhaeng, Sukhothai was likely to continue expanding its influence in the upper Chao Phraya region and among neighboring Tai polities while strengthening its cultural prestige. In the longer term, its fate would depend on stable succession, the conversion of personal kingship into durable institutions, and its response to competition from Angkor, Lan Na, and later stronger Tai states.

description.zh: 新兴泰族王国，正迅速扩展其在上湄南河地区的影响。
description.en: A rising Tai kingdom rapidly expanding its influence in the upper Chao Phraya region.

governmentForm.zh: 以皇帝为核心的儒家官僚君主制，兼具宗室军事贵族色彩
governmentForm.en: A Confucian bureaucratic monarchy centered on the emperor, with strong royal-clan military aristocratic features

socialStructure.zh: 社会由皇族与宗室贵族、文武官僚、地方豪族、僧侣、农民、工匠、商人及少量奴婢构成。红河平原的村社是基层社会核心，国家通过宗室、官僚与乡社共同治理。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and princely aristocracy, civil and military officials, local notable families, monks, peasants, artisans, merchants, and a smaller servile population. Village communities in the Red River plain formed the basic social unit, and the state governed through a combination of royal kin, officials, and local communes.

rulingClass.zh: 陈氏皇族、受封王侯、中央高级文武官员与依附朝廷的地方豪族
rulingClass.en: The Trần imperial clan, enfeoffed princes and marquises, senior central civil and military officials, and local notable lineages tied to the court

succession.zh: 名义上为皇室世袭，通常由皇帝在宗室内指定继承人；陈朝常实行皇帝提前禅位于太子、太上皇继续掌政与辅政的制度。1280年实际为圣宗太上皇与仁宗皇帝并存。
succession.en: Formally hereditary within the royal house, with the emperor typically designating an heir from the dynasty. The Trần commonly practiced early abdication to the crown prince, after which the retired emperor continued to wield major authority. In 1280, real power was shared by Retired Emperor Thánh Tông and Emperor Nhân Tông.

structure.zh: 中央政府以皇帝与太上皇为最高权威，下设中书、尚书、御史等机构，并由六部性质的行政体系、国子监、翰林与军事统帅机关协同运作。地方分路、府、州、县，由朝廷任命或认可的官员与土豪共同管理。
structure.en: The central government was headed by the emperor and retired emperor as supreme authorities, beneath whom operated secretariat, executive, and censorial organs, alongside ministries, the National Academy, Hanlin-style scholarly offices, and military command institutions. Locally, the realm was divided into circuits, prefectures, provinces, and districts administered by court-appointed or court-recognized officials in cooperation with local notable families.

function.zh: 起草诏令、协调朝政、承接皇帝与太上皇意旨
function.en: Drafted edicts, coordinated state affairs, and transmitted the will of the emperor and retired emperor

function.zh: 统辖政务执行、户籍、赋税、工程、军需与官员任用的日常行政
function.en: Oversaw routine administration including registration, taxation, works, military supply, and implementation of appointments

function.zh: 监察官员、纠举贪渎、审议重大政令执行情况
function.en: Supervised officials, investigated corruption, and reviewed implementation of major policies

function.zh: 培养官员后备、教授经学与礼制、主持高等教育
function.en: Trained future officials, taught the classics and ritual, and served as the leading institution of higher learning

function.zh: 负责诏诰文书、史书编修、礼仪与学术咨询
function.en: Handled court documents, historical compilation, ritual matters, and scholarly consultation

function.zh: 掌军事调度、边防部署、水陆军征发与战备
function.en: Managed military mobilization, frontier deployments, naval and land forces, and war preparedness

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依红河核心区与外缘山区而有差异。核心稻作地区由朝廷任命知府、知州、县令等官治理；边缘地区则依赖羁縻安排、土官与地方首领。村社拥有较强自治能力，负责赋役分摊、堤坝维护与治安协作。
localAdmin.en: Local administration varied between the Red River core and upland frontier zones. In the core rice-growing regions, the court appointed prefectural, provincial, and district officials; peripheral areas relied more on indirect rule through native officials and local chiefs. Village communes retained strong self-governing functions, especially in allocating tax burdens, maintaining dikes, and assisting local order.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以皇帝诏令、成文法、礼制规范与地方惯习并用。陈朝承继李朝以来的汉式法制传统，但审判常兼顾宗族、村社与实际治理需要。重罪由中央或高级地方官审理，轻案多在州县层面裁处。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined imperial edicts, written law, ritual norms, and local custom. The Trần inherited the broadly Sinic legal tradition of the earlier Lý period, but adjudication often balanced clan interests, village practice, and practical governance. Serious crimes were heard by central or senior local authorities, while lesser cases were settled at prefectural or district level.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以田赋、户税、徭役、盐铁及贸易关征为主。税收多以稻米、布帛、钱币与劳役形式征纳；战时会加强军粮征集、船只调用与宗室庄田供输。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue rested on land tax, household levies, corvée, and duties on salt, metals, and trade. Taxes were collected in rice, cloth, cash, and labor service; in wartime the court intensified grain requisitions, transport obligations, and contributions from princely estates.

religion.zh: 以佛教最具社会影响力，同时儒学在官僚与教育体系中不断上升，道教、祖先祭祀、地方神祇与山川城隍崇拜广泛并存。
religion.en: Buddhism remained the most socially influential religion, while Confucian learning continued to rise in the bureaucracy and education. Daoist practices, ancestor worship, and widespread cults of local, riverine, and tutelary spirits coexisted alongside it.

philosophy.zh: 思想上呈现佛、儒、道并行格局。治国理念偏重儒家伦理、忠孝与文治秩序；社会生活与王权合法性又深受佛教慈悲观、护国观及宗教仪式影响。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life reflected a Buddhist-Confucian-Daoist synthesis. Statecraft emphasized Confucian ethics, loyalty, filiality, and civil order, yet social life and royal legitimacy were also deeply shaped by Buddhist ideas of compassion, realm protection, and ritual practice.

writingSystem.zh: 主要官方文字为汉文，行政、诏令、科举与史书多以文言书写；同时越人语言以口语形式广泛使用，早期喃字传统已在形成与有限运用中。
writingSystem.en: Classical Chinese was the principal official script, used for administration, edicts, examinations, and historical writing. At the same time, the Vietnamese language was widely spoken, and an early tradition of chữ Nôm was beginning to develop and see limited use.

culturalAchievements.zh: 陈朝时期文教兴盛，科举制度持续发展，佛寺建筑、铜钟铸造、碑刻与宫廷文学均有成就。国家在抗击蒙古威胁前夕强化军事文学、忠义叙事与王朝史学，形成独特的爱国与护国文化传统。
culturalAchievements.en: The Trần period saw flourishing learning, continued development of the examination system, and achievements in temple architecture, bronze casting, inscriptions, and court literature. On the eve of Mongol invasions, the state also fostered military writing, narratives of loyalty and righteousness, and dynastic historiography, helping form a distinctive patriotic and realm-protecting culture.

languageFamily.zh: 越语属南亚语系孟-高棉语族；上层书面文化则深受汉语文言传统影响。
languageFamily.en: Vietnamese belonged to the Austroasiatic family, Mon-Khmer branch, while elite written culture was heavily shaped by the Classical Chinese tradition.

unit.en: strings of cash per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 1280年前后的大越是以高产水稻农业为主的中等规模区域经济体。红河三角洲人口稠密、灌溉与堤防成熟，能够支持较高的农业剩余、城市消费和军事动员。经济货币化程度有限但高于许多内陆农国，国家财政同时依赖实物、劳役与现金。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1280, Đại Việt was a medium-sized regional economy centered on productive wet-rice agriculture. The densely settled Red River delta, with developed irrigation and dike systems, supported substantial agricultural surplus, urban consumption, and military mobilization. Monetization remained partial but was stronger than in many inland agrarian states, and public finance relied on a mix of goods, labor, and cash.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻种植、渔业、盐业、手工业织造、陶瓷、木作、冶铁、铜器铸造、造船与河海运输
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice cultivation, fishing, salt production, textile handicrafts, ceramics, woodworking, ironworking, bronze casting, shipbuilding, and riverine-maritime transport

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、盐、鱼露、丝绸、布匹、陶瓷、木材、象牙、犀角、香料转口品、铜器、铁器以及与南海贸易相关的珍货
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, fish sauce, silk, cloth, ceramics, timber, ivory, rhinoceros horn, re-exported aromatics, bronzeware, iron tools, and luxury goods connected to South China Sea trade

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现在田地使用权、牛、水牛、农具、粮仓、住宅、织机与宗族网络中，而非大量金银积存。核心平原农户相对稳定，但易受水灾、征发与战事影响。
householdWealth.en: For most households, wealth was embodied in use rights to fields, cattle and buffalo, tools, granaries, houses, looms, and kinship networks rather than large stores of precious metals. Households in the delta core were relatively secure, though vulnerable to floods, requisitions, and war.

unit.en: strings of cash per working adult per year

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的农业税收经济与地方市场网络并存。皇室庄田、官田、寺院地产与村社小农经济共同构成生产基础；沿海与边贸则将大越纳入华南、占城、东南亚海域与更广泛的南海商业体系。
economicSystem.en: The economy combined state-led agrarian extraction with local market exchange. Royal estates, official lands, temple properties, and village smallholder production together formed its base, while coastal and frontier trade linked Đại Việt to South China, Champa, Southeast Asian waters, and the wider South China Sea commercial sphere.

source.en: Commutation of labor service and miscellaneous taxes

source.zh: 宗室庄田供输与非常征收
source.en: Contributions from princely estates and extraordinary levies

category.en: Hydraulic works, dikes, and public infrastructure

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库存量以铜钱、绢帛、粮食储备及军需物资构成，贵金属占比不高。由于蒙古威胁迫近，朝廷尤其重视粮仓、船材、兵器和河防储备，而不仅是货币结余。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of cash coin, silk and cloth, grain reserves, and military stores, with precious metals forming only a limited share. Because of the looming Mongol threat, the court placed special emphasis on granaries, ship timber, weapons, and river-defense reserves rather than on monetary balances alone.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体审慎，平时维持轻重适中的赋税以稳定村社生产，战备时期则优先扩充军粮、修治河防与整备水军。朝廷倾向通过宗室动员、实物征收和局部加派来应对危机，而非长期高压征税。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly cautious: in normal times taxes were kept at a moderate level to preserve village production, while periods of military preparation prioritized grain stocks, river defenses, and naval readiness. In crisis, the court preferred mobilizing royal estates, collecting in kind, and imposing limited extraordinary levies rather than maintaining permanently heavy taxation.

description.zh: 驻守升龙与王朝核心地区，负责宫廷安全、快速机动与战时骨干部队，多由朝廷直接控制。
description.en: Stationed in Thăng Long and the dynastic core, these forces secured the court, provided rapid response capability, and formed the military backbone under direct royal control.

description.zh: 由各路州县征发与编练，承担地方防务、运输护卫及战时集中作战，是兵员主体。
description.en: Raised and drilled by the provinces and districts, these troops handled local defense, convoy protection, and wartime concentration, forming the bulk of manpower.

description.zh: 依托红河、支流与沿海航道作战，擅长运输、伏击、截断补给与潮汐环境下的机动作战，是大越抵御北方入侵的重要优势兵种。
description.en: Operating on the Red River system and coastal waterways, these forces excelled in transport, ambush, supply interdiction, and maneuver in tidal environments, making them a major advantage against northern invaders.

description.zh: 由王侯私属、山地部族与边地武装组成，适合侦察、山地作战与地方机动，但训练与装备不甚统一。
description.en: Composed of princely retainers, upland tribal groups, and frontier fighters, they were useful for reconnaissance, mountain warfare, and local mobility, though less uniform in training and equipment.

commanderInChief.zh: 皇帝与太上皇，军事上由宗室名将辅佐
commanderInChief.en: The emperor and retired emperor, assisted by leading royal-clan generals

technology.zh: 军队以长矛、刀剑、弓弩、藤甲与铁甲、战船、火攻器材及攻守城器械为主。其技术水平不及蒙古的草原骑射体系，但在河网作战、设伏、筑垒、后勤和热湿环境适应方面极具优势。
technology.en: The military relied on spears, swords, bows and crossbows, rattan and iron armor, war boats, incendiaries, and siege equipment. Its technology was less formidable than Mongol steppe cavalry systems, but it held major advantages in riverine warfare, ambush, fortification, logistics, and adaptation to hot, humid terrain.

threats.zh: 首要威胁来自北方的元朝与蒙古帝国扩张。大越面临再次入侵的高风险，同时南方占城关系时有紧张，边地山民与交通脆弱性也构成次要安全问题。
threats.en: The primary threat came from the Yuan dynasty and wider Mongol imperial expansion to the north. Đại Việt faced a high risk of renewed invasion, while relations with Champa in the south could also be tense, and upland unrest and transport vulnerability posed secondary security concerns.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于红河三角洲及其周边稻作平原，山区与边疆分布较疏。人口结构年轻，劳动力充足，适合农业扩张与战时动员，但也依赖治水、堤防和粮食调配来维持密集聚居。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly in the Red River delta and adjacent rice plains, with much sparser settlement in uplands and frontier zones. It was relatively young and labor-rich, favorable for agricultural expansion and wartime mobilization, but dense settlement depended heavily on water control, dikes, and grain distribution.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为皇族、王侯、文武高官与地方豪族；中间层包括中下级官员、僧侣、富农、工匠与商人；底层主要为自耕农、佃农、雇工、舟人及少量奴婢。村社内部虽有贫富差异，但共同承担赋役和水利责任。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the imperial clan, princes, senior civil and military officials, and local magnates. Middle groups included lesser officials, monks, prosperous peasants, artisans, and merchants. The lower strata were mainly smallholders, tenants, laborers, boatmen, and a smaller servile population. Although villages showed internal inequalities, they collectively bore tax, labor, and hydraulic obligations.

allies.zh: 无正式大国盟友；与南方部分海上商人网络、地方山地首领及在特定时期与占城存在务实合作空间
allies.en: No formal great-power allies; there was practical cooperation with maritime trading networks, certain upland chiefs, and at times room for pragmatic understanding with Champa

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量为元朝及其蒙古军事体系；与占城并非长期绝对敌对，但边境与海上关系常有摩擦
enemies.en: The principal hostile power was the Yuan dynasty and its Mongol military system; Champa was not a permanent outright enemy, but border and maritime frictions were frequent

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交上采取灵活现实主义：对北方强权在名义朝贡与实际自主之间谨慎周旋，同时巩固南方与海上联系、经营边地首领网络，并为可能到来的蒙古战争争取时间与战略纵深。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was flexible and pragmatic: toward the northern great power it maneuvered carefully between nominal tributary forms and real autonomy, while strengthening southern and maritime ties, cultivating frontier-chief networks, and buying time and strategic depth against probable Mongol war.

era.zh: 中世纪后期东南亚—东亚接触时代
era.en: Late medieval Southeast Asian–East Asian contact era

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的稻作水利与堤坝体系、河海兼用船只建造、铜铁器生产、科举官僚技术、文书行政体系，以及适应三角洲湿地环境的军事后勤与水军战法。
keyInnovations.en: Key strengths included mature rice-hydraulic and dike systems, construction of river-sea capable vessels, bronze and iron production, examination-based bureaucratic techniques, documentary administration, and military logistics and naval methods adapted to delta wetlands.

strengths.zh: 大越的优势在于人口稠密而组织严密的红河核心区、成熟的水稻经济、较强的王朝凝聚力、宗室将领体系以及擅长江河湿地作战的水陆联防能力。国家虽不如元朝庞大，却具备韧性、防御纵深和动员效率。
strengths.en: Đại Việt's strengths lay in its densely populated and well-organized Red River core, mature rice economy, strong dynastic cohesion, royal-clan command structure, and integrated land-river defense well suited to wetland warfare. Though far smaller than the Yuan, it possessed resilience, defensive depth, and efficient mobilization.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点是国土规模与资源远逊于蒙古—元帝国，骑兵力量薄弱，财政货币化有限，政权对红河平原的粮食与堤防依赖极高。一旦遭大规模入侵、洪涝失控或运输线受扰，国力会迅速承压。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were its much smaller territorial and resource base compared with the Mongol-Yuan empire, limited cavalry capacity, only partial monetization, and heavy dependence on the grain and dike system of the Red River plain. Under major invasion, flood mismanagement, or disruption of transport lines, the realm could come under severe strain.

outlook.zh: 1280年的大越总体稳定，但已处于大战前夜。若朝廷能继续维持皇族团结、灵活外交、坚壁清野与水军机动，其独立极有可能得以保全；若内部争权或后勤失序，则将面临来自元朝的生存性危机。
outlook.en: In 1280 Đại Việt was broadly stable, but it stood on the eve of major war. If the court maintained royal unity, flexible diplomacy, scorched-earth and elastic defense measures, and naval mobility, its independence had a strong chance of survival; if internal rivalry or logistical breakdown emerged, it would face an existential threat from the Yuan.

description.zh: 红河流域王朝国家，在蒙古入侵威胁下保持独立。
description.en: The Red River state remained independent under the Trần dynasty despite Mongol threats.

governmentForm.zh: 以国王为核心、由沿海诸邦与地方首领共同构成的曼荼罗式君主制
governmentForm.en: A mandala-style monarchy centered on the king, with coastal principalities and local chiefs integrated into royal authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族与贵族、婆罗门祭司、地方领主、商人、工匠、自由农民、渔民以及数量有限的依附人口组成；沿海港口社会比内地更为开放且多族群并存。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal family and nobles, Brahman priests, local lords, merchants, artisans, free peasants, fishers, and a limited dependent population; coastal port society was more open and multiethnic than the interior.

rulingClass.zh: 占族王族、地方贵族、掌管祭祀与法统的婆罗门，以及控制港口与灌溉资源的地方首领
rulingClass.en: The Cham royal house, regional nobility, Brahmans who upheld ritual legitimacy, and local chiefs controlling ports and irrigation resources

succession.zh: 王位原则上由王族世袭，但实际继承常需获得贵族、宗教精英与地方势力认可，存在兄终弟及、旁支继承与共同统治等灵活做法。
succession.en: The throne was in principle hereditary within the royal house, but succession in practice required acceptance by nobles, religious elites, and regional powers, allowing flexible patterns such as fraternal succession, collateral inheritance, and co-rule.

structure.zh: 中央朝廷驻于毗阇耶，依托王室、宫廷官员、婆罗门顾问与地方领主治理；对各地区的控制并非完全官僚化，而是通过朝贡、婚姻、宗教合法性与军事威慑维系。
structure.en: The central court at Vijaya governed through the royal household, court officials, Brahman advisers, and regional lords; control over the various territories was not fully bureaucratic, but maintained through tribute, marriage alliances, religious legitimacy, and military coercion.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室土地、贡赋接收、使节接待与王命传达
function.en: Managed the court, royal lands, receipt of tribute, reception of envoys, and transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 主持湿婆与其他神祇祭祀、管理寺庙地产、起草梵文与占文碑铭以确认捐献和王权
function.en: Oversaw worship of Shiva and other deities, managed temple estates, and drafted Sanskrit and Cham inscriptions confirming donations and kingship

function.zh: 监管港口贸易、征收关税、管理外商、市舶停泊与海贸纠纷
function.en: Supervised port trade, collected customs dues, managed foreign merchants, anchorage, and maritime commercial disputes

function.zh: 统计地方贡赋、协调稻田水利、监督仓储与劳役征发
function.en: Recorded provincial tribute, coordinated irrigation for rice lands, and supervised storage and labor levies

function.zh: 统筹王军、战船、沿海防御、象队与地方征召武装
function.en: Coordinated the royal forces, war vessels, coastal defense, elephant corps, and regionally levied troops

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托各邦首领、港口长官、村社头人与寺庙网络。王权对核心地区控制较强，对边远山区与某些海湾港口则更多依赖盟属关系与定期朝贡。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on regional chiefs, port officials, village headmen, and temple networks. Royal authority was stronger in the core districts, while more distant uplands and some bayside ports were governed through alliance ties and periodic tribute.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王令、习惯法、宗教规范与地方裁断并行为主。土地、婚姻、捐献、债务与贸易纠纷常由地方首领、婆罗门或港口官员裁决，重大案件由国王或朝廷仲裁。
legalSystem.en: Law combined royal decrees, customary law, religious norms, and local adjudication. Disputes over land, marriage, donations, debt, and trade were often judged by local chiefs, Brahmans, or port officials, while major cases were arbitrated by the king or court.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自稻米地租、地方贡赋、港口关税、海贸抽分、王室土地收益和劳役义务；部分寺庙地产与贵族领地享有减免。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from rice land dues, regional tribute, port customs, levies on maritime trade, income from royal lands, and labor obligations; some temple estates and noble holdings enjoyed exemptions.

religion.zh: 以印度教尤其是湿婆崇拜为主，同时并存大乘佛教、地方祖灵与海神信仰；王权通过建庙、立碑和祭祀表现其神圣合法性。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Hindu, especially Shaivism, alongside Mahayana Buddhism and local ancestor and maritime spirit cults; kingship expressed its sacred legitimacy through temple building, inscriptions, and ritual.

philosophy.zh: 思想上受印度婆罗门传统影响深厚，强调王权、宇宙秩序、功德与祭祀，同时在实践中吸收海洋贸易社会的务实风格与地方传统。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was deeply influenced by Indian Brahmanical traditions, emphasizing kingship, cosmic order, merit, and ritual, while in practice incorporating the pragmatism of a maritime trading society and local traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 使用占文书写本地语言，并以梵文用于正式碑铭、宗教颂辞与王室合法性表达；文字源自南印度婆罗米系文字传统。
writingSystem.en: The Cham language was written in the Cham script, while Sanskrit was used for formal inscriptions, religious eulogies, and expressions of royal legitimacy; the scripts derived from the South Indian Brahmi tradition.

culturalAchievements.zh: 占婆以红砖塔庙建筑、石雕神像、梵占双语碑铭、海洋贸易文化与精致金属工艺著称。美山圣地传统延续，毗阇耶地区寺塔与宫廷艺术在13世纪仍具活力。
culturalAchievements.en: Champa was known for its red-brick temple towers, stone sculpture, bilingual Sanskrit-Cham inscriptions, maritime mercantile culture, and refined metalwork. The sacred tradition of Mỹ Sơn endured, and the temples and court art of the Vijaya region remained vibrant in the 13th century.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系，占语支
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Chamic branch

gdpDescription.zh: 占婆经济规模在东南亚属中等，以沿海稻作、渔业、林产与转口贸易支撑。财富集中于王室、港口商人和宗教地产，整体人均水平不高但沿海贸易节点较富裕。
gdpDescription.en: The Champa economy was medium-sized by Southeast Asian standards, supported by coastal rice cultivation, fishing, forest products, and entrepôt trade. Wealth was concentrated in the royal house, port merchants, and religious estates; overall per capita output was modest, but coastal trade nodes were relatively prosperous.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、渔业、椰子与槟榔种植、沉香与林木采集、制盐、陶器、织造、造船、港口贸易与象牙及贵重木材转运
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, fishing, coconut and areca cultivation, agarwood and timber collection, salt production, pottery, weaving, shipbuilding, port commerce, and the transit trade in ivory and valuable woods

tradeGoods.zh: 出口沉香、香料转口品、象牙、犀角、乌木、奴婢、海产、盐与手工艺品；进口中国陶瓷、丝绸、金属器、印度棉布、珠宝、马匹及宗教用品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included agarwood, re-exported spices, ivory, rhinoceros horn, ebony, slaves, marine products, salt, and handicrafts; imports included Chinese ceramics, silk, metal goods, Indian cotton textiles, jewelry, horses, and ritual items.

householdWealth.zh: 普通乡村家庭财富主要体现为稻田使用权、房屋、渔具、役畜与亲族网络；港口家庭可能持有舟船、货物存货与外来陶瓷、金属器等较高价值财产。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary rural household wealth consisted mainly of use-rights to rice land, houses, fishing gear, draft animals, and kinship networks; port households might own boats, trade inventories, and higher-value goods such as imported ceramics and metalware.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业与港口贸易并重的宫廷—地方混合经济，王室通过贡赋、港税和对战略港口的控制分享商业利润，但私人商人、外来商帮与地方首领也拥有显著经济自主性。
economicSystem.en: A mixed court-regional economy combining agriculture and port trade, in which the royal house shared in commercial profits through tribute, customs, and control of strategic ports, while private merchants, foreign trading groups, and local chiefs retained significant autonomy.

source.zh: 王室土地、林产与其他收入
source.en: Royal lands, forest products, and other income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以贵金属、稻谷储备、珍贵香木、进口奢侈品及可临时征调的地方贡赋承诺构成，不完全是统一铸币储备。稳定时期财政尚可，但一旦遭遇外敌或港口贸易中断，流动性会迅速下降。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metal, grain reserves, valuable aromatics, imported luxury goods, and pledges of tribute that could be called in from the regions, rather than a purely monetized reserve. In stable times finances were sound, but liquidity could decline quickly if faced with invasion or disruption of port trade.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持宫廷威望、海防与港口秩序，避免对地方首领和商人课以过重负担；在稳定年份倾向保有小幅结余，以备对抗大越或支持边地忠诚。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored maintaining court prestige, maritime defense, and port order while avoiding excessive burdens on regional lords and merchants; in stable years the state preferred a modest surplus to prepare for conflict with Đại Việt or to sustain frontier loyalty.

description.zh: 直属国王的核心常备部队，负责都城防卫、宫廷安全与战时中军。
description.en: The core standing troops directly attached to the king, responsible for capital defense, palace security, and the battle center in wartime.

description.zh: 由各地首领按需提供，数量最多，适于守城、山地行军和一般作战，但训练与装备不均。
description.en: Raised by regional chiefs as needed, these were the most numerous forces, suitable for garrison duty, upland marches, and general warfare, though uneven in training and equipment.

description.zh: 象队与贵族随从用于冲击、威慑和指挥中枢保护，是王权威望的重要象征。
description.en: Elephant corps and noble retainers were used for shock action, intimidation, and protection of the command core, and were an important symbol of royal prestige.

description.zh: 以轻快战船和武装运输船为主，负责巡逻海岸、护航商路、拦截袭扰及支援两栖行动。
description.en: Composed mainly of light warcraft and armed transports, responsible for coastal patrol, convoy protection, raiding interception, and support for amphibious operations.

description.zh: 由港口居民、商帮护卫和地方守军组成，用于守卫仓库、码头与城寨。
description.en: Made up of port inhabitants, merchant guards, and local troops, they defended warehouses, quays, and fortified settlements.

commanderInChief.zh: 因陀罗跋摩五世国王

technology.zh: 军备以长矛、弓箭、刀剑、皮甲与少量金属甲为主，配合战象和沿海船只。其海上机动能力强于重装攻城能力，对抗蒙古式骑射军并无明显优势，但熟悉热带地形与海岸补给。
technology.en: Military technology centered on spears, bows, swords, leather armor, and limited metal armor, combined with war elephants and coastal vessels. Its maritime mobility was stronger than its heavy siege capacity, and it held no clear advantage against Mongol-style mounted archery, though it was well adapted to tropical terrain and coastal logistics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方陈朝大越的边境压力、吴哥势力在湄公河与内陆通道方向的竞争，以及1280年前后元朝扩张对整个南海与中南半岛政治格局造成的潜在震荡。
threats.en: Principal threats came from border pressure by Trần Đại Việt in the north, competition from Angkor over Mekong and inland routes, and the broader destabilizing impact of Yuan expansion around 1280 on the South China Sea and mainland Southeast Asia.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在中南部沿海平原、河谷和港口地带，山地人口较少且族群多样。总体人口规模足以维持区域性强国地位，但明显小于同时期的大越与部分中国政权。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the central-southern coastal plains, river valleys, and port zones, while the uplands were more sparsely populated and ethnically diverse. The overall population was sufficient to sustain a regional power, though clearly smaller than contemporary Đại Việt and Chinese states.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族居于顶层，其下为婆罗门与寺庙精英、地方首领、商人与船主、工匠、自由农民和渔民，最底层为战俘、债役者及其他依附人口。港口地区外来华人、马来人、印度商人等形成活跃侨商群体。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal family and nobility, followed by Brahmans and temple elites, local chiefs, merchants and shipowners, artisans, free peasants, and fishers, with war captives, debt dependents, and other subordinated people at the bottom. In port areas, immigrant Chinese, Malay, and Indian merchants formed active diaspora communities.

allies.zh: 无固定条约型盟友，但在对抗大越与制衡吴哥时，常寻求与南方地方势力、海上商人网络及因局势而定的周边政权保持合作。
allies.en: There were no fixed treaty-style allies, but in resisting Đại Việt and balancing Angkor, Champa often sought cooperation with southern regional powers, maritime merchant networks, and neighboring states according to circumstances.

enemies.zh: 主要宿敌为陈朝大越；与吴哥关系长期竞争多于和睦。对元朝则既警惕又谨慎，不愿直接激怒其海上与陆上力量。
enemies.en: Its principal rival was Trần Đại Việt; relations with Angkor were more often competitive than amicable. Toward the Yuan, Champa was wary and cautious, unwilling to provoke its maritime and land power directly.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以平衡北方大越、牵制吴哥、维护海贸通道与保存王国自主为核心。占婆倾向利用朝贡、婚姻、使节往来和港口开放政策，在强邻之间争取回旋空间。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on balancing northern Đại Việt, containing Angkor, preserving trade routes, and safeguarding the kingdom’s autonomy. Champa tended to use tribute, marriage ties, embassies, and open-port policies to create room for maneuver among stronger neighbors.

era.zh: 13世纪中后期东南亚海陆混合技术时代
era.en: Late 13th-century Southeast Asian maritime-agrarian technological era

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的砖塔营造工艺、海船与沿岸航海技术、灌溉稻作、石雕与金属工艺，以及对印度洋—南海贸易知识的灵活吸收。虽然并非技术创新中心，但在海上适应能力和跨文化转译方面表现突出。
keyInnovations.en: Key strengths included mature brick temple construction, seafaring and coastal navigation, irrigated rice agriculture, stone carving and metalworking, and flexible adoption of knowledge from the Indian Ocean and South China Sea trade. It was not a major center of innovation, but was notably capable in maritime adaptation and cross-cultural transmission.

strengths.zh: 占婆的优势在于海上位置优越、港口网络活跃、文化整合能力强，以及能够在分散政治结构下迅速动员地方资源。其王权深植于宗教合法性，且拥有丰富的海贸联系与区域外交经验。
strengths.en: Champa’s strengths lay in its advantageous maritime position, active port network, strong cultural integrative capacity, and ability to mobilize regional resources quickly within a decentralized political structure. Its kingship was deeply rooted in religious legitimacy and backed by extensive trade ties and regional diplomatic experience.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于人口与资源基础有限，政治结构分散，中央对各邦控制力时强时弱；陆上持续作战能力不足，易受大越等人口更密集政权压制，且一旦港口受扰财政即明显受损。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a limited demographic and resource base, a politically fragmented structure, and uneven central control over the constituent polities; its endurance in sustained land warfare was weak, making it vulnerable to denser states such as Đại Việt, while disruption of its ports could quickly damage state finances.

outlook.zh: 1280年前后的占婆总体稳定，但正处于高风险地缘环境之中。若能维持港口贸易、谨慎应对元朝并继续牵制大越，王国可保持区域影响力；若遭遇北方入侵或被卷入蒙古相关战争，其脆弱的财政与分邦结构将面临严峻考验。
outlook.en: Around 1280, Champa was broadly stable but operating in a high-risk geopolitical environment. If it could preserve port trade, respond cautiously to the Yuan, and continue to balance Đại Việt, it could retain regional influence; if faced with northern invasion or drawn into Mongol-related wars, its fragile finances and segmented polity would come under severe strain.

description.zh: 越南中南部沿海的海贸王国，与大越和高棉周旋。
description.en: A maritime kingdom on the central Vietnamese coast balancing against Dai Viet and the Khmer.

governmentForm.zh: 伊斯兰君主制，由柏柏尔马林王族统治，结合宫廷苏丹权威、部族军事支持与马立克派法学官僚体系
governmentForm.en: An Islamic monarchy ruled by the Berber Marinid house, combining court-centered sultanic authority, tribal military backing, and a Maliki legal-bureaucratic order

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族与军事贵族、宗教学者与法官、城市商人与手工业者、部族牧民、乡村农民以及受保护的犹太与基督教少数群体构成；阿拉伯化城市精英与柏柏尔部族结构并存
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and military elites, religious scholars and judges, urban merchants and artisans, tribal pastoralists, rural cultivators, and protected Jewish and Christian minorities; Arabized urban elites coexisted with Berber tribal structures

rulingClass.zh: 马林王族、柏柏尔部族首领、高级军政官员、宫廷近臣、马立克派乌里玛与大法官集团
rulingClass.en: The Marinid royal family, Berber tribal chiefs, senior military and administrative officials, court retainers, and the Maliki ulama headed by leading qadis

succession.zh: 王朝世袭继承，通常在马林王族男子中传承，但实际即位往往取决于宫廷支持、军队拥护与宗教合法性认可
succession.en: Dynastic hereditary succession, usually within the male Marinid line, though actual accession often depended on court backing, army support, and recognition of religious legitimacy

structure.zh: 中央政权以苏丹宫廷为核心，下设掌印文书机构、财政征收机构、军务与边防机构、司法与宗教职位，以及各主要城市和地区的总督治理体系。行政制度较前阿尔摩哈德时期更依赖王朝家臣、书吏与地方总督的组合治理。
structure.en: The central state was organized around the sultan’s court, with chancery and seal offices, fiscal collection bodies, military and frontier commands, judicial and religious posts, and a provincial governorship system for major cities and regions. Compared with the earlier Almohads, administration relied more on a combination of dynastic retainers, scribes, and provincial governors.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保管国玺、处理外交文书、任命官员并传达苏丹命令
function.en: Drafted decrees, kept the state seal, handled diplomatic correspondence, appointed officials, and transmitted the sultan’s orders

function.zh: 征收土地税、商业关税、市场税与宗教性赋税，管理国库、军饷和皇家地产收入
function.en: Collected land taxes, commercial customs, market dues, and religiously framed levies, and managed the treasury, army pay, and revenue from crown lands

function.zh: 组织部族骑兵、守备军、港口防御与对安达卢斯远征的后勤
function.en: Organized tribal cavalry, garrison troops, port defense, and logistics for expeditions into al-Andalus

function.zh: 任命卡迪、监督马立克派法学执行、管理清真寺、宗教基金与部分教育机构
function.en: Appointed qadis, supervised the application of Maliki jurisprudence, and managed mosques, religious endowments, and some educational institutions

function.zh: 治理非斯、马拉喀什、梅克内斯、休达、丹吉尔等要地，负责治安、税收与城防
function.en: Administered key centers such as Fez, Marrakesh, Meknes, Ceuta, and Tangier, overseeing public order, tax collection, and urban defense

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由总督、城长、税吏、法官与部族协商机制共同构成。城市由苏丹任命官员直接控制较强，乡村和山地则更多依赖部族首领、盟约与巡回征税。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out by governors, town officials, tax collectors, judges, and negotiated arrangements with tribal authorities. Cities were more directly controlled by officials appointed by the sultan, while rural plains and mountain zones relied more on tribal leaders, compacts, and itinerant tax collection.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以逊尼派马立克学派伊斯兰法为核心，辅以苏丹敕令、部族习惯与行政裁断。城市司法较成文化，边远地区则常见沙里亚与习惯法并用。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was centered on Sunni Maliki Islamic law, supplemented by sultanic decrees, tribal custom, and administrative judgment. Urban justice was more formalized, while remote areas often combined sharia with customary law.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括农业地租与土地税、市场税、商队与港口关税、牧群征收、战时非常贡赋以及皇家庄园收入。沿海贸易关税在繁荣时期尤其重要。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation included agrarian rents and land taxes, market dues, caravan and port customs, pastoral levies, extraordinary wartime exactions, and income from royal domains. Customs duties from coastal trade were especially important in prosperous years.

religion.zh: 主流宗教为逊尼派伊斯兰教，法学上属马立克学派；苏菲实践与圣徒崇敬在民间持续扩展，犹太社群在城市商业与手工业中具有重要地位。
religion.en: The dominant religion was Sunni Islam of the Maliki school; Sufi practice and saint veneration continued to expand among the populace, while Jewish communities held important roles in urban commerce and crafts.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以伊斯兰法学、神学、阿拉伯文学修养和政治伦理为主，同时承续西马格里布与安达卢斯知识传统，重视学者赞助与宗教正统。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by Islamic jurisprudence, theology, Arabic literary culture, and political ethics, while continuing the western Maghribi and Andalusi scholarly tradition with strong emphasis on patronage of scholars and religious orthodoxy.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为阿拉伯字母，用于行政、公文、宗教、商业和文学；柏柏尔口语广泛存在，但正式书面文化以阿拉伯语为主。
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was the Arabic script, used for administration, state documents, religion, commerce, and literature; Berber vernaculars were widely spoken, but formal written culture was chiefly Arabic.

culturalAchievements.zh: 马林时期推动非斯成为宗教与学术中心，扩建清真寺、经学院与城防设施；建筑与工艺继承安达卢斯-马格里布传统，在木雕、灰泥装饰、瓷砖拼饰和城市建筑方面表现突出。
culturalAchievements.en: The Marinid period strengthened Fez as a center of religion and learning through the expansion of mosques, madrasas, and fortifications; architecture and crafts continued the Andalusi-Maghribi tradition, excelling in carved wood, stucco, tilework, and urban building.

languageFamily.zh: 阿拉伯语属亚非语系闪米特语族，为高地位书面与宗教语言；柏柏尔诸语言属亚非语系柏柏尔语支，为广泛口语基础。
languageFamily.en: Arabic, a Semitic branch of the Afroasiatic family, served as the prestige written and religious language; Berber languages, of the Berber branch of Afroasiatic, formed a broad vernacular foundation.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、城市手工业和跨地中海贸易为支柱。1280年前后王朝控制摩洛哥核心地区并经营海峡贸易，整体处于繁荣阶段，但财富分布极不均衡，且高度依赖政治稳定与商路安全。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, pastoralism, urban crafts, and trans-Mediterranean trade. Around 1280 the dynasty controlled Morocco’s core regions and benefited from Strait commerce; the economy was broadly thriving, though wealth was highly unevenly distributed and strongly dependent on political stability and route security.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物农业、橄榄与果园种植、牧羊牧马、皮革加工、纺织、金属器制造、陶器、木工、制糖与港口贸易服务
mainIndustries.en: Cereal agriculture, olive and orchard cultivation, sheep and horse raising, leatherworking, textiles, metalware, ceramics, woodworking, sugar processing, and port trade services

tradeGoods.zh: 出口皮革、羊毛织物、马匹、蜡、皮货、糖、铜器和部分金属制品；进口安达卢斯与欧洲纺织品、木材、武器、奢侈品以及撒哈拉黄金与奴隶贸易转运货物
tradeGoods.en: Exports included leather, woolen textiles, horses, wax, hides, sugar, copper goods, and some metal manufactures; imports included Andalusi and European cloth, timber, weapons, luxury goods, and trans-Saharan gold and slave-trade transit commodities

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人、官员和大地主拥有明显高于平均水平的财富，而多数农户与牧民仅维持有限剩余。沿海商业城市和非斯的富裕程度显著高于内陆边缘地区。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchants, officials, and large landholders possessed wealth well above the average, while most peasant households and pastoralists maintained only modest surpluses. Coastal commercial towns and Fez were markedly richer than marginal inland regions.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租、市场交换和商路关税为基础的前近代混合经济，国家对港口、税收和军需有较强干预，但日常生产主要由村社、部族网络和城市行会式手工业者完成
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy based on agrarian rents, market exchange, and customs from trade routes, with strong state involvement in ports, taxation, and military supply, while everyday production was carried out chiefly by village communities, tribal networks, and guild-like urban artisans

source.zh: 市场税、牧群征收与杂项贡赋
source.en: Market dues, pastoral levies, and miscellaneous imposts

category.en: Army, frontier defense, and Strait expeditions

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库保持相对充裕，得益于海峡贸易、农业税基恢复与对重要城市的控制，但在持续对伊比利亚干预和部族安抚支出下，财政仍容易因军事失利或港口收入波动而承压。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was comparatively healthy thanks to Strait commerce, a recovering agrarian tax base, and control of major cities, but continued intervention in Iberia and the cost of tribal appeasement meant finances could still come under pressure from military setbacks or fluctuations in port income.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守积储与军事优先，在繁荣年份维持盈余并扩大国库储备，同时通过关税、皇家地产和临时征收支持边境战争与王朝建设。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored cautious accumulation and military priority, maintaining surpluses in prosperous years and enlarging treasury reserves while using customs, royal domains, and occasional extraordinary levies to fund frontier war and dynastic consolidation.

description.zh: 军队核心，主要由马林及其盟属柏柏尔部族组成，机动性强，适用于快速突袭、边境作战和伊比利亚登陆支援。
description.en: The core of the army, drawn mainly from the Marinids and allied Berber tribes, highly mobile and suited to raiding, frontier warfare, and support for landings in Iberia.

description.zh: 由城市守备、宫廷卫队和受薪步兵构成，用于守城、攻城与维持治安。
description.en: Composed of urban garrisons, palace guards, and salaried infantry, used for garrison duty, siege work, and internal order.

description.zh: 包括步弓手和轻装机动部队，常与骑兵配合作战，在山地和围城战中有用。
description.en: Included foot archers and lightly equipped mobile troops, often operating with cavalry and useful in mountain warfare and sieges.

description.zh: 规模有限但战略重要，负责休达、丹吉尔等港口防御、运兵与对安达卢斯海上支援。
description.en: Limited in size but strategically important, responsible for the defense of ports such as Ceuta and Tangier, troop transport, and maritime support to al-Andalus.

commanderInChief.zh: 苏丹阿布·优素福·雅古布

technology.zh: 军事技术以马格里布-安达卢斯传统为主，包括精良骑兵装备、复合弓与步弓、攻城器械、城防工事和有限舰船运兵能力；尚未形成如东方火药军制那样的体系。
technology.en: Military technology was rooted in Maghribi-Andalusi traditions, including effective cavalry equipment, composite and foot bows, siege engines, fortifications, and limited naval transport capacity; it had not developed anything like the gunpowder military systems of the later East.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自伊比利亚基督教诸国，尤其是卡斯蒂利亚；其次是与哈夫斯王朝在马格里布东部的竞争、国内部族叛乱、山地难治区域以及海峡航路安全问题。
threats.en: The main threats came from the Christian kingdoms of Iberia, especially Castile; secondary dangers included rivalry with the Hafsid dynasty in the eastern Maghrib, internal tribal revolts, difficult mountain regions, and the security of the Strait routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于非斯周边、北部平原、大西洋沿岸与若干传统灌溉农业区。民族和语言构成多元，以柏柏尔与阿拉伯化居民为主，城市中有重要犹太社群，部分安达卢斯移民也增强了工艺与学术活力。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly around Fez, the northern plains, the Atlantic littoral, and established irrigated agricultural zones. It was ethnically and linguistically diverse, dominated by Berber and Arabized inhabitants, with important Jewish communities in the towns and some Andalusi migrants adding to craft and scholarly vitality.

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括苏丹家族、部族军事贵族、总督、富商和高级乌里玛；中层由法官、书吏、商人、工匠师傅和中等地主组成；下层为农民、佃户、牧民、雇工、奴隶与城市贫民。犹太社群在贸易、金融与工艺领域占有稳定位置。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the sultan’s family, tribal military nobles, governors, wealthy merchants, and senior ulama; the middle strata consisted of judges, scribes, merchants, master artisans, and middling landholders; the lower strata included peasants, tenants, pastoralists, laborers, slaves, and the urban poor. Jewish communities held a stable place in trade, finance, and crafts.

allies.zh: 主要盟友为纳斯里德格拉纳达，双方因共同面对伊比利亚基督教扩张而合作；也会与部分安达卢斯穆斯林势力和跨海峡商人网络维持实用性关系。
allies.en: Its principal ally was Nasrid Granada, with cooperation driven by the shared challenge of Christian expansion in Iberia; it also maintained practical ties with some Andalusi Muslim factions and trans-Strait merchant networks.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手是卡斯蒂利亚王国，并与葡萄牙、阿拉贡存在潜在或间歇性冲突；在马格里布内部则与哈夫斯伊夫里基亚保持竞争关系。
enemies.en: Its chief enemy was the Kingdom of Castile, while Portugal and Aragon posed potential or intermittent opposition; within the Maghrib it remained in rivalry with Hafsid Ifriqiya.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制直布罗陀海峡、干预安达卢斯局势、扶持穆斯林盟友并维持摩洛哥内部统一为核心。王朝通过军事远征、港口控制、联姻和贸易保护来巩固其西马格里布霸权。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling the Strait of Gibraltar, intervening in Andalusi affairs, supporting Muslim allies, and preserving internal unity in Morocco. The dynasty sought to consolidate western Maghribi hegemony through military expeditions, port control, marriage ties, and protection of trade.

era.zh: 中世纪盛期，处于蒙古帝国时代的西地中海伊斯兰世界
era.en: High Middle Ages, within the western Mediterranean Islamic world during the age of the Mongol Empire

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与实践包括精细灌溉农业、安达卢斯-马格里布建筑技术、皮革与纺织工艺、城防建设、抄写与法学教育网络，以及连接撒哈拉、马格里布和伊比利亚的成熟商贸组织能力。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and practices included refined irrigated agriculture, Andalusi-Maghribi architectural techniques, leather and textile craftsmanship, fortification building, manuscript and legal-educational networks, and mature commercial organization linking the Sahara, the Maghrib, and Iberia.

strengths.zh: 王朝优势在于控制摩洛哥核心农业区和海峡港口，拥有强机动性的部族骑兵，财政受益于海上与跨撒哈拉贸易，并可借安达卢斯事务提升宗教与政治合法性。
strengths.en: The dynasty’s strengths lay in controlling Morocco’s core agrarian zones and Strait ports, fielding highly mobile tribal cavalry, drawing fiscal benefit from maritime and trans-Saharan trade, and enhancing political and religious legitimacy through intervention in Andalusi affairs.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于国家整合程度有限，对部族忠诚和个人统治能力依赖较大，地方征税不够均衡，海军能力相对有限，且长期跨海作战容易耗费财政并激化内部不稳。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included limited state integration, heavy dependence on tribal loyalty and the ruler’s personal authority, uneven local taxation, relatively modest naval capacity, and the high fiscal and political cost of sustained overseas warfare.

outlook.zh: 1280年前后的马林王朝前景总体积极，正处于扩张与繁荣阶段，并有能力继续影响伊比利亚与西马格里布局势；但若对外战争受挫或王位继承失序，其财政与部族联盟结构将迅速承压。
outlook.en: Around 1280 the Marinid Sultanate’s outlook was generally favorable, as it was in a phase of expansion and prosperity and remained capable of influencing both Iberia and the western Maghrib; however, foreign military reverses or disorderly succession could quickly strain its finances and tribal alliance structure.

description.zh: 控制摩洛哥的柏柏尔王朝，并积极干预伊比利亚事务。
description.en: A Berber dynasty ruling Morocco and actively intervening in Iberian affairs.

ruler.zh: 阿里·本·哈桑后裔诸苏丹
ruler.en: Sultans of the line of Ali ibn al-Hasan

description.zh: 东非斯瓦希里海岸的贸易枢纽，连接印度洋金、象牙与奴隶贸易。
description.en: A Swahili coast trade hub linking the Indian Ocean networks of gold, ivory, and slave commerce.

name.zh: 哈夫斯王朝伊夫里基叶

ruler.zh: 阿布·伊斯哈格·易卜拉欣一世

governmentForm.zh: 以马格里布伊斯兰君主制为核心的苏丹-哈里发政体，结合王朝统治、伊斯兰法学权威与城市行政网络
governmentForm.en: A sultan-caliphal monarchy of the Maghrebi Islamic world, combining dynastic rule, Islamic legal authority, and an urban administrative network

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与宫廷精英、阿拉伯与柏柏尔军事部族、乌里玛与法官、城市商人、工匠、乡村农民、游牧群体以及奴隶与释奴构成；沿海城市社会流动性较强，内陆则更多依赖部族与地方名望家族
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal household and court elite, Arab and Berber military tribes, ulama and judges, urban merchants, artisans, rural cultivators, nomadic groups, and slaves and freedmen; coastal cities were relatively more mobile, while the interior relied more on tribes and local notable families

rulingClass.zh: 哈夫斯王族、宫廷军政官员、主要城市中的马立克派法学家与商人名流，以及与王朝结盟的阿拉伯和柏柏尔部族首领
rulingClass.en: The Hafsid royal family, court military and administrative officials, Maliki jurists and mercantile notables in the major cities, and Arab and Berber tribal chiefs allied to the dynasty

succession.zh: 王朝世袭为原则，通常由在位苏丹指定或默认的王子继承，但常受宫廷派系、宗室竞争与地方军政实力影响
succession.en: Succession was dynastic and hereditary in principle, usually passing to a designated or accepted prince, though often shaped by court factions, dynastic rivalry, and provincial military power

structure.zh: 中央政府以苏丹宫廷为核心，依靠掌玺与书记官机构、财政官署、军务官、港务与市场监督体系，以及由马立克派卡迪与地方总督构成的省级行政网络运作
structure.en: The central government was centered on the sultan's court and operated through chancery and seal offices, fiscal bureaus, military officials, port and market supervision, and a provincial network of Maliki qadis and governors

function.zh: 起草诏令、外交文书、任命文书与王室档案，维系中央命令的合法性与传达
function.en: Drafted decrees, diplomatic letters, appointments, and royal records, sustaining the legitimacy and transmission of central commands

function.zh: 征收土地税、市场税、关税、牧税与王室地产收入，管理国库与支出拨付
function.en: Collected land tax, market dues, customs, pasture taxes, and crown estate income, and managed the treasury and disbursements

function.zh: 登记常备军、统筹部族征召、军饷、军械、马匹与边防驻军
function.en: Registered the standing forces and coordinated tribal levies, military pay, arms, horses, and frontier garrisons

function.zh: 管理突尼斯、比贾亚贸易联系中的海关、航运登记、仓储与对基督教商人的课税
function.en: Managed customs, shipping registration, storage, and taxation of Christian merchants connected with the trade of Tunis and wider regional ports

function.zh: 协调卡迪、清真寺与宗教捐赠，监督马立克派法学秩序与公共道德事务
function.en: Coordinated qadis, mosques, and religious endowments, and supervised Maliki legal order and public moral affairs

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托省城总督、城镇长官、卡迪、税吏和市场监督官，并与部族酋长协商征税、治安与征兵；沿海城市较受中央直接控制，内陆与草原地区则需通过盟约和赐封维持效忠
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on provincial governors in major towns, urban officials, qadis, tax collectors, and market inspectors, while negotiating taxation, security, and levies with tribal chiefs; coastal cities were more directly controlled by the center, whereas inland and steppe zones were governed through alliance and patronage

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以逊尼派伊斯兰教法中的马立克学派为主，卡迪审理民商与家事案件，刑罚与治安事务则部分由苏丹法令、行政裁断与地方惯例共同处理
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based primarily on Sunni Islamic law in the Maliki school; qadis judged civil, commercial, and family cases, while criminal punishment and security matters were handled partly through sultanic decrees, administrative decision, and local custom

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括农业地租和土地税、牲畜税、市场摊税、商旅关税、港口海关、王室地产收益及非常时期摊派；对跨地中海贸易征税尤其重要
taxationSystem.en: The tax system included agrarian rents and land tax, livestock taxes, market stall dues, caravan tolls, port customs, crown estate income, and extraordinary levies in times of need; taxation on trans-Mediterranean trade was especially important

religion.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰教为国教，法学上以马立克学派占主导，同时存在苏非虔修传统；城市中亦有犹太人与少量基督徒商旅社群
religion.en: Sunni Islam was the state religion, with the Maliki school dominant in jurisprudence, alongside Sufi devotional traditions; urban centers also contained Jewish communities and small groups of Christian merchants

philosophy.zh: 思想文化以伊斯兰法学、古兰经注释、阿拉伯文文献教育和政治伦理为核心，兼受安达卢斯与马格里布学术传统影响，强调正统信仰、君主合法性与城市教养
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Islamic jurisprudence, Qur'anic commentary, Arabic literary education, and political ethics, influenced by Andalusi and Maghrebi scholarship and stressing orthodoxy, dynastic legitimacy, and urban refinement

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为阿拉伯字母，用于行政、法学、宗教与商业记载；部分柏柏尔语口头流通，但正式文书以阿拉伯文为主
writingSystem.en: The main writing system was the Arabic script, used for administration, law, religion, and commerce; some Berber speech circulated orally, but formal documentation was overwhelmingly in Arabic

culturalAchievements.zh: 哈夫斯时期的伊夫里基叶在宗教教育、法学著述、清真寺与城防建筑、地中海商业文化以及城市手工业方面表现突出；突尼斯成为连接马格里布、安达卢斯、西西里与意大利商路的重要文化港口
culturalAchievements.en: Hafsid Ifriqiya stood out in religious learning, legal writing, mosque and urban fortification building, Mediterranean commercial culture, and city crafts; Tunis became an important cultural port linking the Maghreb with al-Andalus, Sicily, and the Italian trade routes

languageFamily.zh: 以闪米特语系的阿拉伯语为行政与高文化语言，同时存在阿非罗-亚细亚语系中的柏柏尔诸语及贸易环境中的罗曼语和地中海混合语接触
languageFamily.en: Arabic, of the Semitic branch, served as the administrative and high-cultural language, alongside Berber languages of the Afro-Asiatic family and contact with Romance and Mediterranean trade idioms

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、城市手工业和地中海贸易并重。沿海城市税收和转口贸易使伊夫里基叶在马格里布诸国中保持较强财政韧性，尽管其总体财富仍低于同时期的埃及或伊儿汗国核心区
gdpDescription.en: The economy combined agriculture, pastoralism, urban crafts, and Mediterranean trade. Coastal city revenues and entrepot commerce gave Ifriqiya relatively strong fiscal resilience among Maghrebi states, though its total wealth remained below that of contemporary Egypt or the core regions of the Ilkhanate

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄与油料、畜牧、皮革加工、纺织、陶器、金属手工业、造船修船、海运贸易与撒哈拉边缘商路转运
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, olives and oils, stock raising, leatherworking, textiles, ceramics, metal crafts, shipbuilding and repair, maritime commerce, and redistribution linked to Saharan-edge caravan routes

tradeGoods.zh: 出口谷物、橄榄油、皮革、羊毛织物、蜡、盐、马匹与北非手工业品；进口木材、铁器、奴隶、香料、东方奢侈品、优质布匹及欧洲金属制品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included grain, olive oil, leather, woolen textiles, wax, salt, horses, and North African manufactures; imports included timber, iron goods, slaves, spices, eastern luxuries, fine cloth, and European metal wares

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人与学者家庭可积累相当房产、库存和金银器，乡村农户多以土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备和家内工具为主要财富；游牧群体财富主要体现为羊群、马匹和盟友关系
householdWealth.en: Urban merchant and scholarly households could accumulate significant real estate, inventories, and precious objects, while rural families held wealth mainly in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, and domestic tools; nomadic wealth was expressed chiefly through flocks, horses, and alliance networks

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换和税收财政为核心的前近代伊斯兰混合经济，国家直接控制有限，但通过港口、关税、王室地产、军政保护与法学信用体系深刻影响商业活动
economicSystem.en: A premodern Islamic mixed economy centered on market exchange and tax finance, with limited direct state control but strong state influence over commerce through ports, customs, crown lands, military protection, and legal-credit institutions

source.zh: 市场税、手工业税与城市杂税
source.en: Market dues, craft taxes, and urban imposts

source.zh: 部族贡赋、牲畜税与特别征收
source.en: Tribal tribute, livestock taxes, and extraordinary levies

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模对一个中等强国而言较为充足，足以应对短期叛乱、歉收或舰队修整，但若长期与马林王朝或大型海上势力作战，储备将迅速承压
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was substantial for a middling regional power, sufficient for short rebellions, harvest failures, or fleet refitting, but prolonged conflict with the Marinids or major naval powers would strain reserves quickly

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策倾向稳健，优先维持海关收入、稳定币值、安抚部族并保障城市供给；在局势稳定时避免过度榨取农业腹地，以保全长期税基
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally cautious, prioritizing customs revenue, monetary stability, tribal appeasement, and urban provisioning; in stable times it avoided excessive extraction from the countryside in order to preserve the long-term tax base

description.zh: 由王朝亲军、宫廷卫队及精锐骑兵构成，是首都与苏丹安全的核心力量
description.en: Composed of dynastic household troops, palace guards, and elite cavalry, forming the core force for the capital and the sultan's security

description.zh: 依靠阿拉伯与柏柏尔盟部动员，机动性强，适合内陆控制、追击与边境作战，但忠诚受政治关系影响
description.en: Mobilized from allied Arab and Berber tribes; highly mobile and suitable for inland control, pursuit, and frontier warfare, though loyalty depended on political relations

description.zh: 负责守城、港口与税站防务，装备以矛、盾、弓弩及部分甲胄为主
description.en: Responsible for defending towns, ports, and tax stations, armed mainly with spears, shields, bows or crossbows, and some armor

description.zh: 规模有限，主要承担护航、巡逻、港口防御和对海盗及敌对舰船的应急作战
description.en: Limited in size, mainly tasked with escort, patrol, port defense, and emergency action against pirates and hostile shipping

commanderInChief.zh: 阿布·伊斯哈格·易卜拉欣一世，日常军务由宫廷军务官与地方统兵长官分掌
commanderInChief.en: Abu Ishaq Ibrahim I, with day-to-day military affairs handled by court military officials and provincial commanders

technology.zh: 军备以马格里布传统骑兵战术、城防工事、弓箭与近战兵器为主，海军技术属于地中海中等水平；尚未形成火药武器的系统性军事应用
technology.en: Military technology relied on traditional Maghrebi cavalry tactics, fortifications, bows, and melee arms, while naval capabilities were middling by Mediterranean standards; gunpowder weapons had not yet entered systematic military use

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西方的马林王朝竞争、内陆部族叛服不常、沿海海盗活动，以及阿拉贡、西西里和意大利商贸势力在必要时可能施加的海上压力
threats.en: Main threats came from rivalry with the Marinid Sultanate to the west, inconsistent loyalty among inland tribes, coastal piracy, and possible maritime pressure from Aragonese, Sicilian, and Italian commercial powers when circumstances demanded

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于突尼斯及沿海平原、凯鲁万传统农业区、比塞大与萨赫勒地带，以及若干绿洲与内陆商路节点；总人口在13世纪后期属马格里布中较有分量的国家之一
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in Tunis and the coastal plains, the traditional agrarian zone around Kairouan, Bizerte and the Sahel, and several oasis and inland caravan nodes; by the late thirteenth century this made it one of the more substantial states of the Maghreb

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括王族、军政贵显、法学家、富商与地方名望者；中层为城市工匠、店主、小商人与中小地主；下层为农民、雇工、牧民、码头劳工及家内奴仆。部族身份与城市身份并行，是社会组织的重要分界
socialClasses.en: Upper strata included the royal house, military-administrative grandees, jurists, wealthy merchants, and local notables; middle groups consisted of artisans, shopkeepers, small traders, and middling landholders; lower groups included peasants, laborers, herders, dock workers, and household servants. Tribal and urban identities coexisted as major lines of social organization

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的正式长期盟友，但与部分安达卢斯穆斯林势力、地中海意大利商人集团以及若干部族盟伴保持务实合作
allies.en: There were no fully stable long-term formal allies, but the state maintained pragmatic cooperation with some Andalusi Muslim interests, Mediterranean Italian merchant groups, and various allied tribes

enemies.zh: 主要对手是西方的马林王朝；此外在海上需警惕阿拉贡及其他拉丁基督教势力的袭扰，在撒哈拉与内陆边缘则面临局部部族与商路竞争
enemies.en: The main rival was the Marinid Sultanate to the west; at sea the state had to guard against Aragonese and other Latin Christian incursions, while Saharan and inland margins saw localized tribal and trade-route competition

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持伊夫里基叶独立、控制沿海贸易、平衡马格里布诸势力并与欧洲商邦保持可税收的和平往来为中心；对东方伊斯兰世界保持宗教与名义联系，但战略重心在中西地中海
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving Ifriqiyan independence, controlling coastal trade, balancing Maghrebi powers, and maintaining taxable peace with European merchant communities; the state retained religious and nominal links with the eastern Islamic world, but its strategic focus lay in the central and western Mediterranean

era.zh: 中世纪后期伊斯兰-地中海时代
era.en: Late medieval Islamic-Mediterranean era

keyInnovations.zh: 其技术优势主要体现在灌溉与旱地农业结合、城墙与港口设施维护、地中海航海经验、法律文书行政化以及成熟的市场与信用实践，而非突破性的军事发明
keyInnovations.en: Its main technological strengths lay in combining irrigation with dry farming, maintaining walls and port installations, Mediterranean seamanship, routinized legal-administrative documentation, and mature market and credit practices rather than in breakthrough military inventions

strengths.zh: 地理位置优越，控制连接马格里布、撒哈拉边缘与地中海的关键枢纽；首都突尼斯富庶且开放；财政依靠海关和贸易较为稳定；宗教合法性与马立克派法学传统增强了统治凝聚力
strengths.en: It occupied an advantageous position controlling key links between the Maghreb, the Saharan fringe, and the Mediterranean; Tunis was prosperous and outward-looking; customs and trade gave relatively stable revenues; and religious legitimacy together with the Maliki legal tradition strengthened political cohesion

weaknesses.zh: 对部族合作与沿海贸易依赖较深，内陆控制力并不均衡；海军不足以长期挑战更强的海上势力；王朝继承与宗室竞争可能削弱中央；与马林王朝的竞争构成持续外部压力
weaknesses.en: It depended heavily on tribal cooperation and coastal trade, while control over the interior remained uneven; its navy was too weak to challenge stronger maritime powers for long; dynastic succession and intra-family rivalry could weaken the center; and competition with the Marinids created sustained external pressure

outlook.zh: 1280年前后，哈夫斯伊夫里基叶总体稳定，具备继续作为中北非重要商业与政治中心的条件。若能维持王位平稳继承、压制西方竞争者并保障港口安全，其繁荣尚可延续；若宫廷斗争加剧或海陆贸易受挫，则国势将逐步走向分裂与衰弱
outlook.en: Around 1280, Hafsid Ifriqiya was broadly stable and well placed to remain a major commercial and political center of central North Africa.
