succession.en: At the suzerain level succession was shaped by Chagataid power struggles, while locally officeholding depended on Mongol appointment, military leverage, family negotiation, and recognition by urban elites.

structure.zh: 政体并非统一中央集权国家，而是以布哈拉、撒马尔罕等绿洲城市为节点。蒙古达鲁花赤负责监督税赋、驿站与秩序，地方埃米尔和城市长老管理司法、灌溉、市场和宗教基金。
structure.en: The polity was not a single centralized state but a nodal system built around oasis cities such as Bukhara and Samarqand. Mongol darughachis supervised taxation, relay routes, and order, while local emirs and urban elders managed justice, irrigation, markets, and religious endowments.

function.zh: 征收地税、商业税、过境税与对蒙古宗主的贡赋，登记田亩、作坊和商队。
function.en: Collected land tax, commercial dues, transit tolls, and tribute owed to Mongol overlords, while registering fields, workshops, and caravans.

name.en: Bureau of Relay Posts and Roads

function.zh: 维护站赤体系、护送使者、保障商旅补给，并监督主要道路、桥梁与绿洲间水源点。
function.en: Maintained the yam relay system, escorted envoys, ensured supplies for merchants, and supervised major roads, bridges, and water points between oases.

function.zh: 管理集市秩序、度量衡、行会纠纷、外来商人登记及价格监督。
function.en: Managed bazaar order, weights and measures, guild disputes, registration of foreign merchants, and price oversight.

function.zh: 维修运河、分配灌溉水、协调农地界限，并在洪涝与干旱时组织劳役。
function.en: Repaired canals, allocated irrigation water, coordinated field boundaries, and organized labor during floods and droughts.

name.en: Bureau of Justice and Religious Affairs

function.zh: 协调伊斯兰法官、习惯法仲裁与蒙古命令执行，监管瓦克夫、清真寺与经学院。
function.en: Coordinated Islamic qadis, customary arbitration, and the enforcement of Mongol decrees, while overseeing waqf endowments, mosques, and madrasas.

name.en: Bureau of Garrison and City Defense

function.zh: 管理城门、城墙、武库、驻防士兵与地方征召力量。
function.en: Managed gates, walls, arsenals, garrison troops, and locally levied defensive forces.

function.zh: 以波斯语、突厥语和蒙古政令格式处理契约、账册、贸易执照与对外文书。
function.en: Handled contracts, ledgers, trade licenses, and foreign correspondence in Persian, Turkic, and Mongol administrative formats.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托城市议事圈、坊市首领、乡村灌溉共同体与宗教法官网络。每个绿洲周边村社在水利和税负上高度组织化，并与城内商税体系相连。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on urban consultative circles, quarter chiefs, village irrigation communities, and networks of religious judges. Rural settlements around each oasis were highly organized for water management and tax assessment and were linked closely to urban commercial taxation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律是蒙古敕令、伊斯兰教法和地方习惯法的混合体。商贸契约通常由伊斯兰法官和见证人确认，涉及军政命令、治安和贡赋的事务则受蒙古官员直接裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Mongol decrees, Islamic law, and local customary law. Commercial contracts were usually validated by qadis and witnesses, while matters involving military authority, public order, and tribute were judged directly by Mongol officials.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自灌溉农地税、园圃税、市场税、关卡税、商队过境费、作坊税与非常规摊派。蒙古统治初期较重的征敛在13世纪后期于若干城市趋于制度化，以维持商路繁荣。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from irrigated land taxes, orchard dues, market taxes, customs tolls, caravan transit fees, workshop assessments, and occasional extraordinary levies. The heavier extractions of the early Mongol period had become more regularized in several cities by the late thirteenth century in order to preserve trade prosperity.

religion.zh: 主流宗教为逊尼派伊斯兰教，特别是哈乃斐学派；同时仍可见苏菲团体、少量什叶派、景教基督徒、犹太商人社群与蒙古统治者相对宽容下的多元信仰共存。
religion.en: The dominant religion was Sunni Islam, especially the Hanafi school; Sufi groups, small Shi'i communities, Nestorian Christians, Jewish merchant communities, and a broader pluralism under relatively tolerant Mongol rule also remained present.

philosophy.zh: 思想核心为伊斯兰法学、神学与波斯化宫廷伦理，同时延续中亚城市的实用商贸文化、天文学兴趣和苏菲修行传统。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core lay in Islamic jurisprudence, theology, and Persianate courtly ethics, while continuing Central Asia's practical mercantile culture, interest in astronomy, and Sufi devotional traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写语言为波斯语与阿拉伯语，采用阿拉伯字母；行政和日常交流中也广泛使用突厥语，蒙古统治文书偶见蒙古文字或经波斯文转写的蒙古术语。
writingSystem.en: Persian and Arabic were the principal written languages, using the Arabic script; Turkic was also widely used in administration and daily exchange, while Mongol script or Mongol terminology transcribed into Persian appears in some official contexts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 尽管经历12至13世纪的战争破坏，布哈拉和撒马尔罕仍保持经学院、清真寺、书籍贸易、诗歌传统、纺织工艺、金属器制造和跨区域学术交流。城市文化表现为伊朗—突厥—蒙古元素的结合。
culturalAchievements.en: Despite the destruction of the twelfth and thirteenth centuries, Bukhara and Samarqand still sustained madrasas, mosques, book trade, poetic traditions, textile crafts, metalworking, and long-distance scholarly exchange. Urban culture reflected a blend of Iranian, Turkic, and Mongol elements.

languageFamily.zh: 以伊朗语族的波斯语和突厥语族诸方言并行为主，阿拉伯语作为宗教与学术语言具有重要地位。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic environment was dominated by Persian of the Iranian family alongside Turkic dialects of the Turkic family, with Arabic retaining major prestige as a religious and scholarly language.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉农业与跨欧亚商路服务业并重。人均财富高于许多纯农业地区，但整体仍受草原政治变动、运输风险和绿洲生态容量限制。
gdpDescription.en: The economy balanced irrigated agriculture with service activity tied to trans-Eurasian trade routes. Per capita wealth was higher than in many purely agrarian regions, though the whole system remained constrained by steppe political fluctuations, transport risks, and the ecological limits of oasis agriculture.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、棉纺与丝织、染色、皮革、金属加工、纸张与书籍贸易、驮运服务、驿站补给、货币兑换和长途批发贸易
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, cotton weaving and silk textiles, dyeing, leatherwork, metalworking, paper and book trade, pack-animal transport services, relay provisioning, money changing, and long-distance wholesale commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、丝绸、地毯、皮革、纸张、铜器、铁器、干果、葡萄制品、马匹、奴隶、香料转口品、中国丝货与波斯奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, silk, carpets, leather goods, paper, copperware, iron goods, dried fruits, grape products, horses, slaves, re-exported spices, Chinese silks, and Persian luxury items

name.en: Dirham and dinar in parallel use

householdWealth.zh: 城市富商和宗教基金掌握大量流动资本、货栈和地产；普通家庭财富多体现在住房、牲畜、工具、纺织设备和少量贵金属储蓄。乡村家庭依赖灌溉地、果园和公共水利权。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchant houses and religious endowments held substantial liquid capital, warehouses, and property; ordinary families stored wealth mainly in dwellings, livestock, tools, looms, and modest precious-metal savings. Rural households depended on irrigated plots, orchards, and communal water rights.

economicSystem.zh: 这是一种受蒙古帝国保护和抽取的绿洲市场经济：农业剩余通过城市集市、商队金融和长距离转口贸易实现变现，国家干预主要集中于税收、通行安全和驿传。
economicSystem.en: This was an oasis market economy protected and taxed by the Mongol imperial order: agrarian surplus was monetized through urban bazaars, caravan finance, and long-distance entrepôt trade, while state intervention focused mainly on revenue, route security, and relay logistics.

source.zh: 城市地产与瓦克夫相关收益

category.zh: 向蒙古上层的贡纳与馈赠
category.en: Tribute and remittances to Mongol superiors

category.zh: 宗教机构、赈济与公共工程
category.en: Religious institutions, relief, and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备由金银铸币、布匹、粮食、牲畜和应急商税预付款组成。由于需要应对歉收、商路中断和汗国内战，地方官员倾向维持中等规模流动储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves consisted of gold and silver coin, textiles, grain stores, livestock, and advance customs receipts. Because of the need to respond to crop failure, trade disruption, and Chagataid conflict, local rulers preferred to maintain moderate liquid reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以稳商、保路、维持驿传和灌溉为核心。在稳定时期尽量避免破坏性临时征敛，以保护税基；但遇到军事调动或宗主更替时，摊派仍可能突然上升。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on preserving commerce, securing routes, and maintaining relay and irrigation systems. In stable periods authorities tried to avoid destructive emergency exactions in order to protect the tax base, though levies could still rise abruptly during military mobilization or changes in overlordship.

description.zh: 机动核心力量，负责护送、巡逻、镇压叛乱与远程联络，战术上以骑射和快速集结见长。
description.en: The mobile core force, responsible for escort, patrol, suppression of revolt, and long-range communication, excelling in mounted archery and rapid concentration.

description.zh: 由城市驻防、城墙守军和雇佣步兵构成，承担城防、夜巡、税卡与仓库守卫。
description.en: Composed of city garrisons, wall guards, and hired infantry, tasked with urban defense, night patrols, toll stations, and warehouse security.

description.zh: 在危机时由乡村社群、行会与城坊临时征发，用于守卫运河、粮仓和城郊道路。
description.en: Raised in emergencies from village communities, guilds, and urban quarters to guard canals, granaries, and suburban roads.

name.en: Caravan guards and route security troops

description.zh: 半军事化力量，保护商旅、驿站与沙漠通道，常与地方埃米尔和商人团体合作。
description.en: A semi-military force protecting merchants, relay stations, and desert tracks, often working with local emirs and merchant associations.

commanderInChief.zh: 驻布哈拉蒙古总督兼地方军政长官
commanderInChief.en: Mongol governor at Bukhara serving as chief military-administrative commander

technology.zh: 军备以复合弓、骑枪、弯刀、锁子甲、皮甲和攻城器基础技术为主。火药兵器在本地区尚未形成常规主战力量，但能接触到中国和伊朗方向的新技术信息。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on composite bows, lances, sabres, mail, leather armor, and basic siege technologies. Gunpowder weapons had not yet become a regular field force in this region, though the area had access to technical information arriving from China and Iran.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自察合台汗国内部权力斗争、草原部族劫掠、税赋过重引发的城市不满，以及商路遭战争或疫病打断。相比大规模对外征服，军事重点更偏向守路、守城与治安。
threats.en: The chief threats came from Chagataid internal power struggles, steppe raiding, urban resentment triggered by heavy taxation, and interruptions to trade routes caused by war or disease. More than large-scale external conquest, military priorities focused on route security, city defense, and internal order.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于布哈拉、撒马尔罕及诸绿洲带，密度高度依赖灌溉网络。13世纪初蒙古征服曾造成严重人口损失，但到1280年，多数核心城市已恢复基本商业与农业机能。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in Bukhara, Samarqand, and the surrounding oasis belts, with density heavily dependent on irrigation systems. The Mongol conquest of the early thirteenth century caused severe demographic losses, but by 1280 most core cities had recovered their basic commercial and agricultural functions.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为蒙古官员、地方埃米尔、富商和宗教学者；中层为店主、行会匠人、抄写员、法官和中小地主；下层为佃农、灌溉农民、搬运工、仆役与奴隶。游牧与定居人口之间存在持续互动。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of Mongol officials, local emirs, wealthy merchants, and religious scholars; the middle strata included shopkeepers, guild artisans, scribes, judges, and middling landholders; the lower strata were tenant farmers, irrigated cultivators, porters, servants, and slaves. There was sustained interaction between nomadic and sedentary populations.

allies.zh: 正式盟友概念较弱，实际依附与合作对象是察合台体系内的蒙古权力中心，以及与大元、伊儿汗国和波斯湾商邦保持商业互通的商人网络。
allies.en: The concept of formal allies was weak; practical alignment lay with Mongol power centers in the Chagataid sphere and with merchant networks maintaining commercial links to the Great Yuan, the Ilkhanate, and Persian Gulf maritime states.

enemies.zh: 没有固定单一宿敌，但与草原掠夺集团、反税暴动者以及在察合台与钦察、伊儿汗政治冲突中冲击商路的势力关系紧张。
enemies.en: There was no single permanent enemy, but relations were tense with raiding steppe groups, anti-tax rebels, and forces disrupting trade routes during Chagataid, Kipchak, or Ilkhanid political conflicts.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以保商路、求通行与维持多方向贸易平衡为原则。地方统治者通常避免独立扩张，而是通过纳贡、接待使节、保护商队和提供中介市场来换取稳定。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed above all to preserve trade routes, secure safe passage, and maintain balanced commerce in multiple directions. Local rulers generally avoided independent expansion and instead sought stability through tribute, reception of envoys, caravan protection, and service as intermediary markets.

era.zh: 蒙古帝国时代的中世纪欧亚内陆商业技术阶段
era.en: Medieval Inner Eurasian commercial-technological stage under the Mongol Empire

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力不在单项发明，而在制度性技术：站赤驿传、跨区域商队信用、灌溉工程维护、标准化度量衡、城市作坊生产，以及吸收中国、伊朗和草原世界技术流动的中介功能。
keyInnovations.en: Its key innovations lay less in single inventions than in institutional technologies: relay-post logistics, long-distance caravan credit, irrigation maintenance, standardized weights and measures, urban workshop production, and an intermediary role in absorbing technical flows from China, Iran, and the steppe world.

strengths.zh: 优势在于地处欧亚交通中枢、城市商业传统深厚、波斯语文书与伊斯兰法契约体系成熟、手工业基础稳固，并受蒙古帝国总体交通秩序与驿传网络保护。
strengths.en: Its strengths were its position at the center of Eurasian communications, deep urban commercial traditions, mature Persianate record-keeping and Islamic contract law, solid artisanal foundations, and the protection afforded by the broader Mongol transport and relay order.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于缺乏统一强力中央政权、容易受汗国内斗和草原军事波动冲击、农业高度依赖灌溉维护，且城市繁荣对过境贸易依赖很深，一旦商路转移便迅速受损。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in the absence of a strong unified central state, vulnerability to Chagataid infighting and steppe military fluctuations, heavy dependence of agriculture on irrigation maintenance, and deep reliance of urban prosperity on transit trade that could decline quickly if routes shifted.

outlook.zh: 到1280年局势总体稳定，商业生活明显恢复。若蒙古诸汗国间保持相对和平，河中绿洲城市将继续作为东西贸易与伊斯兰学术的重要枢纽；但一旦出现长期内战、税负恶化或水利失修，繁荣基础仍十分脆弱。
outlook.en: By 1280 the situation was broadly stable and commercial life had clearly revived. If relative peace continued among the Mongol successor realms, the oasis cities of Transoxiana would remain major hubs of east-west trade and Islamic scholarship; yet their prosperity would remain fragile if prolonged civil war, worsening fiscal burdens, or neglected irrigation set in.

description.zh: 中亚绿洲城市在蒙古统治框架下维持商贸与地方自治传统。
description.en: Central Asian oasis cities preserved commercial life and local traditions within the Mongol imperial framework.

name.zh: 阿曼与霍尔木兹海贸政权

ruler.zh: 霍尔木兹与阿曼诸马立克及埃米尔
ruler.en: Rulers of Hormuz and Oman (Maliks and Emirs)

rulerTitle.zh: 马立克、埃米尔与港市领主

governmentForm.zh: 以港口君主制为核心、由商人寡头与部族盟约支撑的海上商业政体网络
governmentForm.en: A maritime commercial network of port principalities centered on monarchic rule, supported by merchant oligarchies and tribal compacts

socialStructure.zh: 社会由港口统治家族、富商、船主、伊斯兰法学者、书记官、海员、工匠、椰枣与渔业居民、阿曼内陆部族与外来商旅构成，族群与宗教多元并存。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of ruling port families, wealthy merchants, shipowners, Islamic jurists, scribes, sailors, artisans, date-growing and fishing communities, inland Omani tribes, and foreign merchants, with considerable ethnic and religious diversity.

rulingClass.zh: 港口王族、阿拉伯与波斯商人家族、海关承包人、船队主与宗教学者精英
rulingClass.en: Port dynasties, Arab and Persian merchant houses, customs-farmers, fleet owners, and religious scholarly elites

succession.zh: 以家族世袭为主，但常受商人团体、军事实力与部族支持所左右，必要时通过协商、拥立或宫廷斗争决定继承
succession.en: Succession was primarily hereditary within ruling families, but was often shaped by merchant interests, military backing, and tribal support, with accession sometimes settled by negotiation, acclamation, or palace struggle.

structure.zh: 1280年前后，波斯湾海上城邦并非统一帝国，而是以霍尔木兹、阿曼沿海港口与若干岛屿据点构成的松散政治网络。各港由马立克或埃米尔统治，依靠宫廷书记、税吏、港务官、海关监督与海军首领维持行政；重要政策需兼顾商人利益、宗教法与海上安全。
structure.en: Around 1280, the Persian Gulf maritime states were not a unified empire but a loose political network composed of Hormuz, Omani coastal ports, and several island strongholds. Each port was ruled by a malik or emir, relying on court secretaries, tax officials, harbor masters, customs supervisors, and naval captains; major policy had to balance mercantile interests, Islamic law, and maritime security.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保存契约、处理外交书信、任命地方官员并协调诸港统治者与商人代表
function.en: Drafted decrees, preserved contracts, handled diplomatic correspondence, appointed local officials, and coordinated between port rulers and merchant representatives

function.zh: 征收进出口关税、船舶停泊费、转口税与市场税，是政府财政的核心机构
function.en: Collected import and export duties, anchorage fees, transit taxes, and market dues, forming the fiscal core of government

function.zh: 管理码头、仓储、灯火引航、船舶登记、修船设施与季风航线调度
function.en: Managed quays, warehouses, navigational lights, ship registration, repair facilities, and monsoon shipping schedules

function.zh: 由卡迪、法学者与书记处理商事纠纷、婚姻继承、债务与民刑案件，并确保统治合法性
function.en: Qadis, jurists, and scribes handled commercial disputes, marriage and inheritance, debt, and civil-criminal cases, while upholding political legitimacy

function.zh: 组织巡逻舰船、护航商队、镇压海盗并守卫岛屿、海峡与沿岸据点
function.en: Organized patrol vessels, convoy escorts, anti-piracy operations, and defense of islands, straits, and coastal posts

name.zh: 内陆贡赋与部族事务署
name.en: Interior Tribute and Tribal Affairs Office

function.zh: 协调阿曼内陆部族、绿洲与沿海道路安全，征收贡赋并处理通商保护协议
function.en: Coordinated with inland Omani tribes, oases, and coastal road security, collected tribute, and managed trade-protection agreements

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以港口城市为中心，港长、税官、卡迪与市场监督官共同治理。较小岛屿与沿岸聚落通常由受任命的代理人、家族附庸或部族首领管理，并向主港缴纳税赋或服务义务。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was centered on port cities, governed jointly by harbor officials, tax officers, qadis, and market inspectors. Smaller islands and coastal settlements were usually managed by appointed agents, familial dependents, or tribal chiefs, who rendered taxes or service obligations to the leading ports.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以逊尼派伊斯兰教法为主，结合港口习惯法、商人契约实践与统治者敕令。海运、合伙投资、货损、债务清偿与跨宗教交易常依书面契约与见证制度裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based primarily on Sunni Islamic law, combined with port custom, mercantile contract practice, and ruler's decrees. Maritime transport, partnerships, cargo loss, debt settlement, and interfaith transactions were often adjudicated through written contracts and witness procedures.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以海关关税、港口停泊费、市场税、转口税、船舶费和附属地区贡赋为主，内陆农业税占比较小。由于财政高度依赖贸易，税率通常保持在能吸引外国商人的水平。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied chiefly on customs duties, anchorage charges, market tolls, transit levies, shipping fees, and tribute from dependent districts, while inland agricultural taxes played a smaller role. Because finances depended heavily on commerce, rates were generally kept at levels intended to attract foreign merchants.

religion.zh: 主流宗教为伊斯兰教，阿曼地区伊巴德派传统重要，霍尔木兹及海港也有逊尼派影响，并容纳波斯人、印度商人与少量基督徒、犹太人等侨民社群。
religion.en: Islam was the dominant religion, with Ibadi traditions particularly important in Oman, while Sunni influence was also strong in Hormuz and the ports; Persian, Indian, and smaller Christian and Jewish expatriate communities were also present.

philosophy.zh: 思想文化以伊斯兰法学、商人实用伦理、阿拉伯—波斯行政书写传统和航海知识为核心，重视契约、信用、敬虔与跨海贸易中的互惠关系。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Islamic jurisprudence, mercantile practical ethics, Arab-Persian bureaucratic literary traditions, and navigational knowledge, emphasizing contract, credit, piety, and reciprocity in overseas commerce.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉伯字母书写阿拉伯语与波斯语；商业往来中亦可能接触古吉拉特、泰米尔和其他印度洋贸易文字传统。
writingSystem.en: The Arabic script was primarily used to write Arabic and Persian; commercial exchange also brought contact with Gujarati, Tamil, and other Indian Ocean writing traditions.

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要文化成就在于成熟的海上商业文化、契约文书、港口多语交流、造船与航海经验的积累，以及将阿拉伯半岛、伊朗南岸、印度西岸和东非海岸连结起来的商路网络。
culturalAchievements.en: Its chief cultural achievements lay in a mature maritime commercial culture, sophisticated contract documentation, multilingual port exchange, accumulated shipbuilding and navigational experience, and trade networks linking Arabia, the southern Iranian coast, western India, and East Africa.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属闪米特语系的阿拉伯语与印欧语系伊朗支的波斯语，港口并通行多种印度洋贸易语言
languageFamily.en: The principal languages were Arabic of the Semitic family and Persian of the Iranian branch of Indo-European, alongside multiple Indian Ocean trade languages in the ports

gdpDescription.zh: 经济高度繁荣并显著外向，以转口贸易、航运、仓储、关税收益、造船、珍珠采集、渔业、椰枣与区域手工业为支柱。总量相较农耕帝国有限，但单位人口商业化程度与货币流通水平很高。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was highly prosperous and outward-oriented, supported by entrepôt trade, shipping, warehousing, customs income, shipbuilding, pearl fishing, fisheries, date production, and regional crafts. Its total size was modest compared with agrarian empires, but commercial intensity and monetization per capita were high.

mainIndustries.zh: 转口贸易、航运服务、造船、珍珠业、渔业、椰枣种植、铜与马匹转运、香料与纺织品集散、金融借贷与仓储
mainIndustries.en: Entrepôt trade, shipping services, shipbuilding, pearling, fishing, date cultivation, copper and horse transit trade, spice and textile redistribution, finance, and warehousing

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、珍珠、椰枣、干鱼、盐、铜、硫磺、波斯陶器、印度棉布、靛蓝、香料、糖、药材、沉香、象牙、东非金与奴隶、伊朗与伊拉克市场所需奢侈品及日用品
tradeGoods.en: Horses, pearls, dates, dried fish, salt, copper, sulfur, Persian ceramics, Indian cotton textiles, indigo, spices, sugar, medicinal substances, aloeswood, ivory, East African gold and slaves, and luxury and staple goods for Iranian and Iraqi markets

name.en: Dinar and dirham in concurrent use

householdWealth.zh: 港口富商家庭拥有船只、仓库、现金、金银器与跨海债权，财富显著高于普通渔民与工匠。一般家庭财富以住房、渔具、家畜、椰枣园份额和小额银币储蓄为主。
householdWealth.en: Wealthy port households possessed ships, warehouses, cash, precious metal objects, and overseas credit claims, far surpassing the means of ordinary fishermen and artisans. Typical households held wealth mainly in housing, fishing gear, livestock, shares in date groves, and small silver savings.

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换、海关制度、商人合伙、信用借贷与统治者保护贸易为核心的商业经济；国家直接生产有限，但通过税收、港务管理与治安保障深度嵌入市场。
economicSystem.en: A commercial economy centered on market exchange, customs institutions, merchant partnerships, credit, and ruler-backed trade protection; the state engaged little in direct production but was deeply embedded in markets through taxation, harbor management, and security.

source.zh: 附属地区贡赋与内陆征收
source.en: Tribute from dependent districts and inland collections

source.zh: 王室产业、渔珠与特许收入
source.en: Royal estates, pearling, and concession income

category.en: Harbor works, warehousing, and ship repair

category.zh: 部族补贴、外交赠礼与通商保障
category.en: Tribal subsidies, diplomatic gifts, and trade protection payments

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以金银币、货物库存、珠宝、应收税款与王室储备构成，流动性较强，但受海运风险、政局变动与季风周期影响明显。富裕时期可迅速筹资，危机时亦可能因贸易中断而骤减。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of gold and silver coin, commodity reserves, jewels, receivable taxes, and royal stores, giving it relatively high liquidity, though it was strongly affected by maritime risk, political shifts, and monsoon cycles. In prosperous times funds could be raised quickly, but trade disruption could sharply reduce reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏务实和亲商，倾向通过低至中等关税扩大贸易总量，而非对农业或民众进行高压榨取；在安全受威胁时则增加海防与护航支出。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was pragmatic and pro-merchant, preferring low-to-moderate customs rates to expand total trade rather than heavy extraction from agriculture or commoners; when security was threatened, spending on naval defense and convoy protection increased.

description.zh: 由轻型武装舟、巡逻船与征调商船组成，负责护航、巡逻海峡、打击海盗和封锁敌对港口。
description.en: Composed of light armed craft, patrol vessels, and requisitioned merchant ships, responsible for convoy escort, strait patrols, anti-piracy actions, and blockade of hostile ports.

description.zh: 驻守霍尔木兹、阿曼沿海城镇与岛屿据点，包括步兵、弓箭手和少量骑兵，用于城防与维持秩序。
description.en: Stationed in Hormuz, Omani coastal towns, and island strongholds, including infantry, archers, and small cavalry detachments for urban defense and order.

description.zh: 由阿曼内陆与沿海部族在战时提供，适合山地、沙漠道路与海岸线防御，但纪律和持续动员能力有限。
description.en: Raised in wartime from inland and coastal Omani tribes, suitable for mountain, desert-route, and shoreline defense, though limited in discipline and long-term mobilization.

commanderInChief.zh: 霍尔木兹马立克及其海军长官
commanderInChief.en: The Malik of Hormuz and his naval commanders

technology.zh: 军事技术以印度洋和阿拉伯海航海传统为基础，擅长季风航行、天文导航、港口工事和轻型海战。陆战装备以锁子甲、长矛、弓箭、刀剑和少量攻城器械为主，尚未形成东亚式火药军制。
technology.en: Military technology rested on Indian Ocean and Arabian Sea nautical traditions, with strengths in monsoon sailing, celestial navigation, port fortification, and light naval warfare. Land forces used mail, spears, bows, swords, and limited siege equipment, without a developed East Asian-style gunpowder military system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括海盗、敌对港口竞争、阿曼内陆部族冲突、来自更大帝国对海关收益的干预，以及红海与波斯湾航线竞争所引发的贸易转移。
threats.en: Main threats included piracy, rivalry from hostile ports, conflicts with inland Omani tribes, interference by larger empires seeking customs revenues, and diversion of trade caused by competition between Red Sea and Gulf routes.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约60万，分布极不均衡。多数人口集中于沿海港口、绿洲与阿曼北部聚居区，许多岛屿与海岸据点人口规模不大但商业活动高度活跃。人口构成兼具阿拉伯、波斯、非洲与南亚成分。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 600,000 and very unevenly distributed. Most people were concentrated in coastal ports, oases, and northern Omani settlements, while many islands and shore stations were small in population but highly active commercially. The population included Arab, Persian, African, and South Asian elements.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为统治家族、富商、船东与宗教学者；中层为小商人、工匠、书记、店主与船员；下层为渔民、码头劳工、雇工、奴隶与贫困部族成员。外来商人与侨民社群在经济上可能地位很高，但政治权利受限。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata comprised ruling families, wealthy merchants, shipowners, and religious scholars; the middle strata included petty traders, artisans, scribes, shopkeepers, and mariners; the lower strata consisted of fishermen, dock laborers, hired workers, slaves, and poorer tribal people. Foreign merchants and diaspora communities could be economically influential while politically restricted.

allies.zh: 与伊儿汗国治下的波斯南岸城市、阿曼部分部族首领、印度西岸商人集团以及东非斯瓦希里海岸港口保持务实合作关系；同若干穆斯林商港存在基于贸易利益的松散友好联系。
allies.en: They maintained pragmatic cooperation with cities of the southern Persian coast under the Ilkhanate, certain Omani tribal leaders, merchant groups on the western coast of India, and ports of the Swahili coast; several Muslim trading ports were linked by loose friendship based on commerce.

enemies.zh: 没有固定的单一死敌，但常与海盗集团、竞争性海港、敌对部族和试图强征贡税或控制海关的大国官员发生冲突。
enemies.en: There was no single permanent mortal enemy, but frequent conflict arose with pirate groups, rival ports, hostile tribes, and officials of larger powers seeking tribute or direct control of customs.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以保障航路、维持通商自由、在强邻之间保持弹性平衡并最大化转口收益为核心。相较领土扩张，更偏好通过婚姻、馈赠、互市优惠与保护协定建立影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on safeguarding sea routes, preserving commercial freedom, maintaining flexible balance among powerful neighbors, and maximizing entrepôt profits. Rather than territorial expansion, it preferred marriage ties, gifts, favorable trading terms, and protection agreements.

era.zh: 中世纪后期印度洋海上商业时代，处于蒙古帝国整合欧亚贸易网络的背景之下
era.en: Late medieval Indian Ocean commercial age, under the wider context of Mongol-era integration of Eurasian trade networks

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与知识包括缝合板与钉接混合造船传统、季风航海经验、星象导航、精细港口仓储管理、长距离商人信用网络，以及适应多语多法环境的契约与账簿制度。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and knowledge included mixed sewn-and-nailed shipbuilding traditions, monsoon navigation, stellar wayfinding, sophisticated harbor warehousing management, long-distance merchant credit networks, and contract and bookkeeping systems adapted to multilingual and multi-legal environments.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于地理位置优越、贸易中介能力强、港口社会灵活多元、财政现金化程度高，并能在波斯、阿拉伯、印度和东非之间迅速调度商品与信息。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were strategic location, strong intermediary trade capacity, flexible and diverse port society, high fiscal monetization, and the ability to move goods and information rapidly between Persia, Arabia, India, and East Africa.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于腹地狭小、粮食与淡水资源有限、政治上分散、对海上安全和季风周期高度依赖，且易受更大帝国、海盗或贸易路线变化冲击。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included a narrow hinterland, limited food and fresh-water resources, political fragmentation, heavy dependence on maritime security and monsoon cycles, and vulnerability to larger empires, piracy, or shifts in trade routes.

outlook.zh: 1280年前后前景总体乐观。蒙古时代扩大了欧亚商品流通，对波斯湾转口贸易有利；若霍尔木兹和阿曼诸港能维持海上安全、控制关税竞争并处理内陆部族关系，其繁荣可望持续，但长期仍面临红海路线竞争与地区权力重组风险。
outlook.en: Around 1280 the outlook was broadly favorable. The Mongol age expanded Eurasian circulation of goods, benefiting Gulf entrepôt commerce; if Hormuz and the Omani ports could preserve maritime security, manage customs competition, and handle inland tribal relations, their prosperity was likely to continue, though long-term risks remained from Red Sea competition and regional political realignment.

description.zh: 波斯湾与阿拉伯海的海上商贸节点，连接印度洋与伊朗、伊拉克市场。
description.en: Maritime commercial nodes of the Gulf and Arabian Sea linking the Indian Ocean to Iranian and Iraqi markets.

name.zh: 中墨西哥纳瓦城邦诸国

ruler.zh: 诸位特拉托阿尼统治者

governmentForm.zh: 分散的纳瓦语城邦联盟与竞争体系，以世袭王权、贵族议政和地方社群组织并存
governmentForm.en: A decentralized system of competing Nahua-speaking city-states combining hereditary kingship, noble councils, and local community organization

socialStructure.zh: 社会由统治贵族、祭司、武士、平民农户与工匠、附庸居民及奴隶构成，血缘与地方社群身份十分重要
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around ruling nobles, priests, warriors, commoner farmers and artisans, dependent populations, and slaves, with kinship and local community identity playing central roles

rulingClass.zh: 皮皮尔廷贵族、王族世系、祭司集团与资深武士首领
rulingClass.en: The pipiltin nobility, royal lineages, priestly establishments, and senior warrior leaders

succession.zh: 多为王族内部选择与世袭相结合，由贵族与重要家族认可新任特拉托阿尼
succession.en: Succession usually combined dynastic heredity with selection within the royal lineage, requiring acceptance by nobles and leading families

structure.zh: 1280年的中墨西哥并非统一帝国，而是由多个阿尔特佩特尔（城邦）组成；各城邦由特拉托阿尼、王族贵族议事团、祭司机构和地方卡尔普利社群共同运作。查普尔特佩克一带是墨西加等迁徙群体的重要据点之一，但尚未形成后世特诺奇蒂特兰式的霸权国家。
structure.en: In 1280 Central Mexico was not a unified empire but a mosaic of altepetl city-states. Each polity was governed by a tlatoani, a council of royal and noble elites, priestly institutions, and local calpolli communities. The Chapultepec area was an important seat for migrant groups such as the Mexica, but no hegemonic state like later Tenochtitlan had yet emerged.

function.zh: 辅佐特拉托阿尼处理继承、外交、战争决策、司法仲裁与贵族封地事务
function.en: Assisted the tlatoani in succession matters, diplomacy, war decisions, judicial arbitration, and noble estate affairs

function.zh: 主持祭祀、占卜、历法、教育与圣地维护，规范节庆与国家仪式
function.en: Managed ritual, divination, calendrics, education, and shrine maintenance, and regulated festivals and state ceremonies

function.zh: 征收粮食、棉布、劳役与手工业品，管理公共储备与战争供给
function.en: Collected foodstuffs, cotton cloth, labor dues, and craft goods, and managed public reserves and war supplies

function.zh: 监督集市秩序、度量交换、道路安全及跨城邦贸易联系
function.en: Oversaw market order, exchange standards, route security, and inter-polity trade connections

function.zh: 协调卡尔普利土地分配、灌溉与劳役组织，记录各社群义务
function.en: Coordinated calpolli land allocation, irrigation and labor organization, and recorded community obligations

function.zh: 组织战士召集、武器储备、边境警戒与战俘处置
function.en: Organized warrior levies, weapon reserves, frontier vigilance, and the handling of captives

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以阿尔特佩特尔为核心，下辖若干卡尔普利或类似社群单位。各社群负责耕地、税役、治水和军事征发，地方长老与贵族代理人共同执行政令。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on the altepetl, which contained several calpolli or comparable community units. These communities managed farmland, labor and tribute duties, waterworks, and military levies, with elders and noble deputies jointly implementing policy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王廷裁断和宗教规范为基础。婚姻、土地、债务、贡赋和刑罚多由地方首领与贵族审理，严重罪行由统治者或其法官裁决；惩罚可包括赔偿、劳役、流放、奴役与死刑。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary practice, royal judgment, and religious norms. Marriage, land, debt, tribute, and penal disputes were usually heard by local leaders and nobles, while serious crimes were judged by rulers or their appointed judges; punishments could include compensation, labor service, enslavement, exile, or death.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠贡赋和劳役而非统一货币税。常见项目包括玉米、豆类、辣椒、龙舌兰制品、棉布、羽毛、陶器、黑曜石器、建造劳役和军事服务；附属村社与战败群体负担更重。
taxationSystem.en: Public finance relied chiefly on tribute and labor service rather than a uniform monetary tax. Common obligations included maize, beans, chili, maguey products, cotton cloth, feathers, pottery, obsidian tools, construction labor, and military service; subordinate villages and defeated groups bore heavier burdens.

religion.zh: 宗教为中部墨西哥多神崇拜体系，敬奉雨神特拉洛克、火神修特库特利、羽蛇神魁札尔科亚特尔、太阳与战争相关神祇等。祭祀、历法和献祭是政治合法性的核心组成部分。
religion.en: Religion consisted of the polytheistic cults of Central Mexico, honoring deities such as Tlaloc, Xiuhtecuhtli, Quetzalcoatl, and various sun and war gods. Ritual observance, calendrics, and sacrifice were central to political legitimacy.

philosophy.zh: 思想主要体现于祭司传统、祖先记忆、历法观念与对命运、战争、农业循环和统治责任的理解，尚未形成后世文献中更系统化的纳瓦哲思表达。
philosophy.en: Thought was expressed mainly through priestly tradition, ancestral memory, calendrical concepts, and understandings of fate, warfare, agricultural cycles, and rulership, rather than the more systematically recorded Nahua philosophical expression of later centuries.

writingSystem.zh: 使用中墨西哥图画文字与记事传统，以象形、地名符号、历法符号和人物标记记录谱系、迁徙、贡赋和祭仪；口传仍是最重要的信息保存方式。
writingSystem.en: They used the pictorial writing and record-keeping traditions of Central Mexico, employing glyphic place signs, calendrical notation, and personal markers to record lineages, migrations, tribute, and ritual; oral transmission remained the primary medium of preservation.

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括神庙与祭坛建筑、土台和堤道营造、黑曜石工艺、陶器制造、羽饰艺术、历法知识、地方谱系传统以及围绕市场和节庆形成的区域文化网络。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included temple and altar construction, earthen platforms and causeways, obsidian craftsmanship, pottery production, featherwork, calendrical knowledge, local genealogical traditions, and a regional cultural network shaped by markets and festivals.

languageFamily.zh: 乌托-阿兹特克语系中的纳瓦特尔语支
languageFamily.en: The Nahuan branch of the Uto-Aztecan language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以玉米农业为基础，辅以豆类、南瓜、辣椒、龙舌兰、渔猎、黑曜石开采和区域贸易。市场交换活跃，但整体仍以家庭生产、社群劳役与贡赋再分配为主，尚未形成后期三国同盟时代那种高度整合的帝国性财政经济。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on maize agriculture, supplemented by beans, squash, chili, maguey, fishing, hunting, obsidian extraction, and regional trade. Market exchange was active, but the overall system remained dominated by household production, community labor, and tribute redistribution rather than the highly integrated imperial fiscal economy of the later Triple Alliance.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米与杂粮农业、龙舌兰制品、黑曜石采制、陶器、棉布与纤维织物、建筑营造、羽毛工艺、地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Maize and mixed farming, maguey products, obsidian quarrying and toolmaking, pottery, cotton and fiber textiles, construction, feather craft, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石刀片、玉米、豆类、辣椒、盐、棉布、龙舌兰纤维、陶器、羽毛、石器、可可少量转运品、绿石与装饰品
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian blades, maize, beans, chili, salt, cotton cloth, maguey fiber, pottery, feathers, stone tools, limited transit cacao, greenstone, and ornaments

name.zh: 以物易物与贡赋交换体系

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在住房、农具、储粮、衣物、家庭劳力和社群土地使用权；贵族则拥有更大宅院、附庸劳役、礼器、棉布与奢侈装饰。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was expressed mainly in dwellings, tools, grain stores, clothing, family labor, and access rights to community land; nobles possessed larger residences, dependent labor, ritual goods, cotton cloth, and luxury ornaments.

economicSystem.zh: 属农业—贡赋—市场混合经济，基层由家庭与卡尔普利社群生产，上层通过贡赋、祭祀中心和战争再分配资源；长途贸易存在，但远不如后世波奇特卡体系成熟。
economicSystem.en: This was a mixed agrarian-tributary-market economy in which households and calpolli communities produced at the base, while upper elites redistributed resources through tribute, ritual centers, and warfare; long-distance trade existed, but it was less developed than the later pochteca system.

source.en: Tribute from dependents and defeated groups

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备主要不是铸币，而是仓储中的粮食、布匹、武器、祭祀用品与可再分配奢侈品。财富储藏具有实物性和礼仪性，便于供养王廷、军队和节庆。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were held primarily not as coined money but as grain, cloth, weapons, ritual supplies, and redistributable luxury goods in storehouses. Wealth was stored in material and ceremonial forms suited to supporting the court, military, and festivals.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持神庙祭祀、贵族权威、治水工程和战争能力，平时积累实物储备，歉收时则通过社群和仓储缓冲危机。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining temple ritual, noble authority, waterworks, and military capability, accumulating material reserves in normal years and using community networks and storehouses to cushion poor harvests.

description.zh: 由贵族、王族随从和经验丰富战士组成，是各城邦的核心作战力量与统治象征
description.en: Composed of nobles, royal retainers, and experienced fighters, forming the core combat force and a symbol of rulership in each city-state

description.zh: 由平民社群按需征召，装备以棉甲、盾牌、投矛器、长矛、棍棒剑和石制武器为主
description.en: Raised from commoner communities as needed, equipped chiefly with cotton armor, shields, atlatls, spears, club-swords, and stone weapons

description.zh: 负责警戒道路、护卫粮运、支援围攻和执行地方防务，也吸收附庸与盟友兵力
description.en: Handled road watch, grain convoy protection, siege support, and local defense, and also included troops from dependents and allies

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦特拉托阿尼及其武士首领
commanderInChief.en: Each city-state's tlatoani and his senior war captains

technology.zh: 军事技术以黑曜石刃木兵器、投矛器、长矛、弓箭、棉甲、木盾和土石防御工事为主；作战强调俘虏、威望与对周边村社的控制，尚未出现火器、骑兵和金属兵器主导的战争方式。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wooden weapons edged with obsidian, atlatls, spears, bows, padded cotton armor, shields, and earth-and-stone fortifications. Warfare emphasized capture, prestige, and control of surrounding communities, with no firearms, cavalry, or metal-weapon dominance.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近纳瓦城邦间的竞争、与丘鲁特克—托尔特克遗绪相关势力的冲突、与米却肯和瓦斯特卡方向的边缘交战，以及资源、贡赋和迁徙地盘引发的局部战争。
threats.en: The main threats came from rivalry among neighboring Nahua city-states, conflicts with powers tied to the Chichimec-Toltec political legacy, frontier fighting toward Michoacán and other peripheral regions, and local wars driven by resources, tribute, and claims over migration territory.

populationDescription.zh: 人口密集分布于墨西哥谷及周边高地、湖区和农业盆地，由多个纳瓦语城邦、附属村社和迁徙群体构成。查普尔特佩克并非压倒性大都会，而是区域政治与居住据点之一。
populationDescription.en: The population was densely distributed across the Valley of Mexico and nearby highlands, lake zones, and agricultural basins, consisting of multiple Nahua-speaking city-states, dependent villages, and migrant groups. Chapultepec was not an overwhelming metropolis but one important regional political and residential center.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王族与皮皮尔廷贵族、祭司、职业武士、商贩与工匠、普通马赛瓦尔平民农户、附属社群和奴隶。阶层间有明显差异，但战功、婚姻和王廷服务可在一定程度上改变地位。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included royal families and pipiltin nobles, priests, professional warriors, merchants and artisans, commoner macehualtin farmers, dependent communities, and slaves. Distinctions were marked, though military success, marriage, and court service could alter status to a degree.

allies.zh: 无固定统一盟友；常见的是城邦间短期联姻、共同作战与互保协议，可能与部分纳瓦、托尔特克遗绪和湖区城邦形成阶段性联盟
allies.en: There were no permanent unified allies; rather, city-states formed short-term marriage alliances, military coalitions, and mutual defense arrangements, sometimes aligning with neighboring Nahua, Toltec-legacy, and lake-basin polities

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者是竞争性的邻近城邦、争夺贡赋和土地的地方首领，以及与中墨西哥势力对抗的周边民族政体，如部分米却肯方向诸势力
enemies.en: Principal enemies were rival neighboring city-states, local lords contesting tribute and land, and surrounding ethnic polities resisting Central Mexican expansion, including some forces toward Michoacán

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以婚姻联结、贡赋支配、季节性战争、俘虏交换和市场通道控制为主，目标不是形成广域世界外交，而是在谷地和周边高地维持势力平衡与资源获取。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy relied on marriage ties, tribute dominance, seasonal warfare, captive exchange, and control of market routes. The aim was not broad world diplomacy but maintaining balance and securing resources within the valley and surrounding highlands.

era.zh: 中部墨西哥后古典时期
era.en: Postclassic Central Mexican era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与知识包括黑曜石精制刀片、湖区农业与水利管理、土台与神庙建筑、棉甲、防御工事、图画记事、复杂历法体系和区域市场网络；虽然没有铁器、车轮运输和畜力农业，但在环境适应与工艺组织上高度成熟。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and skills included finely worked obsidian blades, lake-zone agriculture and water management, platform and temple construction, cotton armor, fortifications, pictorial record keeping, complex calendrical systems, and regional market networks. Although lacking iron tools, wheeled transport, and draft-animal agriculture, they were highly developed in environmental adaptation and craft organization.

strengths.zh: 优势在于人口稠密、农业基础稳固、黑曜石资源丰富、市场网络活跃、宗教与王权结合紧密，以及城邦体系带来的政治弹性和持续动员能力。
strengths.en: Strengths included dense population, a solid agricultural base, abundant obsidian resources, active market networks, tight integration of religion and rulership, and the political flexibility and mobilization capacity of the city-state system.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是政治分裂严重、统一协调能力有限、战争频繁消耗人口与物资、对气候波动和局部歉收较敏感，且尚未形成稳定的跨区域帝国机构。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included sharp political fragmentation, limited unified coordination, frequent warfare that consumed manpower and resources, sensitivity to climatic fluctuation and local crop failure, and the absence of durable supra-regional imperial institutions.

outlook.zh: 到1280年，纳瓦世界总体稳定但竞争激烈。未来最可能的发展方向是某一湖区强权通过战争、联姻和贡赋整合建立更大的霸权体系，为14至15世纪中部墨西哥帝国化进程奠定基础。
outlook.en: In 1280 the Nahua world was broadly stable but intensely competitive. The most likely future trajectory was the rise of a dominant lake-basin power able to combine warfare, marriage politics, and tribute integration into a larger hegemonic system, laying foundations for the imperial developments of 14th- and 15th-century Central Mexico.

description.zh: 阿兹特克帝国形成前的中墨西哥纳瓦世界，由众多竞争城邦组成。
description.en: The Nahua world of Central Mexico before the Aztec Empire, composed of many competing city-states.

governmentForm.zh: 由世袭贵族统治的米斯特克城邦联盟，政治上分散，但通过婚姻、宗谱与朝贡关系相互联结。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary noble city-state network of Mixtec polities, politically fragmented yet interconnected through marriage alliances, genealogical legitimacy, and tribute ties.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以贵族宗族为核心，分为统治贵族、祭司与书记、武士、工匠、普通农民及附属劳役群体。身份与土地、婚姻和祖先谱系紧密相关。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around noble lineages and divided into ruling nobles, priests and scribes, warriors, artisans, commoner farmers, and dependent laboring groups. Status was closely tied to land, marriage, and ancestral genealogy.

rulingClass.zh: 世袭贵族家族与其附属祭司—书记阶层
rulingClass.en: Hereditary noble houses together with their attached priest-scribal elite

succession.zh: 以贵族血统继承为原则，常结合跨城邦婚姻、宗谱主张与武力争夺；男性优先但并非绝对，贵族女性可通过婚姻与血统传递合法性。
succession.en: Succession followed hereditary noble descent, often shaped by inter-city marriage alliances, genealogical claims, and military contestation; male preference was common but not absolute, and noble women could transmit legitimacy through marriage and lineage.

structure.zh: 1280年的米斯特克地区并非统一帝国，而是以图图特佩克等强邦为核心的多城邦格局。每个城邦由领主、王族亲属、祭司和书记共同运作，依靠宗谱文书、贡赋网络和地方首领治理辖区村社。
structure.en: In 1280 the Mixtec world was not a unified empire but a constellation of city-states, with powerful centers such as Tututepec. Each polity was governed by a lord, royal kin, priests, and scribes, relying on genealogical records, tribute networks, and subordinate local chiefs to administer surrounding communities.

function.zh: 管理王室家族、婚姻联盟、祖先谱系与合法性文书，保存彩绘抄本和贵族契约。
function.en: Managed the ruling household, marriage alliances, ancestral genealogies, and documents of legitimacy, preserving painted codices and noble agreements.

function.zh: 征收玉米、棉布、可可、羽毛、盐和工艺品等贡物，并负责储藏与再分配。
function.en: Collected tribute in maize, cotton cloth, cacao, feathers, salt, and craft goods, and supervised storage and redistribution.

function.zh: 主持宗教祭礼、占卜、历法安排与神庙财产管理。
function.en: Oversaw religious ceremonies, divination, calendrical scheduling, and temple property.

function.zh: 组织贵族武士、征调村社兵力、防御边界并监督征服地区的忠诚。
function.en: Organized noble warriors, levied community troops, defended frontiers, and supervised the loyalty of conquered districts.

function.zh: 监管金工、绿松石镶嵌、陶器、棉织与远程交换，协调市场与商旅安全。
function.en: Supervised goldworking, turquoise mosaics, ceramics, cotton textiles, and long-distance exchange, coordinating markets and merchant safety.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖隶属村社首领与贵族支系。中心城邦向附属聚落派遣代理人，负责贡赋、劳役和祭祀义务；许多山区社区保有较强自治，但需承认上级领主权威。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on subordinate village chiefs and cadet noble branches. Central city-states dispatched agents to dependent settlements to manage tribute, labor obligations, and ritual duties; many upland communities retained significant autonomy but had to acknowledge the authority of superior lords.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯、贵族裁决、宗教禁忌和赔偿原则为基础。土地继承、婚姻、朝贡违约和贵族间争端多由宫廷与祭司共同审理，严重政治叛乱则可能以战争或处决解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, noble adjudication, ritual taboos, and compensation principles. Land inheritance, marriage, tribute default, and disputes among nobles were commonly judged by the court in consultation with priests, while serious political rebellion could be resolved through warfare or execution.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自贡赋而非统一货币税制。附属村社定期上缴玉米、豆类、棉布、手工制品、盐、蜂蜜、劳役和军事服役，强邦还从贸易通道和被征服地区获取额外贡物。
taxationSystem.en: Public finance rested mainly on tribute rather than a uniform monetary tax system. Dependent communities regularly delivered maize, beans, cotton cloth, craft goods, salt, honey, labor service, and military obligations, while stronger polities also extracted additional tribute from trade routes and conquered districts.

religion.zh: 米斯特克宗教为多神信仰，崇拜雨神、火神、祖先与地方山川神灵，祭祀、占卜、历法和祖先崇拜构成政治合法性的核心。
religion.en: Mixtec religion was polytheistic, venerating rain deities, fire gods, ancestors, and local mountain and landscape spirits; ritual, divination, calendrics, and ancestor worship were central to political legitimacy.

philosophy.zh: 思想传统强调祖先血统、天命征兆、神圣时间循环与城邦间关系的仪式秩序。历史被视为由神意、婚姻联盟与英雄祖先行为塑造的连续谱系。
philosophy.en: The intellectual tradition emphasized ancestral descent, omens, sacred cycles of time, and the ritual ordering of relations among polities. History was understood as a continuing genealogy shaped by divine will, marriage alliances, and the deeds of heroic ancestors.

writingSystem.zh: 使用象形—表意兼具的图像书写传统，以彩绘折页抄本记录王族谱系、战争、婚姻、地名与礼仪日期。
writingSystem.en: They used a pictorial writing tradition combining glyphic and ideographic elements, with painted screenfold codices recording royal genealogies, wars, marriages, place names, and ritual dates.

culturalAchievements.zh: 米斯特克人以精美金工、失蜡铸造、绿松石与贝壳镶嵌、彩陶、细致织物以及复杂的宗谱抄本著称，是中部美洲最杰出的工艺传统之一。
culturalAchievements.en: The Mixtecs were renowned for fine goldwork, lost-wax casting, turquoise and shell mosaics, painted ceramics, refined textiles, and sophisticated genealogical codices, making them one of the foremost craft traditions in Mesoamerica.

languageFamily.zh: 奥托-曼格语系米斯特克语支
languageFamily.en: Mixtecan branch of the Oto-Manguean language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业和贡赋再分配为基础，辅以高价值工艺生产和区域贸易。按全球1280年的标准，米斯特克经济在美洲属于较复杂的中等发展水平，但远非货币化商业经济。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture and tribute redistribution, supplemented by high-value craft production and regional trade. By global standards in 1280, the Mixtec economy was a relatively complex middle-level economy within the Americas, but far from a fully monetized commercial system.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米、豆类、南瓜与龙舌兰农业；棉织；陶器；金工；石器与贝壳加工；盐产；羽毛与奢侈工艺
mainIndustries.en: Maize, bean, squash, and maguey agriculture; cotton weaving; ceramics; goldworking; stone and shell working; salt production; featherwork and luxury crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、可可、金饰、绿松石镶嵌物、贝壳、黑曜石、盐、羽毛、陶器、蜂蜜和染料
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, cacao, gold ornaments, turquoise mosaics, shell goods, obsidian, salt, feathers, ceramics, honey, and dyes

name.zh: 实物贡赋与可可交换体系
name.en: Commodity tribute and cacao exchange system

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、玉米储备、家用陶器、织物和亲族网络；贵族则拥有土地、贡赋权、奢侈品和工匠产出控制权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth for commoners was mainly held in access to farmland, maize stores, domestic pottery, textiles, and kin networks; nobles held wealth through land rights, tribute claims, luxury goods, and control over artisan output.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系属于农业—贡赋—手工业混合体，市场交换存在但不支配全局。政治权力通过控制土地、劳役、工匠和长距离交换品来实现财富集中。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a mixed agrarian-tributary-artisanal one, with market exchange present but not dominant. Political power concentrated wealth through control of land, labor, artisans, and long-distance exchange goods.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非集中于单一金属储藏，而是由宫廷仓储中的粮食、布匹、奢侈工艺品、可可、祭器和少量贵金属共同构成，足以支撑短期战争与礼仪支出。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not concentrated in a single metallic reserve but consisted of palace stores of grain, cloth, luxury crafts, cacao, ritual objects, and limited precious metals, sufficient to support short campaigns and ceremonial expenditure.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健保守，重点在维持贡赋流动、保障神庙与贵族网络、并通过再分配巩固附属社区忠诚。丰年积储，歉年则有限赈济，以防地方离叛。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, focused on maintaining tribute flows, sustaining temples and noble networks, and reinforcing subordinate communities through redistribution. Surpluses were stored in good years, while limited relief in poor years helped prevent local defection.

description.zh: 由贵族及其侍从组成，是城邦军队的指挥与冲击力量，重视俘虏、荣誉和家族声望。
description.en: Composed of nobles and their retainers, this was the command and shock element of city-state forces, emphasizing captives, honor, and family prestige.

description.zh: 由村社征发的主力兵员，装备矛、投枪、盾牌、石刀木剑与弓箭，用于数量支撑和地方防御。
description.en: The main body levied from village communities, equipped with spears, darts, shields, edged wooden weapons, and bows, providing mass and local defense.

description.zh: 驻守险要通道与山地边界，熟悉地形，负责预警、巡逻和拦截敌对袭扰。
description.en: Stationed on strategic routes and upland frontiers, these forces knew the terrain well and handled warning, patrol, and interception of hostile raids.

description.zh: 在大战时由附属社区追加征发，承担运输、筑垒、后勤和支援作战任务。
description.en: Raised from subordinate communities during major campaigns, they performed transport, fortification, logistical, and supporting combat duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 图图特佩克领主及其贵族战首
commanderInChief.en: The Lord of Tututepec and his senior noble war captains

technology.zh: 军事技术属前火药时代的中部美洲水平，以黑曜石刃武器、木盾、棉甲、弓箭、投枪器及山地要塞防御为主，擅长在复杂地形中作战。
technology.en: Military technology belonged to the pre-gunpowder Mesoamerican level, centered on obsidian-edged weapons, wooden shields, quilted cotton armor, bows, atlatls, and hilltop fortifications, with strong adaptation to difficult terrain.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近米斯特克城邦间的世仇、瓦哈卡地区的政治竞争、对海岸与山地通道的争夺，以及与中部墨西哥和周边高地势力的边界摩擦。
threats.en: The main threats came from hereditary rivalries among neighboring Mixtec city-states, political competition within Oaxaca, struggles over coastal and upland routes, and border friction with central Mexican and nearby highland powers.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于瓦哈卡高地、山间谷地与太平洋沿岸据点，形成密集村社与若干重要城邦中心。图图特佩克代表的是强势政治中心之一，而非唯一全国首都式城市。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed across the Oaxaca highlands, intermontane valleys, and Pacific-facing centers, forming dense village networks and several major city-state hubs. Tututepec represented one of the strongest political centers rather than the sole national-style capital city.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致分为王族与高等贵族、次级贵族、祭司与书记、武士、专业工匠、自由农民、附属劳役者与战俘。贵族与祭司垄断政治、礼仪和宗谱知识，普通村社承担农业和贡赋主体。
socialClasses.en: Social classes broadly included royal and high nobles, lesser nobles, priests and scribes, warriors, specialized artisans, free peasant farmers, dependent laborers, and war captives. Nobles and priests monopolized politics, ritual, and genealogical knowledge, while ordinary communities carried the main burden of farming and tribute.

allies.zh: 没有现代意义上的固定国家同盟；实际盟友多为通过婚姻、朝贡或共同作战联系的米斯特克与周边瓦哈卡城邦。
allies.en: There were no permanent state allies in the modern sense; practical allies were neighboring Mixtec and Oaxacan polities linked by marriage, tribute, or temporary military cooperation.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是竞争性的米斯特克城邦、瓦哈卡高地对手，以及中部墨西哥方向可能扩张的纳瓦诸政治体。
enemies.en: Enemies were mainly rival Mixtec city-states, competing powers in the Oaxaca highlands, and potentially expansionary Nahua polities to the northwest and central Mexican sphere.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以 dynastic marriage、祖先合法性宣示、贡赋关系与有限征服为核心。图图特佩克这类强邦倾向扩展其海岸—高地联系，同时避免陷入无法维持的广域统一战争。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on dynastic marriage, declarations of ancestral legitimacy, tribute relationships, and limited conquest. Strong polities such as Tututepec aimed to expand coastal-highland connections while avoiding unsustainable wars for broad territorial unification.

era.zh: 中世纪美洲前火药时代
era.en: Medieval pre-gunpowder Mesoamerican era

keyInnovations.zh: 彩绘宗谱抄本传统、精密金工与失蜡铸造、绿松石与贝壳镶嵌、梯田与山区农业适应、棉织与染色工艺
keyInnovations.en: Painted genealogical codices, advanced goldworking and lost-wax casting, turquoise and shell mosaic techniques, terraced and upland agricultural adaptation, and sophisticated cotton weaving and dyeing

strengths.zh: 米斯特克文明的优势在于强大的贵族合法性体系、高度发达的工艺生产、灵活的婚姻外交、适应山地环境的政治组织，以及图图特佩克等强邦带来的区域整合能力。
strengths.en: The strengths of Mixtec civilization lay in its powerful system of noble legitimacy, highly developed craft production, flexible marriage diplomacy, political organization suited to mountainous terrain, and the regional integrating power of strong centers such as Tututepec.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于政治高度分裂、贵族世仇频繁、缺乏统一常备官僚与税制、军事动员依赖地方忠诚，并且经济总体仍深受农业收成与贡赋网络稳定性的限制。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included strong political fragmentation, frequent aristocratic feuds, the absence of a unified standing bureaucracy and tax system, military mobilization dependent on local loyalty, and an economy still heavily constrained by harvest conditions and the stability of tribute networks.

outlook.zh: 在1280年前后，米斯特克世界总体稳定，图图特佩克等强权有望继续在瓦哈卡扩展影响力。但长期前景仍取决于城邦间联盟是否持久，以及能否在不破坏既有宗谱秩序的情况下扩大统治范围。
outlook.en: Around 1280, the Mixtec world was broadly stable, and powers such as Tututepec were well placed to continue expanding influence in Oaxaca. Its long-term outlook, however, depended on whether inter-city alliances could endure and whether larger rule could be extended without disrupting the established genealogical order.

description.zh: 瓦哈卡地区的山地城邦网络，以复杂谱系政治和手工业闻名。
description.en: A highland city-state network in Oaxaca known for complex genealogical politics and craft production.

ruler.zh: 诸地方酋长（前卡松齐式酋长）

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族酋邦联盟，处于早期国家形成阶段
governmentForm.en: Decentralized tribal chiefdoms in an early state-formation phase

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、村社与地区首领网络为基础，上层为世袭酋长与祭司，下层为普通农民、渔民、工匠与战士。不同湖区与高地社区保持较强自治，但逐渐出现围绕帕茨夸罗湖盆地的等级整合。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on clans, village communities, and regional chief networks. At the top stood hereditary chiefs and ritual specialists, while common farmers, fishers, artisans, and warriors formed the majority. Communities around the lake basin and highlands retained strong autonomy, but increasing hierarchy was emerging around the Lake Pátzcuaro core.

rulingClass.zh: 地方世袭酋长家族、祭司集团与控制黑曜石、铜工与湖区资源的精英家户
rulingClass.en: Local hereditary chiefly families, priestly groups, and elite households controlling obsidian, copperworking, and lacustrine resources

succession.zh: 以氏族内部世袭与酋长议定相结合，通常由同一家系中最有威望的成年男性继任，但地方惯例差异较大
succession.en: Succession combined hereditary transmission within ruling lineages and confirmation by leading chiefs; most often the most prestigious adult male of the lineage succeeded, though practice varied by locality

structure.zh: 1280年的米却肯尚未形成统一王国，政治结构由多个湖区、高地和山谷酋邦组成。各酋邦由地方首领统治，通过亲族联姻、祭祀合作、战争同盟和贡赋往来维系秩序。津津聪灿周边的核心地区已出现较强的区域领导力，预示后来的塔拉斯坎国家形成。
structure.en: In 1280 Michoacán was not yet a unified kingdom. Its political structure consisted of multiple lake, highland, and valley chiefdoms. Each was ruled by local leaders tied together through kinship marriages, ritual cooperation, war alliances, and tribute exchange. The Tzintzuntzan area already showed stronger regional leadership, foreshadowing the later Tarascan state.

function.zh: 协调各主要氏族和聚落之间的联盟、婚姻安排、争端调停与重大决策
function.en: Coordinated alliances, marriage arrangements, dispute mediation, and major decisions among leading clans and settlements

function.zh: 征集玉米、豆类、棉织物、鱼获、手工业品与劳役，并管理地方储备
function.en: Collected maize, beans, cotton cloth, fish products, craft goods, and labor dues, and managed local stores

function.zh: 主持公共祭祀、祖先崇拜、圣地维护与农业时令安排
function.en: Oversaw public rites, ancestor cults, sacred sites, and agricultural scheduling

function.zh: 组织战团、守卫聚落、召集盟友并在冲突时协调地方首领的军事行动
function.en: Organized war bands, defended settlements, summoned allies, and coordinated military action among local chiefs during conflicts

function.zh: 管理铜矿、黑曜石来源、陶器生产与专业工匠服务，尤其在核心地区更为集中
function.en: Managed copper sources, obsidian procurement, pottery production, and specialized artisan service, especially in core districts

localAdmin.zh: 基层行政以村社、氏族和地方酋长宅邸为中心。大多数事务由村社长老、家族首领和祭司共同处理；较大聚落向周边村落征收贡物与劳役。湖区核心地带已出现超越单一村社的区域性支配，但尚未形成高度标准化官僚体系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on villages, clans, and chiefly residences. Most affairs were handled jointly by village elders, lineage heads, and priests. Larger settlements collected tribute and labor from surrounding communities. In the lake core a more regional authority had begun to emerge, but no highly standardized bureaucracy yet existed.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，由氏族传统、长老裁断、酋长裁决和宗教禁忌共同构成。常见案件包括土地与水源使用、婚姻纠纷、贡赋拖欠、盗窃和血仇。处罚形式多为赔偿、劳役、羞辱性惩戒、驱逐，严重案件可引发复仇或处死。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, grounded in clan tradition, elder arbitration, chiefly judgment, and sacred taboo. Common cases involved land and water use, marriage disputes, tribute arrears, theft, and blood-feud matters. Punishments typically included compensation, labor service, public humiliation, or exile; severe cases could lead to revenge killing or execution.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物贡纳和劳役为主，而非统一货币税。常见贡品包括玉米、豆类、辣椒、鱼类、盐、纺织品、陶器、铜制品和狩猎所得。附庸或较弱村社在战时还需提供搬运、修筑与战士支援。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form of tribute in kind and labor rather than uniform monetary tax. Common dues included maize, beans, chili, fish, salt, textiles, pottery, copper goods, and hunting products. Dependent or weaker communities also supplied transport, construction labor, and military support in wartime.

religion.zh: 以本土多神崇拜、祖先祭祀、自然圣地崇拜和火、太阳、山川、湖泊相关仪式为核心。后世塔拉斯坎宗教中著名的库里考韦里等神灵体系在此时期可能已处于形成或地方性崇拜阶段。
religion.en: Religion centered on indigenous polytheism, ancestor veneration, sacred landscape cults, and rites tied to fire, the sun, mountains, and lakes. The divine system later associated with Tarascan religion, including Curicaueri, was likely in formation or existed as localized cult practice at this time.

philosophy.zh: 思想观念主要体现在口传传统、祖先记忆、战争荣誉、农时循环与祭祀秩序之中，并无成文哲学学派。社会强调亲族义务、社区互助、首领威望与神圣合法性。
philosophy.en: Ideas were expressed through oral tradition, ancestral memory, war honor, seasonal agricultural cycles, and ritual order rather than formal written philosophy. Society stressed kin obligation, communal reciprocity, chiefly prestige, and sacred legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟的本土文字系统，主要依赖口头传承、记忆、仪式表演与图像符号表达身份、领地和神圣意义
writingSystem.en: No fully developed native writing system; communication of identity, territory, and sacred meaning relied mainly on oral transmission, memory, ritual performance, and visual symbolism

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要文化成就包括帕茨夸罗湖区聚落网络的发展、早期公共仪式空间、精良陶器、羽饰与木工传统，以及西墨西哥地区突出的铜工技术。该地区文化整合能力较强，为后来的塔拉斯坎国家、宗教礼制与工艺专门化奠定了基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable achievements included the development of settlement networks in the Lake Pátzcuaro basin, early public ritual spaces, fine ceramics, featherwork and woodworking traditions, and the distinctive copperworking of western Mexico. The region’s capacity for cultural integration laid foundations for the later Tarascan state, religious institutions, and craft specialization.

languageFamily.zh: 普雷佩查语系（通常视为孤立语言）及周边多语言接触环境
languageFamily.en: Purépecha language family (generally treated as a language isolate) within a multilingual contact zone

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、渔业、采集和区域手工业为基础，整体规模在中部美洲西部属较强水平，但仍以地方性生产与再分配为主。帕茨夸罗湖区提供渔业、灌溉与交通优势，周边高地则支持玉米农业和矿产开发。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, fishing, gathering, and regional craft production. Its overall scale was comparatively strong for western Mesoamerica, though still dominated by local production and chiefly redistribution. The Lake Pátzcuaro basin offered fishing, irrigation, and transport advantages, while surrounding highlands supported maize farming and mineral extraction.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、渔业、盐产、陶器、纺织、羽饰制作、木工、石器加工、黑曜石交换与早期铜工
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, fishing, salt production, pottery, textiles, featherwork, woodworking, stone-tool production, obsidian exchange, and early copperworking

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、辣椒、鱼干、盐、陶器、棉布与地方织物、羽毛、黑曜石、铜铃与铜饰品、木器、狩猎皮张
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, chili, dried fish, salt, pottery, cotton cloth and local textiles, feathers, obsidian, copper bells and ornaments, wood goods, and animal hides

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富体现在耕地使用权、储粮、房屋、渔具、陶器、衣物与亲族网络上。精英家庭则掌握更多劳役、贡物、稀有羽饰、金属饰品和宴飨能力。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was embodied in access to fields, stored grain, houses, fishing gear, pottery, clothing, and kin ties. Elite households commanded greater labor, tribute, rare feathers, metal ornaments, and the means to host redistributive feasts.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给、酋长再分配、贡赋网络和区域交换相结合的酋邦经济。市场交换存在但尚未成为主导机制，政治权力与资源控制密切相连。
economicSystem.en: A chiefdom economy combining village subsistence, chiefly redistribution, tribute networks, and regional exchange. Market exchange existed but was not yet dominant; political power was closely tied to control over resources.

category.zh: 酋长家户与宴飨再分配

category.en: Support for artisans and prestige production

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中铸币储备，而是分散保存在酋长宅邸、祭祀中心和仓库中的粮食、织物、金属饰品、盐、鱼干及其他可再分配物资。核心地区的储备较充足，足以维持稳定但不适于长期大规模战争。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a centralized coin reserve but dispersed stores of grain, cloth, metal ornaments, salt, dried fish, and other redistributable goods held in chiefly residences, ritual centers, and storehouses. Core districts maintained solid reserves, enough for stability but not for prolonged large-scale war.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持地方忠诚、祭祀合法性与季节性储备，通过贡赋与宴飨形成政治整合。总体上较为稳健，盈余有限但足以应对常见歉收和局部冲突。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining local loyalty, ritual legitimacy, and seasonal reserves, using tribute and feasting as instruments of political integration. It was generally prudent, with modest surpluses sufficient to handle ordinary crop shortfalls and local conflict.

description.zh: 由核心酋长直接维持的常备武装，负责护卫首领、守卫中心聚落和执行快速袭击
description.en: Permanent armed followers directly maintained by major chiefs, used for leader protection, guarding core settlements, and rapid raids

description.zh: 战时由各氏族和村社动员的主体兵力，装备矛、木盾、投射武器和石刃武器，适合地形复杂地区作战
description.en: The main wartime manpower mobilized from clans and villages, equipped with spears, wooden shields, projectile weapons, and stone-edged arms, suited to fighting in broken terrain

description.zh: 负责监视边界、山道和湖岸交通，兼具通报、伏击和护送职能
description.en: Responsible for watching borders, mountain paths, and lake routes, combining warning, ambush, and escort functions

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由最强酋长与战时联盟首领共同指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; command was shared by the strongest chiefs and wartime alliance leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以中美洲晚期前金属化作战传统为主，使用木矛、投矛器、弓箭、石刃棍棒、木盾和棉甲或厚织物防护。铜工已在地区经济中出现，但1280年尚未全面转化为大规模实战武器优势。
technology.en: Military technology remained rooted in late pre-metal Mesoamerican warfare, using wooden spears, atlatls, bows, stone-edged clubs, wooden shields, and cotton armor or thick woven protection. Copperworking was present in the regional economy, but by 1280 had not yet produced a broad battlefield advantage.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边西墨西哥与中部墨西哥方向的竞争性政体、贸易路线争夺、地方叛离和酋长间内斗。此时期尚无阿兹特克帝国式的集中外压，但区域整合竞争正在加剧。
threats.en: The chief threats came from rival polities in western and central Mexico, struggles over trade routes, local secession, and conflict among chiefs. There was not yet the kind of centralized external pressure later represented by the Aztec Empire, but regional competition for integration was intensifying.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于帕茨夸罗湖盆地、周边高地农业区及若干交通要冲。整体人口密度在西墨西哥属较高水平，得益于湖区资源、火山高地农业和区域聚落网络的发展。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the Lake Pátzcuaro basin, surrounding highland farming zones, and key transit corridors. Overall density was relatively high for western Mexico, supported by lake resources, volcanic highland agriculture, and a developed regional settlement network.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括世袭酋长与其家族、祭司与仪式专家、战士、专业工匠、普通农民与渔民，以及依附性较强的村社成员与战俘。阶层分化正在增强，但仍保留浓厚的氏族和地方共同体色彩。
socialClasses.en: Social strata included hereditary chiefs and their families, priests and ritual specialists, warriors, specialized artisans, ordinary farmers and fishers, and more dependent village members and war captives. Stratification was increasing, though strong clan and local communal identities remained.

allies.zh: 无固定的长期国家级盟友，主要是帕茨夸罗湖区及周边高地诸酋邦之间基于婚姻、祭祀和共同防御形成的临时联盟
allies.en: No fixed long-term state-level allies; diplomacy mainly involved temporary alliances among the Lake Pátzcuaro and surrounding highland chiefdoms based on marriage, ritual ties, and common defense

enemies.zh: 周边竞争性纳瓦及西墨西哥诸政体、敌对氏族联盟，以及争夺资源和贸易节点的地方首领
enemies.en: Rival Nahua and western Mexican polities, hostile clan coalitions, and local leaders competing for resources and trade nodes

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持区域平衡、争取附庸村社、控制湖区与高地通道、并通过婚姻和馈赠扩大影响力为主。由于尚未形成统一国家，其外交更多表现为酋长网络互动而非正式国家关系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining regional balance, attracting dependent communities, controlling lake and highland corridors, and expanding influence through marriage and gift exchange. Because no unified state yet existed, diplomacy operated more through chiefly networks than formal interstate relations.

era.zh: 中美洲后古典时期
era.en: Mesoamerican Postclassic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与实践包括湖区农业与渔业资源综合利用、梯田和高地农耕经验、区域陶器专业化、远距离黑曜石流通，以及西墨西哥尤为重要的早期铜冶炼与铜器制作传统。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations and practices included integrated lake agriculture and fishing, terracing and highland cultivation experience, regional ceramic specialization, long-distance obsidian circulation, and the especially important early tradition of copper smelting and copper artifact production in western Mexico.

strengths.zh: 主要优势在于帕茨夸罗湖区的生态多样性、较高人口密度、强韧的氏族网络、活跃的区域交换和突出的铜工传统。核心地区已有较强整合潜力，能够在稳定环境中逐步向更复杂的国家形态发展。
strengths.en: Major strengths included the ecological diversity of the Lake Pátzcuaro basin, relatively high population density, resilient clan networks, active regional exchange, and notable copperworking traditions. The core zone already possessed significant integrative potential, enabling gradual evolution toward a more complex state in stable conditions.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分散、官僚化不足、军事指挥不统一，以及各地习惯法和贡赋标准差异较大。地方酋长若拒绝合作，整体动员能力便会明显受限。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses lay in political fragmentation, limited bureaucratization, lack of unified military command, and considerable variation in legal and tribute norms. If local chiefs refused cooperation, overall mobilization capacity was sharply constrained.

outlook.zh: 1280年前后的米却肯处于稳定而关键的转型阶段。若湖区核心首领能够继续整合周边酋邦、垄断仪式合法性并控制手工业与贡赋网络，则未来数代内极有可能形成更集中的普雷佩查—塔拉斯坎政权。
outlook.en: Around 1280 Michoacán stood in a stable but pivotal transitional phase. If the chiefs of the lake core continued to integrate neighboring chiefdoms, monopolize ritual legitimacy, and control craft and tribute networks, a more centralized Purépecha-Tarascan polity was very likely to emerge within the following generations.

description.zh: 米却肯高地尚未完成统一，但已形成后世塔拉斯坎国家的基础。
description.en: The Michoacán highlands were not yet unified, but the foundations of the later Tarascan state were emerging.

ruler.zh: 诸位哈拉赫·维尼克与阿豪（地方统治者）
ruler.en: Various halach uinic rulers and ajawob (local rulers)

dynasty.zh: 各地方王族与贵族家族
dynasty.en: Local royal houses and noble lineages

governmentForm.zh: 以玛雅潘为首的城邦联盟与地方君主制并存
governmentForm.en: A city-state league centered on Mayapan combined with local kingship

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈严格分层，顶层为王族、贵族与祭司，其下为武士、书吏、工匠、商人、普通农民，以及战俘与奴役人口。不同城邦之间虽制度相近，但地方氏族与亲族网络对社会组织影响很大。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with royal families, nobles, and priests at the top, followed by warriors, scribes, artisans, merchants, common farmers, and captives or enslaved people. While institutions were similar across polities, local lineage and kinship networks remained highly important.

rulingClass.zh: 世袭王族、地方贵族家系、祭司集团与武士精英
rulingClass.en: Hereditary royal houses, local noble lineages, priestly groups, and warrior elites

succession.zh: 通常为王族内部世袭继承，以父子或近亲传承为主，但也常受贵族会议、联盟势力与军事实力影响
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary within royal houses, commonly passing from father to son or close kin, but it was often shaped by noble councils, league politics, and military power

structure.zh: 1280年前后的玛雅政治并非统一帝国，而是由尤卡坦北部的玛雅潘联盟、若干附属或关联城镇，以及危地马拉高地和低地残存的地方王国共同组成。玛雅潘的统治者在联盟中具有较高威望，但各城邦仍保留自身统治者、贵族议事机构、祭司体系与地方贡赋结构。
structure.en: Around 1280, Maya politics did not form a unified empire but consisted of the Mayapan league in northern Yucatán, a number of subordinate or associated towns, and surviving local kingdoms in the Guatemalan highlands and lowlands. The ruler of Mayapan held considerable prestige within the league, but individual city-states retained their own rulers, noble councils, priestly institutions, and tribute arrangements.

function.zh: 协调王族、贵族家系与附属城镇之间的政治关系，处理继承、盟约、战争与贡赋事务
function.en: Coordinated relations among royal houses, noble lineages, and subordinate towns, and handled succession, alliances, war, and tribute

function.zh: 征集玉米、棉布、蜂蜜、可可、盐、羽毛及劳役，管理地方仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected maize, cotton cloth, honey, cacao, salt, feathers, and labor service, and managed local storage and redistribution

function.zh: 主持祭祀、历法计算、占卜、神庙维护与合法性仪式
function.en: Oversaw rituals, calendar calculations, divination, temple maintenance, and ceremonies of legitimacy

function.zh: 记录贡赋、婚姻、地界、争端与政治协议，并协助贵族审理案件
function.en: Recorded tribute, marriages, boundaries, disputes, and political agreements, and assisted nobles in hearing cases

function.zh: 组织城邦武士、边界防守、道路巡逻与战时动员
function.en: Organized city-state warriors, frontier defense, road patrols, and wartime mobilization

function.zh: 统筹村社首领、公共工程、农田水井维护与劳役征发
function.en: Coordinated village headmen, public works, field and well maintenance, and labor drafts

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以城邦为核心，周围村社和小镇向中心城邑缴纳贡赋与服劳役。地方首领、贵族和祭司共同管理土地、集市、神庙与纠纷。玛雅潘联盟范围内的若干城镇可能受联盟核心家族更直接影响，但高地和边远地区的王国自治性更强。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was centered on city-states, with surrounding villages and small towns paying tribute and labor service to the urban core. Local chiefs, nobles, and priests jointly managed land, markets, temples, and disputes. Towns within the Mayapan league were likely more directly influenced by the core ruling families, while highland and peripheral kingdoms remained more autonomous.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依习惯法、王室裁断与宗教规范运行。土地边界、婚姻、债务、盗窃、伤害和贡赋违欠是常见案件。贵族与祭司在审理中占主导地位，惩罚包括赔偿、劳役、羞辱性处罚、没收财物与对严重罪行施以死刑。
legalSystem.en: Law functioned through customary norms, royal judgment, and religious authority. Common cases involved land boundaries, marriage, debt, theft, injury, and unpaid tribute. Nobles and priests dominated adjudication, and punishments included compensation, labor, public humiliation, confiscation, and death for grave offenses.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物和劳役为主，而非统一货币税。主要项目包括玉米、豆类、棉布、盐、蜂蜜、蜡、可可、羽毛、陶器及建筑或军事劳役。附属城镇和战败群体承担更重贡赋，核心贵族则通过分配贡品和控制贸易获利。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on goods and labor rather than a uniform cash tax. Major obligations included maize, beans, cotton cloth, salt, honey, wax, cacao, feathers, pottery, and construction or military labor. Subordinate towns and conquered groups bore heavier tribute, while core nobles profited from redistribution and control of trade.

religion.zh: 玛雅宗教为多神信仰，强调天体、祖先、自然力量与地方神祇。1280年的尤卡坦和危地马拉地区仍维持庙宇祭祀、献祭、历法仪式和神谕传统。库库尔坎、恰克、伊察姆纳等神灵崇拜广泛，祖先祭祀与统治合法性密切相关。
religion.en: Maya religion was polytheistic, emphasizing celestial bodies, ancestors, natural forces, and local deities. In 1280, temple ritual, offerings, calendrical ceremonies, and divinatory traditions remained active in Yucatán and Guatemala. Deities such as Kukulkan, Chaac, and Itzamna were widely revered, and ancestor worship was closely linked to political legitimacy.

philosophy.zh: 玛雅思想并非哲学体系化传统，而是以历法宇宙观、王权神授、时间循环、祖先记忆和礼仪秩序为核心。知识由祭司和书吏掌握，强调人与神、土地、时序之间的平衡关系。
philosophy.en: Maya thought was not a formal philosophical system in the later Eurasian sense, but centered on a calendrical cosmology, divinely sanctioned rulership, cyclical time, ancestral memory, and ritual order. Knowledge was held by priests and scribes, stressing balance among humans, gods, land, and sacred time.

writingSystem.zh: 玛雅文字为音义结合的象形文字系统。到后古典时期，碑铭传统较古典时代减弱，但书写仍用于宗教文本、谱系记录、历法知识和可能的屏风式手抄本。
writingSystem.en: The Maya writing system was a logosyllabic hieroglyphic script. By the Postclassic period, monumental inscription had declined compared to the Classic era, but writing was still used for religious texts, genealogical records, calendrical knowledge, and likely screenfold manuscripts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 后古典玛雅在建筑、壁画、祭仪空间规划、天文历法、织造、羽饰、玉石与贝壳工艺方面延续高水平传统。玛雅潘、图卢姆等地反映出区域性政治与宗教风格的变化，同时保留深厚的古典遗产。
culturalAchievements.en: Postclassic Maya culture maintained high traditions in architecture, mural art, ceremonial urban planning, astronomy and calendrics, weaving, featherwork, and jade and shell craftsmanship. Sites such as Mayapan and Tulum reflect changing regional political and religious styles while preserving deep Classic-era inheritances.

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系，包含尤卡坦玛雅语、基切诸语及其他地方变体
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family, including Yucatec Maya, K'iche'an languages, and other regional varieties

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 玛雅经济以农业、地方手工业、朝贡再分配与区域贸易为基础，货币化程度有限。由于缺乏统一国家和普遍铸币，经济估值只能换算为贵金属当量。整体经济足以维持密集定居、祭祀建筑与地区性战争，但不及同时期欧亚大型帝国的财政集中能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Maya economy was based on agriculture, local craft production, tributary redistribution, and regional trade, with limited monetization. Because there was no unified state and no widespread coinage, economic valuation can only be expressed in precious-metal equivalents. Overall output was sufficient to support dense settlement, ritual building, and regional warfare, but it lacked the fiscal concentration of the great Eurasian empires of the same era.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、棉布织造、盐业、蜂蜜与蜂蜡生产、陶器、石器、羽毛与贝壳工艺、可可与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cotton weaving, salt production, honey and beeswax production, pottery, stone tools, feather and shell craftsmanship, cacao, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、可可、棉布、蜂蜜、蜂蜡、黑曜石、玉石、羽毛、贝壳、陶器、铜铃、小型奢侈品与食物剩余
tradeGoods.en: Salt, cacao, cotton cloth, honey, beeswax, obsidian, jade, feathers, shell goods, pottery, copper bells, small luxury items, and food surpluses

name.zh: 无统一法定货币，通行可可豆与布匹等交换单位
name.en: No unified legal currency; cacao beans and cloth served as exchange media

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富体现在房屋、农具、储粮、纺织品、家禽、蜂箱与亲族土地使用权上；贵族家庭则拥有更多劳动力、祭祀器物、奢侈品与贡赋份额。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth took the form of dwellings, tools, stored grain, textiles, poultry, beehives, and lineage-based access to land; noble households possessed more labor, ritual objects, luxury goods, and shares of tribute.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per worker per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制是村社农业、贵族征贡、劳役动员与市场交换并存的混合体系。地方市场活跃，但再分配与礼仪性消费仍很重要。长途贸易由专业商人、沿海航运者与政治精英共同控制。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a mixed one combining village agriculture, noble tribute extraction, labor mobilization, and market exchange. Local markets were active, but redistribution and ritual consumption remained important. Long-distance trade was controlled jointly by professional merchants, coastal transporters, and political elites.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Labor service and public works equivalent

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 附属城镇贡品与战利品
source.en: Tribute from subordinate towns and war gains

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 盐业、蜂蜡与王室控制资源
source.en: Salt, beeswax, and resources under elite control

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非以大量金银铸币形式存在，而是以储粮、布匹、可可、蜂蜡、盐、礼器、俘虏劳动力与少量贵重物品构成。玛雅潘及主要城邦维持分散式仓储体系，以应对祭祀、旱灾、军事和政治馈赠需求。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury did not exist primarily as masses of coined gold or silver, but as stores of grain, cloth, cacao, beeswax, salt, ritual goods, captive labor, and a smaller quantity of precious items. Mayapan and major city-states maintained dispersed storage systems to meet ritual, drought, military, and political gift-giving needs.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持联盟稳定、保障祭祀支出、囤积应急物资和在战争中快速动员。由于统治依赖贡赋与人身网络，财政征收更偏向协商、威慑与再分配，而非严格统一预算制度。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on preserving league stability, sustaining ritual expenditure, stockpiling emergency supplies, and enabling rapid wartime mobilization. Because rule depended on tribute and personal networks, revenue collection leaned more on negotiation, coercion, and redistribution than on a strict unified budgetary system.

name.en: Royal guards and urban garrison warriors

description.zh: 常备力量主要驻守玛雅潘及各主要城镇，负责宫殿、神庙、城墙、道路和仓储安全，也在战时担任核心突击力量。
description.en: The permanent force was concentrated at Mayapan and other major towns, guarding palaces, temples, walls, roads, and storehouses, and serving as the core striking force in war.

description.zh: 由附属村社和地方贵族动员的主体兵力，装备较为简朴，以长矛、投枪器、弓箭和木盾为主，适合袭扰、围困和短期会战。
description.en: This was the main body of forces mobilized by subordinate communities and local nobles. Their equipment was relatively simple, centered on spears, atlatls, bows, and wooden shields, suitable for raiding, sieges, and short campaigns.

name.en: Noble retainers and allied elite warriors

description.zh: 由贵族家系及联盟成员提供，人数较少但训练和地位较高，常执行指挥、突袭、俘获敌方首领和保卫礼仪中心等任务。
description.en: Provided by noble houses and league partners, these were fewer in number but of higher training and status, often tasked with command, shock assaults, capturing enemy leaders, and defending ceremonial centers.

commanderInChief.zh: 玛雅潘主政者与各城邦阿豪分别统军
commanderInChief.en: The ruler of Mayapan and the ajaw of each polity commanded their own forces

technology.zh: 军事技术属中等水平，主要使用弓箭、长矛、投枪器、木棉甲、木盾、石刃武器以及少量铜制装饰或工具性金属器。缺乏骑兵、铁器和火药，但熟悉围墙、堑壕、伏击和丛林作战。
technology.en: Military technology was moderate, relying mainly on bows, spears, atlatls, quilted cotton armor, wooden shields, stone-edged weapons, and a limited use of copper prestige or utility items. There was no cavalry, iron weaponry, or gunpowder, but Maya forces were experienced in walls, ditches, ambushes, and forest warfare.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自玛雅内部城邦竞争、尤卡坦半岛的权力斗争、贸易路线争夺，以及与中部墨西哥和周边美洲政体的间接冲突。更大的风险是联盟内部失衡和地方王族叛离，而非外来跨洋入侵。
threats.en: The main threats came from internal Maya rivalries, power struggles in Yucatán, competition over trade routes, and indirect conflicts with Central Mexican and neighboring American polities. The greater danger was imbalance within the league and defections by local dynasties rather than any overseas invasion.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约二百万，分布于尤卡坦北部城镇网络、乡村村社、危地马拉高地聚落及若干低地遗存社区。人口较古典盛期后出现区域转移，北部尤卡坦的政治和人口重要性上升。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 2,000,000, distributed across the urban network of northern Yucatán, rural village communities, highland Guatemalan settlements, and residual lowland populations. After shifts from the Classic peak, northern Yucatán had become more politically and demographically important.

name.zh: 乌斯马尔地区遗存聚落

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王族与大贵族、次级贵族、祭司、武士、书吏、商人、工匠、农民、附庸社群与奴役人口。不同地区的氏族组织和血缘关系会改变阶层边界，但整体上政治、宗教和经济特权高度集中于上层。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included royal families and high nobles, lesser nobles, priests, warriors, scribes, merchants, artisans, farmers, dependent communities, and enslaved people. Regional clan organization and kinship could modify class boundaries, but political, religious, and economic privilege remained highly concentrated in the upper strata.

allies.zh: 主要盟友为玛雅潘联盟内部城镇、若干尤卡坦地方贵族集团，以及与玛雅贸易网络相连的沿海与内陆伙伴
